ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ tšé̱ la le̱ ſane, ni duṅ le̱ etšé̱le̱ nyōṅ. Ni dše na ni dše tše̱re, ni efe kleṅkleṅ gbĩ le̱.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ tšé̱ heni egbĩ le̱ šikpoṅ, ni nui le̱ anābuamo̱ le̱ etšé̱le̱ ṅšo̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehĩ.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Šikpoṅ le̱ akwẽ dſe̱i, bāi ni woo̱ wui ke̱ tšei ni woo̱ yibii ye̱ eheno̱, ní ewui le̱ yo̱ emli, ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱! Ni efe nakai.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ni šikpoṅ le̱ kwẽ dſe̱i, bāi ni woo̱ wui ye̱ eheno̱, ke̱ tšei ní woo̱ yibii ní ewui le̱ yo̱ emli ye̱ eheno̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehī.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Ni dše na ni dše tše̱re, ni efe gbĩ ni dši gbĩ etẽ le̱.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Kanei aba ṅwei atatu le̱ mli, koni ame̱gbla ſane ke̱ nyōṅ mli, ni ame̱tšō okadii ke̱ bei ke̱ gbĩĩ ke̱ afii; Lal.

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: ni ame̱tšṍmo̱ kanei ye̱ ṅwei atatu le̱ mli, ní ame̱tšo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱. Ni efe nakai.

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ fe kanei wudši enyo̱ le̱: kane wulu le̱ ní eye ſane no̱, ke̱ kane bibiō le̱ ni eye nyōṅ no̱, ke̱ ṅulamii le̱ hũ. Lal.

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: ni ame̱ye ſane ke̱ nyõṅ ne̱, ni ame̱gbla la le̱ ke̱ duṅ le̱ mli. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehĩ.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Nui le̱ awo lōi ni hĩe kāmo̱ nyẽṅẽnyẽṅẽ, ni lofõdši afliki ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ye̱ ṅwei atatu le̱ hĩe.

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ bo̱ ṅšo̱ṅloi wudši le̱ ke̱ bo̱nii fẽ ni hĩe kāmo̱ ní tšio̱ ame̱he ni nui le̱ wo nyēṅẽnyẽṅē ye̱ ame̱heno̱ le̱, ke̱ lofõdši fẽ ni yo̱ fidši ye̱ ame̱heno̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehĩ.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Ni dše na ni dše tše̱re, ni efe gbĩ ni dši gbī enumo̱ le̱.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ fe šikpoṅ koloi ye̱ ame̱heno̱, ke̱ šĩa koloi ye ame̱heno̱, ke̱ nii fẽ ní wamoo̱ ye̱ šikpoṅ ye̱ ame̱heno̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehĩ.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ni *Nyoṅmo̱ bo̱ gbomo ye̱ esubãṅ le̱ no̱, Nyoṅmo̱ subãṅ le̱ no̱ ebo̱le̱ ye̱; †nū ke̱ yō ebo̱ame̱. *Ef.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ dšō̱ame̱, ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyefo̱a ni nyeyi afáa ni nyeyia šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ obõ, ni nyenānāa no̱, ni nyeyea ṅšo̱ṅloi ke̱ ṅwei lofõdši ke̱ kolo-fẽ-kolo ni tšio̱ ehe ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱ no̱. Bo̱f.

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: ni koloi fẽ ni yo̱ šikpoṅ lē̱ no̱ ke̱ ṅwei lofõdši fē ke̱ nibii ſẽ ni wamoo̱ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni wala susuma yo̱ ame̱mli le̱, aṅõ dſe̱fo̱ fẽ ahãame̱ ni ame̱ye. Ni efe nakai.

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kwe̱ nibii fẽ ni efe le̱, ni na, ehĩ naakpa. Ni dše na ni dše tše̱re, ni efe gbĩ ni dši gbĩ ekpa le̱.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Ene̱mei dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ šišidšē he sãdši, beni abo̱ame̱ le̱, ye̱ gbĩ no̱ ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ fe šikpoṅ ke̱ ṅwei le̱.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ fe trom ye̱ Eden ye̱ bokāgbe̱, ni eṅõ gbomo ní ešõ le̱ eto dše̱i.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Ni fā ni dši etẽ le̱ gbe̱i dši Hidekel, noni hoo̱ ye̱ Ašur bokãgbe̱ le̱; ni fā ni dši edſe̱ le̱ dši Eufrate.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ wo gbomo le̱ kitã ekē̱: Trom le̱ mli tšei le̱ fẽ no̱ yibii le̱ ye bo̱ni osumoo̱;

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: ši ekpakpa ke̱ efõṅ lẽ tšo le̱ yibii le̱ kãye eko; edšake̱ gbĩ no̱ ni ooye eko le̱, *gbo oogbo! *Rom.

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Ehĩĩ ake̱ gbomo le̱ kome hĩo̱ ši; mafe hefatalo̱ ko ní sale̱ mahãle̱. Abe̱i

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ hã wo̱ ni tšī nyõ Adam no̱, ni ewo̱; ni edšie eṅmãwui le̱ eko, ni eṅõ lō etšĩmo̱ nãdšiaṅ.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ *ṅõ ṅmãwu ni edšie ye̱ Adam mli le̱ efe yō, ni eke̱le̱ ba Adam ṅõ. *

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ni Adam kē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ene̱ dši wu ni dše̱ miwui le̱ amli ke̱ lō ní dše̱ mihelo le̱ mli; aatšé̱ mone̱ yō, edšake̱ nū mli adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱.

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ nū aašĩ etše̱ ke̱ enye, ni eekpe̱te̱ eṅã he, ni ame̱atšṍ heló kome. Mat

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Ni ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱nyie ya-yai, nū le̱ ke̱ eṅã le̱, ni ame̱hĩe gboo he.

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Ni beni yō le̱ *na ake̱ tšo le̱ hĩ hã yeli, ni efeo̱ hĩṅme̱ii ako̱no̱, ni tšo le̱ baä mo tšine, ni etšõo̱ mo nii le̱, †etše eno̱ yibii le̱ eko ni eye, ni ehã ewù ní ke̱le̱ yo̱ le̱ hũ eko, ni eye. *Iak.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Ni ame̱nu Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ gbē ní enyie trom le̱ mli, beni gbe̱ke̱nāši kō̱yo̱ ſie le̱; ni Adam ke̱ eṅã le̱ *tē ame̱he ye̱ trom le̱ṅ tšei le̱ ateṅ ke̱-dše̱ Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ hĩe. *Ier.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ tšé̱ Adam ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Nẽgbe̱ oyo̱?

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱ yō le̱ ake̱: Mẽba ofe ene̱? Ni yō le̱ kē̱: Onũfũ le̱ lakami, ni miye.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Ni mike̱ nyē̱ aaṅmẽ oke̱ yō le̱ teṅ, ke̱ oseši ke̱ *eseši teṅ; †eetſa oyitšo nõ, ni **bo hũ ootſa enanetšitši nõ. *Gal.

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Yō le̱, ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Fá mahā ohedšrame̱ ke̱ oho̱ṅō̃ afá; dšramo̱ mli oofo̱ bii ye̱, ni owù le̱ se̱gbe̱ ootao, ni le̱ *eeye ono̱. *Ef.

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Ni Adam le̱, ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Ake̱ni obo oṅã le̱ toi ni oye tšo le̱ ni miwoö he kĩtã ake̱ okaye eyibii le̱ eko hewe̱ le̱, alomo̱ šikpoṅ sũ ye̱ ohewo̱, dšramo̱ mii ooye eno̱ nii ye̱, owala gbĩĩ abo̱ fẽ.

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Ni ṅmei ke̱ aṅlẽṅlẽ eewo ehão, ni ṅãno̱ bāi ooye.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: Ohĩe *latšā mli ooye nii ye̱, ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni ookũ ose̱ ke̱-ate šikpoṅ sũ mli, edšake̱ emli adšieo ke̱-dše̱. Edšake̱ sũ dšio, ni sũ mli ookũ ose̱ ke̱-ate! *

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Ni Adam tšē̱ eṅã le̱ gbe̱i Hawa, edšake̱ le̱ dši hĩekãle̱i fẽ anye.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ fe wolo atadei ehã Adam ke̱ eṅã le̱, ni eṅõ-woame̱.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Ni Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Na, Adam etšṍ *tamo̱ wo̱teṅ mokome, ni ele ekpakpa ke̱ efõṅ; ni agbe̱ne̱ beni afe ni ekakpã enine mli, ni etše wala tšo le̱ yibii le̱ hũ eko, nieye, ni ehĩe mīkã ke̱-mīya nāno̱! *K.

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: Hewo̱ le̱ Iehowa Nyoṅmo̱ dšiele̱ ye̱ Eden trom le̱ mli, koni eyahũ šikpoṅ, nõ mli ni adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱ le̱;

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: ni eſie Adam, ni eṅõ *kernbim ke̱ lalile̱i klante ni tšṍmoe̱ ebe eto Eden trom le̱ hĩe ye̱ bokãgbe̱, koni ebu wala tšo le̱ nāgbe̱ le̱ he. *Ezek.

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: Ni efo̱ enyemi Habel efata he; ni Habel tšṍ tōkwe̱lo̱, ni Kain tšṍ šikpoṅhũlo̱.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ ye̱ mli āhũ le̱ Kain ṅõ šikpoṅ nibānii le̱ eko eyašã afō̱le ehã Iehowa;

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: ši Kain ke̱ efo̱le le̱, ehĩe eso̱o̱ no. Ni Kain mli wo la naakpa, ni etũ ehĩe.

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Aso ke̱dši ofe ekpakpa le̱, ohĩe baṅ nyam lo? ši ke̱dši ofee ekpakpa le̱, eša *bu šĩnā le̱ ši, ni bo ose̱gbe̱ etaoo̱; ši bo le̱ †ye eno̱. *Gal.

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Ni ekē̱: Meni ofe nē̱? Onyemi le̱ *lá gbē mīblo̱ mītšé̱mi ke̱-mīdše̱ šikpoṅ sũ le̱ mli! *Mat.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: Ni agbe̱ne̱, alomo̱o ke̱-dše̱ šikpoṅ le̱ ni gble enā ni eke̱mī̃ onyemi le̱ lá le̱ ye̱ odeṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
text: Na, oſiemi ṅme̱ne̱ ke̱-dše šikpoṅ le̱ hĩe, ni matẽ mihe ke̱-dše̱ ohĩe, ni mitšṍ šikpalo̱ ke̱ kobo̱fo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni aaba mli ake̱ mofẽmo ni aanami le̱, eegbemi. Hiob

ref: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱le̱ ake̱: Nohewo̱ le̱ mofẽmo ni aagbe Kain le̱, aato̱le̱ owele toi kpawo. Ni Iehowa kadi Kain, koni kē̱ moko nale̱ ni ekatſale̱ nõ.

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Ni Kain le eṅã le̱, ni ená mũsũ, ni efo̱ Henok. Ni eto maṅ, ni eṅõ ebi Henok gbe̱i ewo maṅ le̱.

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: Ni Lamek kē̱ eṅãmei le̱ ake̱: Ada ke̱ Zila, nyenua migbē; nye Lamek ṅãmei, nyeboa miwiemo̱ le̱ toi! Ši mipilamo̱ hewo̱ migbe nū ko, ni mihe flawõ hewo̱ hũ migbe oblanyo ko.

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Ké̱ aato̱ Kain he owele toi kpawo le̱, asane̱kwa Lamek le̱, toi nyoṅmai-kpawo ke̱ kpawo! K.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Ni Adam le eṅã le̱ ekoṅ, ni efo̱ binū, ni ewole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Set; edšake̱ ekē̱: Nyoṅmo̱ eṅõ seši kroko etomi Habel nãdšiaṅ, edšakē̱ Kain egbele̱.

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Ni Set hũ fo̱ binū, ni ewole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Enoš. No be le̱ mli abõi *Iehowa gbe̱i tšé̱mo̱. *

ref: God created men and women to be like himself. He gave them his blessing and called them human beings. The following is a list of the descendants of Adam, the first man:
text: nū ke̱ yō ebo̱ame̱, ni edšō̱ame̱, ni ewoame̱ gbe̱i ake̱ gbomo, gbĩ no̱ ni abo̱ame̱ le̱.

ref: and God took him away at the age of
text: ni Henok ke̱ Nyoṅmo̱ nyie, ni anaale̱, edšake̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ṅõle̱ ke̱-te.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: ni ewole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Noa, ni ekē̱: Mone̱ baše̱dše wo̱mī ye̱ wo̱nitšume̱i ke̱ wo̱nidšiaṅ to̱le̱i le̱ ni dše̱ šikpoṅ le̱ *lōmo̱ ni Iehowa elomo̱le̱ le̱ bewo̱ le̱ mli. *

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Mene̱mei dši Noa sešibii le̱: Noa dši *dšalo̱, ni eye emū ye̱ eyino̱bii le̱ ateṅ; ni Noa ke †Nyoṅmo̱ nyie. *Heb.

ref: He had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text: Ni Noa fo̱ bihĩĩ etẽ: Šem ke̱ Ham ke̱ Iafet.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Ṅõ gofertšo ofe adeka ohã ohe, ni ofe tšũi ye̱ adeka le̱ mli, ni owo emli ke̱ ehe amã.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Ni Noa fe nakai; bo̱ni Nyoṅmo̱ fã́le̱ le̱, nakai pe̱pē̱pe̱ efe.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: Ši mi le̱, na, mike̱ nu afua mība šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, koni mike̱-kpãtã helo fẽ ní wala mumo̱ yo̱ mli le̱ hĩe ke̱-dše̱ ṅwei šiši; nofẽno ni yo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ aaplenē.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ši mate kpãṅmo̱ ši mamã mike̱o teṅ, ni oobote adeka le̱ mli, bo ke̱ obihĩĩ le̱ ke̱ oṅã ke̱ obihĩĩ le̱ aṅãmei le̱.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Lofõdši ye̱ ame̱heno̱, ke̱ koloi ye̱ ame̱heno̱, ke̱ nii ni wamoo̱ ye̱ ame̱heno̱, sro̱toi fẽ enyo̱nyo̱ aba oṅõ, koni ahere ame̱yiwala.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Ni bo le̱, ṅõ niyenii fẽ ní ayeo̱ le̱ eko ke̱-ba oṅō, ni obna nā ohã bodientše̱ ke̱ame̱ yeli.

ref: He obeyed God and took a male and a female of each kind of animal and bird into the boat with him.
text: enyo̱nyo̱ dše̱ ame̱teṅ te Noa ṅõ ye̱ adeka le̱ mli, nū ke̱ yō, take̱ bo̱ni *Ie-howa fã́ Noa le̱. *

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ gbĩĩ kpawo le̱ se̱ le̱ nu afua le̱ ba šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: The water under the earth started gushing out everywhere, the sky opened like windows, and rain poured down for
text: ni nyoṅmo̱ tue ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ſaneſane ke̱ nyōṅnyōṅ gbĩĩ nyoṅmai-edfe̱.

ref: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.
text: Nakai gbĩ le̱ no̱ noṅṅ Noa ke̱ ebii le̱, Šem ke̱ Ham ke̱ Iafet, ke̱ Noa ṅã le̱ ke̱ ebii le̱ aṅãmei etẽ le̱ bote adeka le̱ mli,

ref: Finally, the mighty flood was so deep that even the highest mountain peaks were about seven meters below the surface of the water.
text: Ninetalo̱i nyoṅma ke̱ enumo̱ nui le̱ kwo̱ ke̱-teke gõdši ni eha ame̱no̱ le̱ ano̱.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Ni nni le̱ damo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, gbĩĩ oha ke̱ nyoṅmai-enumo̱.

ref: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
text: Ni emẽ gbĩĩ kpawo kroko, ni esā etšũ okpo le̱ ekoṅ ke̱-dše̱ adeka le̱ mli.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Ni okpo le̱ ba eṅõ gbe̱ke̱nãši, ni na, mũtšo-bāfo̱ kõ enā; ni Noa ná le ake̱ nui le̱ emī̃ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Ni esã emẽ gbĩĩ kpawo kroko hũ, ni etšũ okpo le̱; ni ekũũ ese̱ ke̱-baa eṅõ doṅṅ.

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: Dše adeka le̱ mli, bo ke̱ oṅã ke̱ obii ke̱ obii aṅãmei ni fata ohe le̱.

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Ni Noa ke̱ ebii le̱ ke̱ eṅã ke̱ ebii le̱ aṅãmei ni fata ehe le̱ dše kpo;

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Ni beni Iehowa here ṅma dšũrõ le̱, Iehowa kē̱ ye̱ etšui mli ake̱; Milomo̱ṅ šikpoṅ le̱ ye̱ gbomo hewo̱ doṅṅ; edšake̱ gbomo *tšuiṅ dſẽṅmo̱ le̱ efõṅ dši ke̱-dše̱ egbekẽbiiaši; ni asaṅ †mitſiaṅ nofëno ni hĩe kã le̱ nii butū tamo̱ bo̱ni mife ne̱ doṅṅ.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Gbĩĩ abo̱ fẽ ni šikpoṅ le̱ yo̱ ne̱, nidũmo̱ ke̱ nikpamo̱, fẽi ke̱ latšā, agbie̱nā ke̱ aharabata ke̱ ſane ke̱ nyõṅ se̱ efoṅ. Ier.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: Ni nye le̱, nyefo̱a ni nyeyi afáa; ni nyewoa ſiei babaō ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni nyeyi afáa ye̱ no̱.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Ni mahã mikpãṅmo̱ ní mike̱nye ekpãṅ le̱ amã ši, ni nu afua le̱ ebaplenẽṅ helo fẽ doṅṅ, ni asaṅ nu afua ko ebakpãtãṅ šikpoṅ le̱ hĩe gbĩkogbĩko.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Miṅõ mikwãbue̱te̱ mito atatui le̱ amli, ni efe kpāṅmo̱ ni yo̱ mike̱ šikpoṅ le̱ teṅ le̱ he okadi.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Ni aaba mli ake̱, ke̱dši mihã atatui wo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ke̱ke̱ le̱ aana kwābue̱te̱ le̱ ye̱ atatui le̱ amli,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: ni makai mikpãṅmo̱ le̱ ni mike̱nye ke̱ wala snsuma-fẽ-wala susuma ní yo̱ helo fẽ mli ekpãṅ le̱, ni nui le̱ tšṍṅ afua ni eekpãtã helo fẽ hĩe doṅṅ;

ref: Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, left the boat. Ham later had a son named Canaan.
text: Ni Noa bii le̱ ni dše adeka le̱ mli le̱ dši Šem ke̱ Ham ke̱ Iafet. Ni Ham le̱, Kanaan tše̱ dšile̱.

ref: All people on earth are descendants of Noah's three sons.
text: Mene̱mei dši Noa hii etẽ le̱; ni ame̱mli mei dše̱ ſã šikpoù mū le̱ fẽ no̱.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Ni Noa dše šiši ni etšṍ šikpoṅhũlo̱, ni efe weintrom.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Ni enu wein le̱ eko, ni eto̱, ni ekã ebũ le̱ mli ke̱ ehe flo.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Ni Ham, Kanaan tše̱ le̱, yana etše̱ yayaiaṅ, ni eyakē̱ enyemimei enyo̱ le̱ ye̱ agbó le̱ nā. Abe̱i

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Ši Šem ke̱ Iafet ko̱ mama ni ame̱ṅõ-fõ ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱kõdši ano̱, ni ame̱nyie se̱se̱, ni ame̱te ni ame̱ke̱-yaha ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ; ši ame̱tšṍmo̱ ame̱hĩeaṅ ame̱hã se̱gbe̱, ni ame̱naa ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Ši beni Noa hĩe tšere̱le̱ ke̱-dše̱ edā̃tō̱ le̱ mli, ni ená ele noni ebi gbekẽ le̱ efele̱ le̱

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: ekē̱: Alomo̱ Kanaan, nyṍdši anyṍṅ eetšṍ ehā enyemimei le̱!

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Ni esā eké̱: Aadšō̱ Iehowa, Šem Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, ni Kanaan aatšṍ enyṍṅ! Rom.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Nyoṅmo̱ aabā Iafet mli alē̱, ni *eehī Šem bũi le̱ amli, ni Kanaan aatšṍ ame̱-nyṍṅ! *Ef.

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Mene̱mei amli mei dše̱ ſã dšeṅmādši le̱ aṅšo̱kpo̱i le̱ ano̱, ye̱ ame̱šikpõdši le̱ ano̱, mofẽmo ke̱ ewiemo̱, ye̱ ame̱kutšei ke̱ ame̱dšeṅmādši le̱ anā. Zak.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Mene̱mei dši Noa bii le̱ awekui ye̱ ame̱sešibii le̱ anã ye̱ ame̱mãdši le̱ amli; ni mene̱mei amli dšeṅmãdši le̱ dše̱ fã šikpoṅ le̱ ṅo̱ ye̱ nu afua le̱ se̱.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Yiē̱, nyehãa wo̱toa maṅ wo̱hãa wo̱he, ni wo̱mãa mõ ni eyiteṅ ayaši ṅwei, ni wo̱ke̱-he gbe̱i, koni wo̱-kaſã šikpoṅ mü le̱ fẽ no̱.

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Ni Iehowa *kpleke ši ke̱-ha, ni ebakwe̱ maṅ ke̱ mõ ní gbomei abii le̱ mãa le̱. *

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱: Na, maṅ kome ni, ni ame̱fẽ ame̱ye̱ nābu kome; ni ene̱ ame̱-bõi femo̱ ne̱; ni agbe̱ne̱ le̱, noni ame̱susu ake̱ ame̱afe le̱ atšĩṅame̱ ekoeko gbe̱.

ref: Two years after the flood, when Shem was
text: Ni beni Šem fo̱ Arpakšad se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-enumo̱, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni beni Arpakšad fo̱ Šela se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-edfe̱ ke̱ etẽ, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Shelah had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni beni Šela fo̱ Eber se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-edſe̱ ke̱ etẽ, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Eber had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni beni Eber fo̱ Peleg se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-edſe̱ ke̱ nyoṅmai-etẽ, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Peleg had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni beni Peleg fo̱ Reu se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-enyo̱ ke̱ ne̱hũ, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Reu had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni heni Reu fo̱ Serug se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-enyo̱ ke̱ kpawo, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni beni Serug fo̱ Nahor se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-enyo̱, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Nahor had more children and died at the age of
text: Ni heni Nahor fo̱ Tera se̱ le̱, eye afii oha ke̱ nyoṅma ke̱ ne̱hũ, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Ni Tera ṅõ ebi Abram ke̱ Haran bi Lot, ni dši enãbi le̱, ke̱ ešāyõ Sarai, ni dši ebi Abram ṅã le̱, ni ame̱ke̱ame̱ dše *Ur ye̱ Kaldea, ni ame̱baya Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱; ši beni ame̱ba Haran le̱, ame̱bahĩ dše̱i. *Ios.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: Ni efã ye̱ dše̱i ke̱-te goṅ ko ni yo̱ Betel bokãgbe̱ le̱ he, ni eši ebū le̱, ni Betel ye anaigbe̱ ni Ai ye bokãgbe̱; ni dše̱i hũ emā afo̱lešãlate̱ ye̱ ehã Iehowa, ni *etšé̱ Iehowa gbe̱i. *

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Ni Abram mīfã ke̱-mīya wuoyigbe̱ le̱ lolo.

ref: The crops failed, and there was no food anywhere in Canaan. So Abram and his wife Sarai went to live in Egypt for a while. But just before they got there, Abram said, “Sarai, you are really beautiful!
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni eſe̱ flō ni ebaše̱ Mizraim le̱, ekē̱ eṅã Sarai ake̱: Agbe̱ne̱ na, mile ake̱ yō ní he yo̱ fe̱o dšio, ni osa kwe̱mo̱,

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: nohewo̱ le̱ aaba mli ake̱, ké̱ Mizraimbii le̱ nao le̱, ame̱akē̱ akē̱: Eṅã ne̱! ni ame̱agbemi, ṅi bo le̱ ame̱ahere oyiwala.

ref: Please save my life by saying you are my sister.”
text: Nohewo̱ le̱, mikpao fai, kē̱mo̱ ake̱ minyemiyō dšio, koni ahĩ ahāmi ye̱ ohewo̱, ni misusuma yi aná wala ye̱ ohewo̱.

ref: The king's officials told him about her, and she was taken to his house.
text: Ni Farao lume̱i le̱ hũ nale̱, ni ame̱yadšie eyi ame̱hã Farao; ni abaṅõ yō le̱ ke̱-te Farao we le̱.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ni ehĩe Abram dšogbāṅ ye̱ ehewo̱, ni ená tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ tedšihĩĩ ke̱ we-hĩĩ ke̱ weyei ke̱ tedšiyei ke̱ yomai.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Ši Iehowa ke̱ pĩmo̱i ní nā wa pĩ Farao ke̱ e we le̱ ye̱ Abram ṅã Sarai le̱ he wo̱. Lal.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Ní Farao tšé̱ Abram, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Meni ofemi ne̱? mẽba okē̱ē̱mi ake̱ yō le̱ oṅãṅ?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Mẽba okē̱ aké̱: Minyemiyō dšile? nto kũlē̱ mibaṅõlē̱ mafe miṅã. Ni agbe̱né̱, na oṅã, ṅõle̱ ni oke̱le̱ aya!

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Ni Farao fã́mo̱ hĩĩ komei eto ehe ake̱ ame̱yadšiele̱ ke̱ eṅã ke̱ nofẽno ni eyo̱ le̱ gbe̱.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Ni Abram ená nii babaō ye̱ šĩa koloi ke̱ dſie̱te̱i ke̱ šika gbe̱fãṅ. Abe̱i

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Ni ekã egbe̱fā̃i le̱ ahe ke̱-dše̱ wuoyigbe̱ le̱ ke̱-te Betel tō̃, ke̱-te heni eši ebū le̱ ye̱ tšutšu le̱, ye̱ Betel ke̱ Ai teṅ,

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Ni Lot hũ ni ke̱ Abram te le̱ ye̱ tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ būi.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Ni Abram kē̱ Lot akē̱: Oſai ne̱, kāhã béi mība mike̱o teṅ, ke̱ mikolo-kwe̱lo̱i le̱ ke̱ okolokwe̱lo̱i le̱ ateṅ; ši nyemimei dšiwo̱. Lal.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Lot ko̱ Iordan niiaṅ kutū le̱ fẽ, ni Lot fã ke̱-te bokãgbe̱; ni ame̱gbla ame̱mli, mo ke̱-dše enyemi he;

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: ši šikpoṅ fẽ ni onaä ne̱, bo maṅõhã ke̱ oseši ke̱-ate nāno̱.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Ni mahã osešibii afá tamo̱ šikpoṅ mlu, ni ke̱dši moko aanyẽ šikpoṅ mlu akane le̱, bĕle̱ aanyẽ osešibii le̱ hũ akane.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Te ši, nyiemo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ye̱ eke̱le̱ mli ke̱ elē̱mo̱ mli, ši bo mikē̱-bahã.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abram fā ebū le̱ ni eba ebahī Mamre tšei kutū ni yo̱ Hebron le̱ ašiši, ni emā afo̱lešàlate̱ ye̱ dše̱i ehã Iehowa.

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: ake̱ ame̱ke̱ Sodom maṅtše̱ Bera, ke̱ Gomora maṅtše̱ Birša, ke̱ *Adăma maṅtše̱ Šinab, ke̱ Zeboim maṅtše̱ Šemeber, ke̱ Bela ni dši Zoar le̱ maṅtše̱ le̱ yawu ta. *

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Ni afi noni dši nyoṅma ke̱ edſe̱ le̱ no̱ le̱ Kedorlaomer ke̱ maṅtše̱mei ni fata ehe le̱ ba, ni ame̱bagbe Refabii ye̱ Ašterot-Karnaim, ke̱ Zuzbii ye̱ Ham, ke̱ Embii ye̱ Kiriataim ṅã le̱ no̱,

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: ke̱ Horbii ye̱ ame̱goṅ Seir le̱ no̱ ke̱-yaši El-Paran ni yo̱ ṅã le̱ uo̱ le̱;

ref: At Siddim Valley, the armies of the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela fought the armies of King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Babylonia, and King Arioch of Ellasar. The valley
text: ni ame̱ke̱ Elam maṅtše̱ Kedorlaomer, ke̱ dšeṅmādši le̱ amaṅtše̱ Tidal, ke̱ Šinear maṅtše̱ Amrafel, ke̱ Elasar maṅtše̱ Ariok, maṅtše̱mei edſe̱ ne̱ ke̱ maṅtše̱mei enumo̱ le̱, wu.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Ni ame̱ṅõ Sodom ke̱ Gomora nii fẽ ke̱ ame̱niyenii nom, ni ame̱yiṅ ame̱te.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Ni foidšelo̱ ko ba bagba Abram, Hebrinyo le̱, ni ehe ši ye̱ Mamre, Amornyo le̱, tšei kutū le̱ ašiši le̱. Si Mamre dši Eškol ke̱ Aner nyemi; ni mene̱mei ke̱ Abram ekpãṅ.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Ni beni Abram nu ake̱ aṅõ enyemi le̱ nom le̱, eke̱ etšũdši ohai-etẽ ke̱ nyoṅma ke̱ kpãnyo̱ ni le tahenii ní afo̱ame̱ ye̱ ewe le̱ fã ta, ni etiuame̱ ke̱-te Dan.

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Ni eke̱ etšũdši le̱ gbá ta wo ame̱he nyõṅ, ni egbeame̱, ni etiuame̱ ke̱-te Hoba ni yo̱ Damasko abe̱kuiaṅgbe̱ le̱.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Ni eṅõ nibii le̱ fẽ, ke̱ enyemi Lot hũ ke̱ enibii le̱, ke̱ yei le̱ hũ ke̱ maṅ le̱ ke̱-kũ se̱ ke̱-ba ekoṅ.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Ni beni ekũ ese̱ ke̱-dše̱ Kedorlaomer ke̱ maṅtše̱mei ní fata ehe le̱ agbẽ le̱ ke̱-baä le̱, Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ dše kpo eya-kpele̱ ye̱ ṅäno̱dšō̱ ni atšé̱o̱le̱ maṅtše̱dšō̱ le̱ mli.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Ni *Salem maṅtše̱ †Melkizedek le̱ ke̱ abolo ke̱ wein dše kpo ba; ni ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ le oso̱fo dšile̱. *Lal.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Ni edšō̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Aadšō̱ Abram ahã ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ ni dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ tše̱ le̱;

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: ni aadšie ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ yi, ake̱ eṅõ ohenye̱lo̱i le̱ ewo odeṅ! Ni Abram hãle̱ nii le̱ fẽ mlidšãi nyoṅma mli ekome.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Ni Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ kē̱ Abram ake̱: Hãmi gbomei le̱, ni oṅõ nii le̱ ohã ohe.

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Ni Abram kē̱ Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ ake̱: Mīwó minine no̱ mītšõ Iehowa ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ ni dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ tše̱ le̱,

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: ake̱ ke̱-dše̱ kpã no̱ āhũ ke̱-yaši tokotafĩkpã no̱ lo nii fẽ ni dši onõ le̱, ke̱dši miko̱ eko, fĩ! koni okakē̱ ake̱: Mi, mife Abram niiatše̱;

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: dša noni oblahĩĩ le̱ eye ke̱ke̱, ke̱ hĩĩ ni ke̱mi te ni dši Aner ke̱ Eškol ke̱ Mamre le̱ agbe̱fãṅnō, ame̱ le̱ hã ame̱ṅõ ame̱gbe̱fãṅnõ!

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Ṅõ tšinayō ni eye afii etẽ, ke̱ tōabotia ní eye afii etẽ, ke̱ tõagbo ní eye afii etẽ, ke̱ kose̱-okpo ke̱ okpobi obahãmi.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Ni lofõdši lōyelo̱i kpleke ši basõmo̱ gbohĩĩ le̱ ano̱, ni Abram ſeame̱.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Ši yino̱ ní dši edſe̱ le̱ no̱ le̱ ame̱akũ ame̱se̱ ke̱-aba bie̱ ekoṅ; edšake̱ Amorbii le̱ anõša le̱ náko ayi.

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Ni beni hũlũ enyõ ši ni hĩe ebu le̱, na, flõnõ ni fãa lasũ ke̱ lalile̱i batšṍ kukudši ne̱ ateṅ.

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: ke̱ Hitbii ke̱ Perizbii ke̱ *Refabii, *

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: ke̱ Amorbii ke̱ Kanaanbii ke̱ Girgašbii ke̱ Iebusbii le̱.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Ni Abram ṅã Sarai le̱ ṅõ Mizraimyō Hagar, eweyō le̱, ehā ewù Abram gblã, beni Abram eye afii nyoṅma ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Ni eba Hagar ṅõ, ni ená mũsũ; ši beni ena ake̱ ehĩe mũsũ le̱, ewura le̱ he guo̱ gbo ye̱ ehĩe.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Sarai kē̱ Abram ake̱: Mihĩegbẽle ne̱ aakã ono̱! Miṅõ miweyō le̱ miwo omliši; ni beni ena ake̱ ehĩe mũsũ le̱, mihe guo̱ egbo ye̱ ehĩe. Iehowa aakodšo mike̱o teṅ!

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Ši Abram kē̱ Sarai ake̱: Na, oweyō le̱ ye̱ odeṅ; femo̱le̱, bo̱ni sa ohĩe. Ni beni ebaäle̱ ši le̱, edšo foi ye̱ ehĩe.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Ni Iehowa bo̱fo le̱ nale̱ ye̱ nubu ko he ye̱ ṅã le̱ ne̱, ye̱ Sur gbe̱ le̱ ne̱ nubu le̱ he.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Hagar, Sarai weyō, nẽgbe̱ odše̱, ni nẽgbe̱ hũ oyaä? Ni ekē̱: Miwura Sarai hĩe midšoo̱ foi ye̱ ne̱.

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Ni Iehowa bo̱fo le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Kū ose̱ ke̱-ya owura le̱ ṅõ, ni oba ohe ši ohãle̱.

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Ni e̱etšṍ gbomo ní tamo̱ ṅãṅ tedši; enine aakã mofẽmo he, ni mofẽmo nine aakã ehe; ni eehĩ ši ye̱ enyemimei le̱ fẽ hĩe.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Ni etšē̱ Iehowa ni ke̱le̱ wie le̱ gbe̱i ake̱: Bo dši Mawu ni naämi! Edšake̱ ekē̱: Ani bie̱ ne̱ hũ mikwe̱ moni naämi le̱ yi se̱ lo?

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ atšé̱ ne̱ke̱ nubu ne̱ ake̱ hĩekãlo̱ ni naämi le̱ nubu; na, eye̱ Kadeš ke̱ Bared teṅ.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Ni mike̱ mikpãṅmo̱ le̱ aṅmẽ mike̱o teṅ, ni mahã oyi afá babaō dientše̱.

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Ni akatšé̱ ogbe̱i Abram doṅṅ, ši moṅ Abraham aatšé̱ ogbe̱i; edšake̱ mike̱o efe dšeṅmãdši asafo yū atše̱. Rom.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ sā kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Ši bo hũ, ye mikpãṅmo̱ le̱ no̱, bo ke̱ osešibii ni aaba ye̱ ose̱ le̱ ye̱ ame̱yino̱i le̱ amli.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Ni aflo̱nye ketia, ni atšṍ kpãṅmo̱ ni yo̱ mike̱ nyeteṅ le̱ he okadi.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Ni nū fōlo̱ ko ní afokole̱ ketia le̱, aafo ne̱ke̱ susuma ne̱ ye̱ emã le̱ṅ aše̱ afõ; edšake̱ ekwa mikpãṅmo̱ le̱.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ sã kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Oṅã Sarai le̱, kātšé̱ egbe̱i ake̱ Sarai doṅṅ, ši Sara dši egbe̱i.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Le̱le̱ṅ, oṅã Sara aafo̱ binū ahão, ni oowole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Isak; ni mahã mike̱le̱ kpãṅmo̱ le̱ amã ši, ni atšṍ *nãno̱ kpãṅmo̱ ahã éseši ni aaba ye̱ ese̱ le̱.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Ni kẽle̱ Išmael hũ hewo̱ le̱ miboö toi; na, midšō̱le̱ ni mahã ewo ſĩei ni eyi afá *babaō dientše̱. †Eefo̱ lume̱i nyoṅma ke̱ enyo̱, ni mahã efe dšeṅmaṅ kpeteṅkple. *

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Ni eke̱le̱ fõ wiemo̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ yiṅ ke̱-dše̱ Abraham ṅõ *ke̱-te ṅwei. *

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: ni ewehĩĩ le̱ fẽ, mei ni afo̱ame̱ ye̱ šĩa ke̱ mei ni ake̱ šika heame̱ ye̱ gbo̱i adeṅ le̱, aflo̱ame̱ ketia afata ehe.

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Meni hewo̱ Sara ṅmõ ni ekē̱ ake̱: Aso le̱le̱ṅ ake̱ mi ni mitšṍ yōmo ne maná hi mafo̱ lo le̱?

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: *Ani sane ko wa hã Iehowa? Be ni mito le̱ mli le̱ maba oṅõ eko̱ṅ afi nākplo̱ bene̱, ni Sara aaná binū! *Luk.

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: ake̱-ni Abraham batšṍ dšeṅmaṅ kpeteṅkple ní he wa, ni abadšō̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ dšeṅmãdši fẽ ye̱ emli le̱?

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱: Edšakē̱ Sodom ke̱ Gomora *blo̱mo̱ le̱ fá ni ame̱he ešai le̱ etšī naakpa hewo̱ le̱,

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: *makpleke ši mate agbe̱ne̱, ni †mayakwe̱ ake̱, bo̱ni blo̱mo̱ le̱ ba miṅõ le̱, ame̱fe nakai kwrā lo; ni ke̱-dšee nakai hũ, ni male. *

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Ni Abraham tši be̱ṅke̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Ani ookpãtã dšalo̱ ke̱ gbomo fõṅ fẽ hĩe šikome lo?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Ene̱ akagbaleo ake̱ ooye ne̱ke̱ sane, ni ogbe dšalo̱ ke̱ gbomo fõṅ šikome, ní dšalo̱ atšṍ tamo̱ gbomo fõṅ! Ene̱ akagboleo! Ani šikpoṅ fẽ no̱ kodšolo̱ le̱ ekodšoṅ dšale̱ kodšomo̱?

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Ni Abraham here no̱ ni ekē̱ ake̱: Na, miṅõ-fõ mino̱ ake̱ mike̱ Nuṅtšo̱ le̱ aawie, tše̱ mi le̱, sũ ke̱ lamlu ke̱ke̱ dšimi.

ref: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
text: Ni ekē̱: Ne, miṅõ-fō mino̱ ake̱ mike̱ Nuṅtšo̱ le̱ aawie; ekole̱ aana mei nyoṅmoi-enyo̱ ye̱ dše̱i. Ni ekē̱: Mikpãtãṅ maṅ le̱ hĩe ye̱ mei nyoṅmai-enyo̱ le̱ hewo̱.

ref: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
text: Ni ekē̱: Nuṅtšo̱ mli akawo la, ni mawie šikome ne̱ pe̱; ekole̱ aana mei nyoṅma ye̱ dše̱i. Ni ekē̱: Mikpãtãṅ maṅ le̱ hĩe ye̱ mei nyoṅma le̱ hewo̱!

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: Ni beni Iehowa ke̱ Abraham efõ wiemo̱ le̱, eyiṅ ete; ni Abraham kũ ese̱ ke̱-ba edšē̱he le̱.

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Ni Lot dše kpo ni eke̱ ešãhĩmei le̱ ní baw̌e̱ ebiyei le̱ wie ekē̱: Nyetea ši *nyedšea bie̱ ne̱, ši Iehowa bakpãtã maṅ ne̱ hĩe! Ši etamo̱ moni yeo̱ fe̱o ye̱ ešāhĩmei le̱ ahĩe. *

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Ni beni dše tše̱re le̱, bo̱foi le̱ tšire̱ Lot yi se̱, ni ame̱kē̱: Te ši, ṅõ oṅã ke̱ obiyei enyo̱ ni yo̱ bie̱ le̱, koni akalōo aſie ye̱ maṅ le̱ nõša le̱ mli.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Na, ne̱ke̱ maṅ ne̱ be̱ṅke̱, bo̱ni aadšo foi ke̱-ate dše̱i, ni no edaa; hã madšo foi ke̱-ya dše̱i; ani dšee bibiō ni? ni misusuma yi aná wala.

ref: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Na, miwo ohĩe nyam ye̱ ne̱ke̱ sane ne̱ hū mli, ni mibutuṅ maṅ ni owie he ne̱.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Ni hũlũdšē mli pe̱ Lot bote Zoar.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iehowa hã swō̱ ke̱ la ne̱ ke̱-dše̱ Iehowa ṅõ ye̱ ṅwei eſie Sodom ke̱ Gomora no̱,

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: ni ebutu ne̱ke̱ mãdši ne̱ ke̱ dše̱i niiaṅ fẽ ke̱ mãdši le̱ amli bii fẽ ke̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ nibii ni ekwẽkwẽi le̱.

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Ši eṅã le̱ kwe̱ ese̱, ni etšṍ ṅōkpo̱ ni mã ši. Luk.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Ni Abraham te ši lebi mā̃ṅkpa ete heni edamo̱ Iehowa hĩe ye̱ le̱;

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: ni etšṍ ehĩe ehã Sodom ke̱ Gomora ke̱ dše̱i niiaṅ šikpoṅ kutũ le̱ fẽ, ni ekwe̱, ni na, šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ lasũ mīte ši take̱ flõnõṅ lasũ,

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Nyoṅmo̱ kpãtã mãdši ni yo̱ dše̱i niiaṅ kutũ le̱ ahĩe le̱, Nyoṅmo̱ kai Abraham ni edšie Lot ke̱-dše butumo̱ le̱ mli, beni ebutu mãdši le̱ ni Lot hī mli le̱.

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Ni Lot dše Zoar, ni eke̱ ebiyei enyo̱ le̱ yahĩ goṅ le̱ no̱, edšake̱ ẽše Zoar hĩle̱ gbeyei; ni eyahĩ te̱kplo ko mli, le̱ ke̱ ebiyei enyo̱ le̱.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni onukpa le̱ kē̱ gbekẽ le̱ ake̱: Wo̱tše̱ egbo̱, ni nū ko be̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni aaba wo̱ṅõ, take̱ bo̱ni afeo̱ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱ le̱;

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: ba, hã ni wo̱hã wo̱tše̱ wein enu, ni *wo̱ke̱le̱ awo̱, koni wo̱ke̱ wo̱tše̱ afo̱ bii. *

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ni ame̱hã ame̱tše̱ wein enu nakai nyō le̱ṅ; ni onukpa le̱ yiṅ te, ni eke̱ etše̱ yawo̱; ni elee ešikãmo̱ mli lo ešitē mli.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Ni dšetše̱reno̱ lebi le̱ eba mli ake̱ onukpa le̱ kē̱ gbekẽ lē̱ akē̱; Na, nye nyōṅ le̱ mike̱ mitše̱ yawo̱; hã ni wo̱hãle̱ wein enu nyō ne̱ṅ hũ, ni bo hũ oya oke̱le̱ ayawo̱, koni wo̱ke̱ we̱tše̱ afo̱ bii.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ni ame̱hã ame̱tše̱ wein enu nakai nyō le̱ṅ hũ; ni gbekẽ le̱ hũ yiṅ te, ni eke̱le̱ yawo̱; ni elee ešikãmo̱ mli lo ešitē mli.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Bo̱ni fe ni Lot biyei enyo̱ le̱ ke̱ ame̱tše̱ ná mũsũ ne̱.

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Ni kromo̱bi le̱ fo̱ binū, ni ewole̱ gbē̱i ake̱ Moab; le̱ dši Moabbii le̱ atše̱ ke̱-baši ṅme̱ne̱.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Ni gbekẽ le̱ hũ fo̱ binū, ni ewole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Ben-Ami; le̱ dši Amonbii le̱ atše̱ ke̱ ṅme̱ne̱.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: Ni Abraham kē̱ ye̱ eṅã Sara hewo̱ akē̱: Minyemiyō dšilē̱. Ni Abimelek, Gerar maṅtšē̱ le̱, tšũ ni ayaṅõ Sara ke̱-ba.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ ba Abimelek ṅõ nyōṅ ye̱ lamõ mli, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Na, obagbo ye̱ yō le̱ ní oṅõ le̱ hewo̱, edšake̱ nū ṅã dšile̱.

ref: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”
text: Ani dšee le̱ ekē̱mi ake̱: Minyemiyō dšile̱? ni yō le̱ dientše̱ hũ kē̱ ake̱: Minyeminū dšile̱? Mitšui mū ke̱ mideṅ tšemo̱ mike̱-fe ene̱ ne̱.

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱le̱ ye̱ lamõ le̱ mli ake̱: Mi hũ mile ake̱ otšui mū oke̱-fe ene̱; ni mi hũ mitšĩ onā, ní okafe eša ošimi; nohewo̱ ni mihāão hegbe̱ ake̱ ota ehe le̱.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ ṅõ nū le̱ ṅã le̱ ohãle̱ ekoṅ, ši *gbalo̱ dšile̱, ni eekpa fai ehão, ni oyi anã wala; ši ke̱dši oke̱le̱ hāāle̱ le̱, no le̱ ná ole ake̱ gbo oogbo, bo ke̱ mei fẽ ni dši onõ le̱! *Lal.

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Ni Abimelek te ši lebi mā̃ṅkpa, ni etšé̱ etšũdši le̱ fẽ, ni ewie wiemo̱i ne̱ fẽ ewo ame̱toiiaṅ; ni hĩĩ le̱ še gbeyei naakpa.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abimelek tšé̱ Abraham, ni eké̱le̱ ake̱: Meni ofewo̱ ne̱? Ni meni eša mifeo, ni oke̱ eša kpeteṅkple ebami ke̱ mimaṅtše̱yeli le̱ no̱ ne̱? Noni afee ofemi ne̱!

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Ni Abimelek sã kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Meni ona, ni oye ne̱ke̱ sane ne̱?

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
text: Ni Abraham kē̱: Edšake̱ miyasusu ake̱, ekole̱ Nyoṅmo̱ gbeyeišemo̱ be̱ bie̱, ni ame̱abagbemi ye̱ miṅã le̱ hewo̱.

ref: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
text: Ni asaṅ eye̱ mli le̱le̱ṅ ake̱ minyemiyō ni; mitše̱bi ni, ši dšee minyebiṅ; ni etšṍ miṅã.

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Nyoṅmo̱ hã midše mitše̱ we ni miyatšomlo le̱, mikē̱le̱ ake̱: Edšũrõ ni ofemi ne̱: hefẽhe ni wo̱o̱te le̱, kē̱mo̱ ye̱ mihe ake̱: Minyeminū ni.

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abimelek ṅõ tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ wehĩĩ ke̱ weyei eyabã Abraham, ni eṅõ eṅã Sara ebãle̱ ekoṅ.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Ni Abimelek kē̱: Na, mišikpoṅ le̱ kã ohĩe ne̱; hĩ heni osumoo̱.

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Edšake̱ Iehowa etšĩmo̱ yei fẽ ni yo̱ Abimelek we le̱ amũsũi anā ye̱ Abraham ṅã Sara sane le̱ hewo̱.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Ni Iehowa basra Sara, take̱ bo̱ni ekē̱ le̱, ni Iehowa fe ehã Sara, take̱ bo̱ni ewie le̱.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Ni Sara ná mũsũ, ni eke̱ Abraham fo̱ binū ye̱ egbo̱le̱ mli, ye̱ be ni Nyoṅmo̱ ekē̱le̱ le̱ mli. Heb.

ref: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
text: Ni Sara kē̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ewo *ṅmlõkpã ehãmi; mofẽmo ni aanu le̱ aaṅmõ eko ahãmi. *

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: Ni ekē̱ Abraham ake̱: *ſiemo̱ tšũlo̱yō ne̱ ke̱ ebi le̱; ši tšũlo̱yõ ne̱ bi le̱ ke̱ mibi Isak akaye gbošinii le̱. *Gal.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Ni tšũlo̱yō le̱ bi le̱ hũ, *mahã etšṍ dšeṅmaṅ, edšake̱ oseši dšile̱. *

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Ni Abraham te ši lebi mā̃ṅkpa, ni eko̱ abolo ke̱ nu ke̱ woloto̱ ehã Hagar, ni eṅõ-tſa ekõṅ, ke̱ gbekẽ le̱ hũ, ni ewole̱ gbe̱. Ni eyiṅ ete ni eyatšomlo ye̱ Beer-šeba ṅã le̱ṅ.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ nu oblanyo le̱ gbē, ni Nyoṅmo̱ bo̱fo le̱ tšé̱ Hagar ke̱-dše̱ ṅwei, ni eké̱le̱ ake̱: Meni feo̱o, Hagar? Kāše gbeyei; ši Nyoṅmo̱ enu oblanyo le̱ gbē ye̱ heni eyo̱ le̱.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ gble ehĩṅme̱ii, ni ena nubu ko; ni eyiṅ ete, ni eyaye̱ nu ewo woloto̱ le̱ obõ, ni eṅõ-hã oblanyo le̱ enu.

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: Ni eba mli ye̱ nakai be le̱ mli ake̱ Abimelek ke̱ etatše̱ Fikol bakē̱ Abraham ake̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱o ye̱ ye̱ nibii fẽ ní ofeo̱ le̱ mli.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ kã Nyoṅmo̱ kitã otšõmi ye̱ bie̱ ne̱, ake̱ oyeṅmi lo mibii ke̱ miho̱i akũtũmpo̱o; ši edšũrõ bo̱ni *mifeo le̱, nakai ooṅō-femi ke̱ šikpoṅ ni obato gbo̱ ye̱ no̱ ne̱. *

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.
text: Ni Abraham kē̱: Makã kitã le̱.

ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Ni Abraham kã Abimelek hĩe ye̱ nubu ko ni Abimelek tšũdši le̱ šõ ekā̃ nā le̱ hewo̱.

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abraham ṅõ tōi ke̱ tšinai ebã Abimelek, ni ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱kpãṅ.

ref: and Abimelech asked, “Why have you done this?”
text: Ni Abimelek kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Ne̱ke̱ gwanteṅbiyei kpawo ni odšie oto afã ne̱, té ešišiṅ.

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Ni ekē̱: Gwanteṅbiyei kpawo ne̱ oṅõ ye̱ mideṅ, koni aṅō-ye odase ahãmi, ake̱ mi midše ne̱ke̱ nubu ne̱.

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ etšé̱ dše̱i *Beeršeba, edšake̱ dše̱i ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱kãmo̱ kitã ye̱. *

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Ni ame̱kpãṅ ye̱ Beeršeba; ni Abimelek ke̱ etatše̱ Fikol te ši, ni ame̱kũ ame̱se̱ ke̱-ba Filistibii le̱ ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ekoṅ.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Ni Abraham te̱o tamarisketšo ye̱ Beeršeba, ni *etšé̱ Iehowa, †nāno̱ Mawu le̱, gbe̱i ye̱ dše̱i. *

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Ni Abraham to gbo̱ ye̱ Filistibii le̱ ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱ gbĩĩ pī.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Ni gbĩ ni dši gbĩ etẽ le̱ Abraham hole ehĩṅme̱ii ano̱, ni ena dše̱i ye̱ šoṅṅ.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Ni ame̱ba heni Nyoṅmo̱ etšõle̱ le̱; ni Abraham mã afo̱lešãlate̱ ye̱ dše̱i, ni eto lai le̱ gbe̱dšiano̱, ni efĩ ebi Isak le̱, ni eṅõle̱ eṅmẽ lai le̱ no̱ ye̱ afo̱lešãlate̱ le̱ no̱.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Ni Abraham kpã enine mli eko̱ kakla le̱, koni eke̱-gbe ebi le̱. Iak.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Ni Abraham wo dše̱i gbe̱i: Iehowa aakwe̱. Nohewo̱ le̱ ake̱o̱ ke̱ ṅme̱ne̱ fẽ ake̱: Iehowa goṅ le̱ no̱ aakwe̱ ye̱.

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: bewo̱ le̱ dšō̱mo̱ madšō̱o, ni fá mahā *oseši afá tamo̱ ṅwei ṅulamii ke̱ ṅšo̱ nā šia, ni oseši le̱ †aaye ehenye̱lo̱i agbói le̱ ano̱;

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: ni oseši le̱ mli aadše̱ adšō̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ dšeṅmãdši fẽ, edšake̱ obo migbẽ toi.

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abraham kũ ese̱ ke̱-ba eblahĩĩ le̱ aṅõ; ni ame̱te ši, ni ame̱fẽ ame̱ṅõ-ſie no̱ ke̱-te Beeršeba. Ni Abraham hĩ ši ye̱ Beeršeba.

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Ni eye̱ ṅã kroko ní atšé̱o̱le̱ Reuma, le̱ hũ efo̱ Teba ke̱ Gaham ke̱ Tahaš ke̱ Maaka.

ref: he went to the Hittites and said,
text: Ni Abraham te ši ke̱-dše̱ egbonyo le̱ he, ni eke̱ Hetbii le̱ yawie ake̱:

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: *Gbo̱ dšimi, ni nye mibato; nyehãami fũhe ko ye̱ nyeteṅ, koni mato migbonyo ni kã mihīe ne̱.

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: Bowo̱ toi, minuṅtšo̱! Nyoṅmo̱ lumo̱ dšio ye̱ wo̱teṅ; wo̱fū̃hei le̱ ateṅ noni hĩ fè fẽ le̱, fũ ogbonyo le̱ ye̱ mli; wo̱teṅ mokomoko etšīṅo gbe̱ ake̱ okafũ ogbonyo le̱ ye̱ efū̃he le̱.

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: ni eke̱ame̱ wie ekē̱: Ke̱dši nyeye̱ he tšui ake̱ mifũ migbonyo ni kã mihĩe ne̱, nyeboami toi, ni nyekpaä Zohar bi Efron le̱ fai nyehãami,

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: Dahi, minuṅtšo̱, bomi toi! Šikpoṅ le̱ mike̱-mīhão, ni te̱kplo le̱ ni yo̱ mli le̱ hũ mike̱-mīhão; mimaṅbii le̱ ahĩe mike̱-hãao ye̱ ne̱. Fū ogbonyo le̱!

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: ni mahã okã Iehowa, ṅwei Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱ šikpoṅ Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, kitã ake̱ *ow̌e̱ṅ Kanaanbii ni miyo̱ ame̱teṅ ne̱ abiyei le̱ eko ohãṅ mibi le̱; *

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: ši moṅ oote mišikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ke̱ miweku le̱ mli ni oyaw̌e̱ yō abahã mibi Isak.

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Ni tšũlo̱ le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ekole̱ yō le̱ ebakplẽṅ mise̱ nyiemo̱ ke̱ šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱ bã, bĕle̱ mike̱ obi le̱ akũ se̱ aya šikpoṅ no̱ heni odše̱ oba le̱ lo?

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Ni Abraham kē̱le̱ ake̱: Kwe̱ ohe ni ahĩ ní oke̱ mibi le̱ akakũ se̱ ke̱-mīya dše̱i doṅṅ!

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Ni ke̱dši yö le̱ sumo̱o̱ ake̱ eenyie ose̱ le̱, no le̱ obe edše mikitã ne̱ mli; mibi le̱ ke̱ke̱ ni oke̱le̱ akaya dše̱i doṅṅ.

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Ni tšũlo̱ le̱ ṅõ enine eho̱ enuṅtšo̱ Abraham ſuo̱ šiši, ni ekãle̱ kitã ye̱ sane le̱ hewo̱.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Ni tšũlo̱ le̱ ṅõ enuṅtšo̱ yomai le̱ ateṅ nyoṅma, ni eṅõ enuṅtšo̱ nibii kpakpai le̱ fẽ eko ehĩe edẽ, ni ete ši ni eyiṅ ke̱-te *Nahor mã le̱ṅ ye̱ Mesopotamia. *

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Ni ekē̱: Iehowa, minuṅtšo̱ Abraham Nyoṅmo̱! Mĩkpao fai ni ohã mihe aba nii ṅme̱ne̱ gbĩ ne̱, ni omli ahĩ ye̱ minuṅtšo̱ Abraham no̱.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Na, midamo̱ nubu ne̱ he, ni mã le̱ṅ bii abiyei le̱ mīdše kpo mība nuyē̱:

ref: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
text: Ni ekē̱: Nu, minuṅtšo̱! Ni ehe fe oyá ni eto egbé̱ le̱ ehĩe edẽ ni ehã enu.

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: Ni ehe fe oyá ni etšṍ egbé̱ le̱ mli nu le̱ ewo nunumo̱nõ le̱ mli, ni eša foi ete bu le̱ṅ ekoṅ eyaye nu le ehã eyomai le̱ fẽ.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Ni nū le̱ nā ni kpe̱ ehe ye̱ ehe hewo̱ le̱ efe diṅṅ, koni eṅõ-le ake̱ Iehowa ehã ehe eba nii ye̱ egbe̱fā̃ le̱ mli lo.

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Milka bi Betuel ni eke̱ Nahor fo̱ le̱ bi dšimi.

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: Ni nū le̱ mã eyi ši ni edšá Iehowa,

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: ni ekē̱: Aadšie Iehowa, minuṅtšo̱ Abraham Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, yi ake̱ ehãko emlihĩle̱ ke̱ eno̱kwayeli se̱ afo ye̱ minuṅtšo̱ no̱; ni mi hũ, Iehowa ebatšõmi minuṅtšo̱ nyemimei awe gbe̱!

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Ni oblayō le̱ ša foi ke̱-te ni eyagba mei ní yo̱ enye we le̱ ne̱ke̱ sãdši ne̱.

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Ši eba mli ake̱, beni ena ga le̱ ke̱ ninešigai le̱ ye̱ enyemiyõ le̱ nine ši, ni enu enyemiyō Rebeka wiemo̱i ni ewie ake̱: Bo̱ni nū le̱ kē̱mi ne̱ le̱, eba nū le̱ ṅõ, ni na, edamo̱ yomai le̱ amase̱i ye̱ nubu le̱ he.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Ni eke̱: Ba šĩa, bo moni Iehowa edšō̱o! mẽhewo̱ odamo̱ ṅmaṅmaša? Ši midientše̱ misā šĩa le̱ ke̱ gbe̱ hũ mihã yomai le̱.

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Ni nū le̱ ba šĩa le̱. Ni edšie yomai le̱ ano̱nii le̱, ni ehã yomai le̱ adſe̱i ke̱ niyenii, ni ebãle̱ ke̱ mei ni eke̱-nyie le̱ nu ni ame̱ke̱-fo̱ ame̱nãdši ahe.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ni ake̱ nii ba mã ehĩe ni eye; ši eke̱ ake̱: Miyeṅ nii, ake̱ dša midšadše nõ sane ní mihĩe ke̱-ba le̱ dã. Ni ekē̱: Wiemo̱!

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Ni ekē̱: Abraham tšũlo̱ dšimi.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Ni Iehowa edšō̱ minuṅtšo̱ naakpa, ni etšṍ gbomo agbo; ni ehãle̱ tōi ke̱ tšinai, ke̱ dſie̱te̱i ke̱ šika, ke̱ wehĩĩ ke̱ weyei, ke̱ yomai ke̱ tedšii.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: Ni minuṅtšo̱ ṅã Sara le̱ ke̱ minuṅtšo̱ efo̱ binū ye̱ egbo̱le̱ se̱ ne̱; ni le̱ eṅõ enii fẽ ni eyo̱ le̱ ehã.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Ni minuṅtšo̱ hã mikã kitã ake̱: Kaw̌e̱ Kanaanbii, mei ašikpoṅ no̱ ni miyo̱ ne̱ abiyei le̱ ateṅ moko obã mibi le̱,

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: ši ya mitše̱ we le̱ ke̱ midšaku le̱ moṅ oyawe̱ yō obahã mibi le̱.

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: Ni mikē̱ minuṅtšo̱ akē̱: Ekole̱ yō le̱ ebanyieṅ mise̱.

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Ni ekē̱mi akē̱: Iehowa, *mo hĩe ní minyie ne̱, aatšũ ebo̱fo afata ohe ke̱-ya, ni eehã ogbe̱ le̱ adša, ni oyaw̌e̱ yō ye̱ midšaku ke̱ mitše̱ we lē̱ obahã mibi le̱. *

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: No dãni obe aadše mikitã lē̱ mli; kē̱ ote midšaku le̱, ni ame̱hãāo le̱, bẽle̱ ohe edše mikitã le̱ mli.

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: Ni ṅme̱ne̱ miba nubu le̱ he, ni mikē̱: Iehowa, minuṅtšo̱ Abraham Nyoṅmo̱! ke̱dši oobã migbe̱ ni minyie ne̱ adša le̱,

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: no le̱ na, midamo̱ nubu ne̱ he, ni hã aba mli ake̱ oblayō ni aadše kpo aba nuyē̱, ni makē̱le̱ ake̱: Mikpao fai ni ohãmi ogbé̱ le̱ mli nu le̱ fiō manu,

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: ni eekē̱mi ake̱: Bodientše̱ nu, ni masã maye̱ eko mahã oyomai le̱ hũ le̱, hã le̱ noṅṅ efe yō̱ moni Iehowa edšie ebã minuṅtšo̱ bi le̱.

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Ni beni mikē̱ miwieo̱ migbeo̱ nā ye̱ mitšui mli pe̱ le̱, na, Rebeka pue, ni egbé̱ tſa ekõṅ ke̱-mība, ni ekpleke ši ke̱-te bu le̱ mli eyaye̱ nu; ni mikē̱le̱ ake̱: Mīkpao fai ni ohã manu eko!

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: Ni ehe fe oyá, ni eto egbé̱ le̱ ehĩe edẽ, ni eké̱: Nu! ni mahã oyomai le̱ hũ eko ame̱nu. Ni minu, ni yomai le̱ hũ ehãame̱ eko ame̱nu.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ni mibile̱ ake̱: Namo bi dšio? Ni ekē̱: Betuel, Nahor bi ni eke̱ Milka fo̱ le̱, bi dšimi. Ni miṅõ ga le̱ miwo egugõṅ, ni miṅõ ninešigai le̱ miwo enine;

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: ni mimã miyi ši, ni midšá Iehowa, ni midšie Iehowa, minuṅtšo̱ Abraham Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, yi ake̱ etšõmi gbe̱ kpakpa, ní mike̱-yaṅõ minuṅtšo̱ nyemibi mihã ebi le̱.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Ni agbē̱ne̱, ke̱dši nyeke̱ minuṅtšo̱ aaye ye̱ mlihìle̱ ke̱ ano̱kwayeli mli le̱, no le̱ nyekē̱ mi; ni ke̱-dšee nakai hũ, nyekē̱ami, koni mawo ninedšũrõ lo abe̱kuiaṅ tšṍmo̱.

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Laban ke̱ Betuel here no̱ ni ame̱kē̱: Iehowa ṅõ sane nē̱ dše̱; wo̱nyẽṅ wo̱ke̱o awie efõṅ lo ekpakpa.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Na, Rebeka le̱ ne̱, ṅõle̱ ni oke̱le̱ aya, ni eyatšṍ onuntše̱ bi le̱ ṅã, take̱ bo̱ni Iehowa ekē̱ lē̱.

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Ni tšũlo̱ le̱ dšie dſie̱te̱i nibii ke̱ šika nibii ke̱ mamai ke̱-ba, ni eke̱-bahã Rebeka; ni esā ehã enyeminũ le̱ ke̱ enye hū nibii ní dšra wa.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni eke̱ mei ni fata ehe le̱ ye nii ni ame̱nu noko, ni ame̱wo̱ dše̱i nakai nyō le̱ṅ; ni beni ame̱te ši lebi le̱, ekē̱: Nyewoami gbe̱, ni maya minuṅtšo̱ ṅõ!

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Ni enyeminũ le̱ ke̱ enye kē̱: Hā oblayō le̱ ke̱wo̱ ahĩ ši gbĩĩ fiō ake̱ gbĩĩ nyoṅma ne̱ke̱, no se̱ koni oke̱le̱ aya.

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Ni ekē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekatšĩa minā, edšake̱ Iehowa ehã migbe̱ le̱ edša; nyewoami gbe̱, ní maya minuṅtšo̱ ṅõ!

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Wo̱tšé̱ oblayō le̱, ni wo̱bi edāṅ gbē.

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Ni ame̱tšē̱ Rebeka ni ame̱bile̱ ake̱: Oke̱ nū ne̱ aate lo? Ni ekē̱: Mate.

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ni ame̱wo ame̱nyemiyō Rebeka ke̱ efufo̱hālo̱ le̱ ke̱ Abraham tšũlo̱ le̱ ke̱ emei ni fata ehe le̱ agbe̱.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Niame̱dšō̱ Rebeka, ni ame̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Wo̱nyemiyō, tšṍ mei akpei huhā anye, ni oseši aye ehenye̱lo̱i agbói le̱ ano̱!

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ni Rebeka te ši ke̱ eblayei le̱, ni ame̱ho ame̱trá yomai le̱ ano̱ ke̱-nyie nū le̱ se̱; ni tšũlo̱ le̱ ṅõ Rebeka ni eṅõſie no̱.

ref: At that time Isaac was living in the southern part of Canaan near a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Ni Isak dše̱ hĩekãlo̱ ní naämi le̱ *nubu le̱ gbe̱ le̱ no̱ ẽba; ši wuoyigbe̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ehĩo̱; *

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Ni tšũlo̱ le̱ gba Isak sãdši fẽ ní eyaye le̱.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Ni Isak ke̱le̱ ba enye Sara bū le̱ mli; ni eṅõ Rebeka ni ebatšṍ eṅã, ni esumo̱le̱. Ni Isak tšé̱ etšui eṅmẽ emli ye̱ *enye gbĕle le̱ se̱. *

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: Ni eke̱le̱ fo̱ Zimran ke̱ Iokšan ke̱ Medān ke̱ Midian ke̱ Išbak ke̱ Šua.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Ni Iokšon fe̱ Šeba ke̱ Dedan. Ni Dedan bii le̱ dši Ašurbii ke̱ Letušbii ke̱ Leumbii.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.
text: Ni Midian bii dši Efa ke̱ Efer ke̱ Hanok ke̱ Abida ke̱ Eldaa. Mene̱mei fẽ le̱ Ketura bii ni.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Ni Abraham gbẽle se̱ le̱ Nyoṅmo̱ dšō̱ ebi Isak; ni Isak hĩ ši ye̱ hĩekãlo̱ ni naämi le̱ *nubu le̱ hewo̱. *

ref: Ishmael had twelve sons, in this order: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text: Išmaelbii le̱ agbe̱ii ni aṅõ-tšē̱o̱ ame̱-sešibii le̱ ne̱: Išmael kromo̱bi le̱ Nebaiot, ke̱ Kedar ke̱ Adbeel ke̱ Mibsam,

ref: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text: ke̱ Mišma ke̱ Duma ke̱ Masa,

ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: ke̱ Hadad ke̱ Tema ke̱ Ietur ke̱ Nafiš ke̱ Kedema.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: Ni ame̱he ši ke̱-dše̱ Havila ke̱-yaši Šur ni ye̱ Mizraim hĩe, ke̱-yaä Ašur gbe̱ le̱. *Enyemimei le̱ fẽ hĩegbe̱ ehe ši ye̱. *

ref: Isaac was the son of Abraham,
text: Abraham bi Isak le̱ sešibii ne̱: Abraham fo̱ Isak.

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Ni Isak kpa Iehowa fai ehã eṅã le̱, edšake̱ efe kene; ni Iehowa bo efai-kpamo̱ le̱ toi, ni eṅã Rebeka ná mũsũ.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Ni gbekẽbii le̱ šimo̱šimō̱ ame̱he ye̱ emũsũṅ. Ni ekē̱: Kē̱dši nakai le̱, mẽha hũ ni mife ne̱ke̱ ne̱? Ni ete eyabi Iehowa,

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱le̱ ake̱: Dšeṅmãdši enyo̱ yo̱ omũsũṅ, ni mãdši enyo̱ aadše̱ omūsūṅ bẽbe agbla ame̱mli; ni maṅ kome he aawa ſè maṅ ko le̱, ni *onukpa le̱ aasúmo̱ gbekẽ le̱. *

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Kleṅkleṅmo ni ba le̱, etšuo̱ fö̱, ke̱ ehe fẽ sulaṅṅ tamo̱ bò ni ake̱ tšo̱i lò, ni ame̱wole̱ Esau.

ref: The second baby grabbed on to his brother's heel, so they named him Jacob. Isaac was
text: Ni no se̱ le̱ enyemi le̱ hũ ha, ni enine hĩe Esau nanetšitši mli, ni awole̱ Iakob. Ni beni afo̱ame̱ le̱ Isak eye afii nyoṅmai-ekpa.

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Ni Isak sumoo̱ Esau, edšake̱ mĩle̱lo ṅõ enā; ši Rebeka sumoo̱ Iakob.

ref: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
text: Ni Iakob ho niyenii ko. Ni Esau dše̱ ṅãṅ eba ni eto̱le̱.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: Ni Esau kē̱ Iakob ake̱: Mīkpao fai, hãmi pe̱ke̱ nitšuru ne̱ eko maye, ši eto̱mi! Nohewo̱ le̱ awole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Edom.

ref: Jacob replied, “Sell me your rights as the first-born son.”
text: Ši Iakob kē̱: Hõmo̱ okromo̱bi-gbe̱nā le̱ ohãmi dãl

ref: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
text: Ni Esau kē̱: Na, gbo mibagbo sa, ni meni kromo̱bi-gbe̱nā nē̱ bafe ahãmi?

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ ake̱: Kãmi no̱ kitã dã! Ni ekãle̱ no̱ kitã, ni *ehõ ekromo̱bi-gbe̱nā le̱ ebã Iakob. *

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iakob bã Esau abolo ke̱ abo̱bo̱i ní eho le̱ eko; ni eye ni enu noko eſie no̱, ni ete ši, ni eyiṅ ete. Ne̱ke̱ Esau gbe ekromo̱bi-gbe̱nã le̱ he guo̱.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Ni hõmo̱ ha šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, sōro *kleṅkleṅ hõmo̱ ni ba ye̱ Abraham yino̱ le̱; ni Isak yiṅ ete Filistibii amaṅtše̱ †Abimelek ṅõ ye̱ Gerar. *

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: Ni mahã oseši *afá tamo̱ ṅwei ṅulamii, ni maṅõ ne̱ke̱ šikpõdši ne̱ fẽ mahã oseši le̱, ni oseši le̱ †mli aadše̱ adšō̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ dšeṅmādši le̱ fẽ; *

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: Ni Isak hĩ ši ye̱ Gerar.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Ni dše̱ibii le̱ bi eṅã le̱ ši; ni ekē̱ akē̱: Minyemiyō ni; edšakē̱ ēše gbeyei ake̱ eekē̱ ake̱: Miṅã ni, ekē̱: Beni afe ni dše̱ibii le̱ akabagbemi ye̱ Rebeka hewo̱, edšake̱ ebe ye̱ fe̱o.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Ni Abimelek tšē̱ Isak ni ekē̱ ake̱: Ei, yō le̱ oṅã ni kwá; ni té fe tēṅ ni okē̱ akē̱: Minyemiyõ dšile̱ le̱? Ni Isak kē̱le̱ ake̱: Edšake̱ mikē̱: Beni afe ní akabagbemi ye̱ ehewo̱.

ref: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
text: Ni Abimelek wo maṅ le̱ fẽ kitã ake̱: Moni aata nū ne̱ lo eṅã le̱ he le̱, gbe aagbele̱!

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: Ni nū le̱ tšṍ gbomo agbo, ni ehle ſeremo̱ āhũ ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni etšṍ agbo kpanã.

ref: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
text: Eye̱ tōkui ke̱ tšinakui ke̱ webii pī; ni Filistibii le̱ ahĩe kõ eno̱.

ref: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
text: Ni *nubui fẽ ni etše̱ Abraham tšũdši le̱ dše ye̱ etše̱ yino̱ le̱, Filistibii le̱ etšĩmo̱, ni amelō sũ ame̱wo mli totō̱to̱. *

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Ni Abimelek kē̱ Isak ake̱: Dše wo̱mase̱i, ši ohe wa babaō fèwo̱!

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Ni Isak sā edše nubui le̱ ni adše ye̱ etše̱ Abraham yino̱ le̱, edšake̱ Abraham gbẽle se̱ le̱ Filistibii le̱ tšĩmo̱; ni eṅõ gbe̱ii ni etše̱ ṅõ-woame̱ le̱ noṅṅ ewoame̱.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: Ni Isak tšũdši le̱ tša dšō̱ le̱ mli, ni ame̱ná nubu ni baä nu ye̱ dše̱i.

ref: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
text: Ni Gerar kolokwe̱le̱i ke̱ Isak kolokwe̱lo̱i le̱ bé ni ame̱kē̱: Nu le̱ wo̱nõṅ! ni ewo nubu le̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Esek, edšake̱ ame̱ke̱le̱ blo̱ ye̱ he.

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Ni edše dše̱i ete Beeršeba.

ref: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
text: Ni Iehowa dšie ehe kpo etšõle̱ nakai nyõ le̱ṅ noṅṅ, ni eké̱: Midši otše̱ Abraham Nyoṅmo̱ le̱; kãše gbeyei, ši mike̱o ye̱, ni madšō̱o, ni mahã oseši afá ye̱ mitšũlo̱ Abraham hewo̱.

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Ni emã afo̱lešãlate̱ ye̱ dše̱i, ni etšé̱ Iehowa gbe̱i, ni eši ebū le̱ ye̱ dše̱i; ni Isak tšũdši le̱ dše nubu ye̱ dše̱i hũ.

ref: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
