text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abimelek ke̱ enanyo Ahuzat ke̱ etatše̱ Fikol dše Gerar ba Isak ṅõ.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Ni Isak kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Meni bewo̱ nyebaä miṅõ, nye ni nyenye̱o̱mi, ni nyeſiemi ye̱ nyemase̱i le̱?

ref: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Na wo̱na ake̱ Iehowa ke̱o ye̱; ni wo̱kē̱: Hã kitã ahĩ wo̱teṅ, wo̱ke̱o fẽ teṅ, ni ohã wo̱ke̱o akpãṅ,

ref: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
text: ake̱ ofeṅwo̱ efõṅ ko; edšake̱ wo̱tako ohe, ni wo̱fekoö nokonoko, ake̱ dša ekpakpa ke̱ke̱, ni wosā wo̱woö gbe̱ ye̱ hedšo̱le̱ mli. Ni agbe̱ne̱ bo Iehowa edšō̱ ne̱!

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Ni eṅmẽame̱ okplõ, ni ame̱ye nii, ni ame̱nu noko.

ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: Ni eba mli nakai gbĩ le̱ noṅṅ ake̱ Isak tšũdši le̱ ha ni ame̱bagbale̱ nubu ni ame̱dše le̱ he sane ake̱: Wo̱yanina nu.

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Ni ewole̱ *Šeba; nohewo̱ ni atšé̱o̱ maṅ le̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Beeršeba ke̱ ṅme̱ne̱ṅme̱ne̱ le̱. *

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Isak egbo̱, ni ebĩṅme̱ii ano̱ eha ni enaa nii dšogbāṅ le̱, etšé̱ ebi nukpa Esau le̱, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Mibi! Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Nami!

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: ni obafele̱ niyenii ke̱ ṅõmo̱, take̱ bo̱ni misumoo̱ le̱, ni oke̱-bahãmi, koni maye, beni afe ni misusuma adšō̱o, dãni magbo. Heb.

ref: Rebekah had been listening, and as soon as Esau left to go hunting,
text: Ni Rebeka nu, beni Isak ke̱ ebi Esau wieo̱ le̱. Ni Esau yiṅ ete ṅã le̱ṅ eyatao mĩle̱lo ke̱-ba.

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: Ni Rebeka yakē̱ ebi Iakob ake̱: Na, minu ake̱ otše̱ ke̱ onyemi Esau miwie ake̱:

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: Yatao mĩle̱lo, ni ofele̱ niyenii ke̱ ṅõmo̱ obahãmi, koni maye, ni madšō̱o ye̱ Iehowa hĩe, dãni magbo.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Ya tōi le̱ ateṅ ní oyamõmo̱ tōbii kpakpai enyo̱ ye̱ abotiai le̱ ateṅ obahãmi, ni maṅõ-fe niyenii ke̱ ṅõmo̱ mahã otše̱, take̱ bo̱ni esumoo̱ le̱,

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ enye Rebeka ake̱: Na, minyemi *Esau le̱, tšo̱iatše̱ dšile̱, ni mi le̱, mihe ye̱ trotro. *

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: Ni enye kē̱le̱ ake̱: Mi ne̱ olōmo̱ le̱ aba, mibi, bo migbẽ toi ke̱ke̱, ni oyamõmo̱ obahãmi!

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Ni eyiṅ ete, ni eyamõmo̱ ni eke̱bahã enye; ni enye ṅõ-fe niyenii ke̱ ṅõmo̱, tamo̱ bo̱ni etše̱ sumoo̱ le̱.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Ni Rebeka kō̱lo̱ ebi nukpa Esau atadei fe̱fe̱dši ní yo̱ eṅõ ye̱ šĩa le̱, ni eṅṍ-wo Iakob, ebi gbekẽ le̱;

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: ni eṅõ tōbii le̱ ahe wodši le̱ ewo enidši ke̱ ekue̱, hei ní ye̱ trotro le̱;

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Ni eba etše̱ ṅõ ni ebakē̱: Mitše̱! Ni ekē̱: Nami! Bo namoṅ, mibi?

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ etše̱ ake̱: Mi, Esau, okromo̱bi le̱ṅ; mife be̱ni okē̱mi le̱. Ofai ne̱, te ši otá ši, ni oye mimĩle̱lo le̱ eko, koni osusuma adšō̱mi!

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Ni Isak kē̱ Iakob ake̱: Tši oba, mibi, ni matăra ohe makwe̱ ake̱ mibi Esau dientše̱ dšio lo dšee le̱ ni.

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Ni Iakob tši ebe̱ṅke̱ etše̱ Isak, ni etāra ebe, ni ekē̱: Gbē le̱ Iakob gbē ni, Ši nidši le̱ Esau nidši ni.

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Ni etši eba ni ebaſo̱ enā; ni enu etadei le̱ amli ṅma le̱, ni edšō̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Kwē̱, mibi lē̱ he ṅma tamo̱ ṅã ko ni Iehowa edšō̱ le̱ ṅma!

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Nyoṅmo̱ ahão ṅwei hō̱ ke̱ šikpoṅ fo̱ le̱ eko ke̱ ṅmā̃ ke̱ wein babaō hũ.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Mãdši asúmo̱o, ni dšeṅmãdši akotóö. Ye onyemimei le̱ ano̱ *nuṅtšo̱, ni onye bihĩĩ akotoö. Mofẽmo ni †lomoo̱o le̱, aalomo̱le̱; ni mofẽmo ní dšō̱o̱o le̱, aadšō̱le̱! *

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Isak egbe Iakob dšō̱mo̱ le̱ nā, ni Iakob kē̱ edšeo̱ etše̱ Isak hĩe pe̱ le̱, enyemi Esau hũ dše̱ emĩle̱ le̱ eba;

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: ni le̱ hũ efe niyenii ke̱ ṅõmo̱ ni eke̱-bahã etše̱, ni ekē̱ etše̱ ake̱: Hã mitše̱ ate ši, ni eye ehi mĩle̱lo le̱ eko, koni osusuma adšō̱mi.

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Ni etše̱ Isak kē̱le̱ ake̱: Bo namoṅ? Ni ekē̱: Mi, obi, okromo̱bi Esau ni.

ref: Esau cried out in great distress, “Father, give me a blessing too!”
text: Ni beni Esau nu etše̱ wiemo̱i le̱, ebo wā dientše̱ ke̱ aw̌ere̱hō babaō, ni ekē̱ etše̱ ake̱: Mi hũ, dšō̱mo̱mi eko, mitše̱! Heb.

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Ni ekē̱: Egbe̱i *Iakob ni awole̱ le̱ edi ehe. Šii enyo̱ so̱ṅṅ ní etõtṍo̱ minane ne̱: †mikromo̱bi-gbe̱nā le̱ ešõ, ni na, agbe̱ne̱ ebaṅõ midšō̱mo̱ le̱ bũ! Ni ekē̱: Ani oſe̱e̱ dšō̱mo̱ le̱ eko ohããmi? *

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Ni Isak to be ni ekē̱ Esau ake̱: Na, miwole̱ ono̱ nuṅtšo̱, ni miṅṍ enyemimei le̱ fẽ mife etšũdši, ni miṅṍ ṅmā̃ ke̱ wein mihãle̱; ni bo le̱, meni mafe mahão mo̱, mibi?

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Ni Esau kē̱ etše̱ ake̱: Ani no kome pe̱ dši dšō̱mo̱ ni oyo̱, mitše̱? Mi hũ dšō̱mo̱mi eko, mitše̱! Ni Esau wó egbẽ ni efó.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Ni etše̱ Isak to he, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Na, heni šikpoṅ fo̱ be̱ oohĩ ke̱ heni ṅwei bō̱ hũ be̱;

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: ni oklante nā oyi aaná wala ye̱, ni onyemi le̱ oosúmo̱. Ši aaba mli ake̱, ké̱ ople ohe le̱, oohwãnyã epã le̱ ke̱-dše okue̱.

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Ni Esau nye̱ Iakob ye̱ dšō̱mo̱ ni etše̱ dšō̱le̱ le̱ hewo̱. Ni Esau kē̱ ye̱ etšui mli ake̱: Gbĩĩ ní ake̱-bafó mitše̱ le̱ eše̱ etã, ni magbe minyemi Iakob.

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Ni abagba Rebeka ebi nukpa Esau wiemo̱i ne̱; ni etšũ ayatšé̱ Iakob, ebi gbekẽ le̱, ke̱-ba, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Na, onyemi Esau bato̱ ehe owele ye̱ ono̱ ni egbeo.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱, mibi, bo migbē toi, ni ote ši ni odšo foi ke̱-ya minyemi Laban ṅõ ye̱ *Haran, *

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: ni oke̱le̱ ayaht ši gbĩĩ flō ko ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni onyemi le̱ mlila le̱ aadše emli,

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni onyemi le̱ mlifũ ní enáo le̱ aadše emli, ni ehĩe aakpa noni ofele̱ le̱ no̱, koni matšũ ni abaṅõo ke̱-dše̱ dše̱i ke̱-ba; mẽba nyeyi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ nyeaṅmẽmi gbī kome?

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Ni Rebeka kē̱ Isak ake̱: Midšeṅhĩle̱ edše mitšine ye̱ Hetbiyei le̱ ahewo̱. Ke̱-dši Iakob w̌e̱ Hetbiyei ni tame̱ mene̱mei ni dši šikpoṅ ne̱, no̱ biyei le̱ eko le̱, té mahĩ dšeṅ make̱ tẽṅ?

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: ni eṅõ *Abraham dšō̱mo̱ le̱ ehão ke̱ oseši fẽ, koni ofe ogbo̱tō šikpoṅ ne̱ ni Nyóṅmo̱ ṅõ-bã Abraham le̱ yelo̱; *

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Ni beni Esau na ake̱ Isak edšō̱ Iakob, ni ewole̱ gbe̱ ke̱-te Mesopotamia, ni eyaw̌e̱ yō ye̱ dše̱i, ni asaṅ beni edšō̱le̱ le̱, ewole̱ kitã ake̱: Kāw̌e̱ yō ye̱ Kanaan biyei le̱ ateṅ,

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: ni Esau sā na ake̱ etše̱ Isak nyã́ã́ Kanaan biyei le̱ ahe le̱:

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: Esau yiṅ ete Abraham bi Išmael ṅõ, ni eyaw̌e̱ ebiyō Mahalat, *Nebaiot nyemiyõ le̱, †efata eṅãmei ni eyo̱ le̱ ahe. *

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Ni Iakob dše̱ Beeršeba ēya Haran.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Ni eše̱ heko ni eto dše̱i, edšake̱ hũlũ enyõ ši; ni eko̱ dše̱i te̱i le̱ eko etšo̱le, ni ekā dše̱i ewo̱.

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Ni Iakob hĩe tšẽ kē̱-dše̱ ewo̱ le̱ mli ni ekē̱: Iehowa ye̱ bie̱ kwá, ni mi le̱ milee ne̱!

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Ni eše gbeyei ni ekē̱: Bie̱ ne̱ ye̱ *gbeyei pi! Bie̱ ne̱ dšee hekoheko ake̱ Nyoṅmo̱ we ne̱, ni ṅwei agbó ne̱! *

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: Ni Iakob te ši lebi mā̃ṅkpa, ni etē te̱ le̱ ni etšo̱le le̱ ši emã ši eṅõ-to okadi, ni efo̱se mũ eſie yiteṅ;

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: ni ewo dše̱i *Betel; ši sá le̱ nakai maṅ le̱ gbe̱i dši Luz. *

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Ni Iakob wo ši ni ekē̱: Ke̱dši Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱mi hĩ ši, ni ebu mihe ye̱ gbe̱ ni miyanyie ne̱ mli, ni ehãmi ṅmā̃ miye ke̱ atade miwo,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: ni mikũ mise̱ miba mitše̱ we le̱ ye̱ hedšo̱le̱ mli le̱, no le̱ Iehowa aatšṍ mi-Nyoṅmo̱;

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: ni ne̱ke̱ te̱ ni miṅṍ-mã ši mito okadi ne̱ *aatšṍ Nyoṅmo̱ we; ni nibii fẽ ni oohãmi le̱, maṅõ mlidšāi nyoṅma mli ekome mahão. *

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iakob wó enane no̱ ni eba bokãgbe̱bii ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Ni ekwe̱, ni na, nubu ko ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱, ni na, tōkui etẽ dſehe he ye̱ dše̱i; edšake̱ nakai bu le̱ mli nu le̱ aye̱e̱ ahãa tōi le̱ nuo̱; ni te̱ kpeteṅkple kã bu le̱ nā

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Minyemimei, nẽgbe̱ nyedše̱? Ni ame̱kē̱: Wo̱dšē̱ Haran.

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: Ni ebiame̱ ake̱: Ani nyele Nahor bi Laban le̱? Ni ame̱kē̱: Wo̱lelē̱.

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Ni ebiame̱ ake̱: Ani eye̱ dšogbāṅ lo? Ni ame̱kē̱: Eye̱ dšogbãṅ; ni na, ebiyō Rahel ke̱ tōi baä ne̱!

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Ni eké̱: Na, dše nako kwrā, ni eše̱ko koloi awo̱be; nyehãa tōi le̱ anua nu, ni nyeyahãaame̱ nii!

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Ni ame̱ké̱: Wo̱nyẽṅ, dša abna tō-kuikui le̱ fẽ nā ke̱-kpe, ni ame̱koklo te̱ le̱ ke̱-dše bu le̱ nā, dãni wo̱o̱hã tōi le̱ anu.

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Ni beni eke̱ame̱ wieo̱ lolo le̱, Rahel ke̱ etše̱ tōi le̱ ba, edšake̱ le̱ ekwe̱o̱ame̱.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Ni Iakob ſo̱ Rahel nā, ewó egbẽ no̱ ni efó.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ Rahel ake̱ etše̱ nyemi dšile̱, ni Rebeka bi dšile̱; ke̱ke̱ ni eša foi ete ni eyakē̱ etše̱.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Laban nu enyemibi Iakob sane le̱ he le̱, eša foi etele̱ kpẽmo̱, ni elōle̱ atū ni eſo̱ enā, ni eke̱le̱ ba ewe le̱; ni egba Laban sãdši ne̱ fẽ.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Ni Laban kē̱le̱ ake̱: Asano̱kwa, miwú ke̱ mihelo dšio! Ni eke̱le̱ hĩ ši nyõṅ kome.

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Ni Laban kē̱ Iakob ake̱: Ake̱-ní oyafe minyemi hewo̱ le̱, oosúmo̱mi yaka lo? Tšõmo̱ bo̱ni oohemi.

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Ni Lea hĩṅme̱ii ye̱ sukusuku, ši Rahel he ye̱ fe̱o ni ehĩe hũ ye̱ fe̱o.

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Ni Laban kē̱: Ehĩ ake̱ mike̱le̱ hãao fè noni mike̱le̱ aahã nū kroko; oke̱mi ahĩ ši!

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Ni Iakob súmo̱ afii kpawo ye̱ Rahel hewo̱, ni suomo̱ ní esumoo̱le̱ hewo̱ le̱, efele̱ tamo̱ gbĩ komekome ke̱ke̱.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ Laban ake̱: Oke̱ miṅã le̱ ahãmi, koni ebawo̱ miṅō, edšake̱ mi-gbĩĩ le̱ eše̱.

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Ni Laban bua dše̱i bii le̱ fẽ nā, ni eṅmẽ okplõ.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Ni gbe̱ke̱ le̱ eba mli ake̱ eṅõ ebiyō Lea ni eke̱le̱ bahãle̱; ni ebawo̱ eṅõ.

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Ni Laban ṅõ eweyō Zilpa ehã ebi Lea, ni efe eweyõ.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni dše tše̱re le̱, na, Lea ni. Ni ekē̱ Laban ake̱: Meni ofemi ne̱? Ani dšee Rahel hewo̱ ni misúmo̱o le̱? Mẽhewo̱ mo̱ ni ošišiumi ne̱?

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Ni Laban kē̱: Wo̱bie̱ ne̱ afee nakai, ahãã gbekẽ gblã dāni ahã kromo̱bi.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Gbe mone̱ otši le̱ nā, ni wo̱ke̱ mokroko le̱ hũ aahão ye̱ súomo̱ ní oosúmo̱mi afii kpawo kroko hũ hewo̱.

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Ni beni Iehowa na ake̱ asumo̱o̱ Lea le̱, egble emũsũ nā, ši Rahel fe kene.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Ni Lea ná mũsũ, ni efo̱ binū, ni ewole̱ Ruben, ni eke̱: Edšake̱ Iehowa ekwe̱ mimanehũlũ le̱; ni agbe̱ne̱ miwù basumo̱mi!

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Ni ená mũsũ ekoṅ, ni efo̱ binū, ni ekē̱: Edšakē̱ Iehowa enu ake̱ asumo̱o̱-mi, hewo̱ le̱ ehāmi bi ne̱ bũ, ni ewole̱ Simeon.

ref: When Leah's third son was born, she said, “Now my husband will hold me close.” So this son was named Levi.
text: Ni esā ená mũsũ, ni efo̱ binù, ni ekē̱: Ene̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ miwù ke̱ ehe aakpe̱te̱ mihe, edšake̱ mike̱le̱ efo̱ bihĩĩ etẽ; nohewo̱ le̱ ewole̱ Levi.

ref: She had one more son and named him Judah, because she said, “I'll praise the Lord!”
text: Ni ená mũsũ ekoṅ, ni efo̱ binū, ni ekē̱: Ene̱ le̱ madšie Iehowa yi; nohe-wo̱ le̱ ewole̱ Iuda. Ni efo fo̱mo̱ dã.

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Ni ekē̱: Na, miweyō Bilha; hã ebawo̱ oṅõ, *ni efo̱ eſie miſuo̱ no̱, koni mi hū matšṍ eno̱ maná bii. *

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Ni Rahel ké̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ekodšo ehã-mi, ni enu migbē hũ, ni ehãmi binū; nohewo̱ lo̱ ewole̱ Dan.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Ni Bilha, Rahel weyō le̱, ná mũsũ ekoṅ, ni eke̱ Iakob fo̱ binū ni dši enyo̱.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Ni Rahel ké̱: Mike̱ minyemiyō le̱ eši Nyoṅmo̱ mple, ni minyẽle̱! Ni ewole̱ Naftali.

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Ni beni Lea na ake̱ efo fo̱mo̱ le̱, le̱ hũ eṅõ eweyō Zilpa le̱ ehã Iakob gblā.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Ni Lea weyō Zilpa le̱ ke̱ Iakob fo̱ binū.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Ni Lea kē̱: Asafo eba! ni ewole̱ Gad.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Ni Lea kē̱: Midšō̱mo̱ mli nē̱! edšake̱ biyei badšō̱mi; ni ewole̱ Ašer.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Ni ṅmā̃kū̃ be mli le̱ Ruben yana suomo̱yibii ko ye̱ ṅãno̱, ni eke̱-bahã enye Lea. Ke̱ke̱ ni Rahel kē̱ Lea akē̱: Mīkpao fai ni ohãmi obi lē̱ suomo̱yibii le̱ eko!

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Aso ni ošõ miwù le̱, no fáá, ši obašõ mibi le̱ suomo̱yibii le̱ hũ? Ni Rahel kē̱: Nohewo̱ le̱ yawo̱ eṅõ ṅme̱no̱ nyõ ne̱ṅ ye̱ obi le̱ suomo̱yibii le̱ hewo̱.

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ bo Lea toi, ni ená mũsũ, ni eke̱ Iakob fo̱ binū ni dši enumo̱.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Ni Lea kē̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ehãmi mipābō̱nii, edšake̱ mike̱ miweyō le̱ ehã miwù gblã; ni ewole̱ Isakar.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Ni no se̱ le̱ efo̱ biyō, ni ewole̱ Dina.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Ni ená mũsũ, ni efo̱ binū, ni ekē̱: Nyoṅmo̱ edšie *mihĩegbĕle le̱; Ies.

ref: “I'll name the boy Joseph, and I'll pray that the Lord will give me another son.” Jacob and Laban
text: ni ewole̱ Iosef, ni ekē̱: Iehowa aaṅõ bi kroko afata he ahãmi!

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Ni eba mli ake, beni Rahel efo̱ Iosef se̱ le̱, Iakob kē̱ Laban akē̱: Womi gbe̱, koni maya midientše̱ midšē̱he ke̱ mišikpoṅ no̱.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Oke̱ miṅãmei ke̱ mibii ni *misúmo̱o ye̱ ame̱hewo̱ le̱ ahāmi, ni maya; ši bo-dientše̱ ole bo̱ni misúmo̱o mihã. *

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Bodientše̱ ole, bo̱ni misúmo̱o mihá, ke̱ bo̱ hũ ni okoloi le̱ ht miṅõ hã.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Ši fiõ ko oyo̱ beni miba le̱, ni agbe̱ne̱ le̱ ame̱ſere babaõ, ni Iehowa etšṍ minine no̱ edšō̱o, ni agbe̱ne̱ le̱, té gbĩ ni makwe̱ midientše̱ miwe hũ?

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Ni ekē̱: Meni mahão? Ni Iakob kē̱: Kāhãmi nokonoko; kē̱dši oofe ene̱ ohãmi le̱, no le̱ masã malè̱ okoloi le̱, ni ma-kwe̱ame̱ ekoṅ:

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Nme̱ne̱ makpa otoi le̱ fẽ mli, ni mahala tõbi-fẽ-tõbi ni he yo̱ truntrãi ke̱ damdam, ke̱ tōbi-fẽ-tōbi ni dĩo̱ ye̱ tō-gwanteṅi le̱ amli, ke̱ abotiai le̱ ateṅ nii ni he yo̱ damdam ke̱ nii ni be yo̱ truntrãi le̱; ni nomei aatšṍ minyṍmo̱wo,

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: koni wō̱ lo wō̱se̱, beni oobakwe̱ minyõmo̱wo le̱, midšale̱ le̱ adšie minã ye̱ ohĩe; abotiai le̱ ateṅ nii ní be be̱ truntrãi ke̱ damdam, ke̱ gwanteṅi le̱ amli nii ni be dĩĩ le̱, dšũnii dši no ye̱ mideṅ.

ref: “I agree to that,” was Laban's response.
text: Ni Laban kē̱: Na, bã afe be̱ni okē̱ ne̱.

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: Ni nakai gbĩ le̱ no̱ le̱ eyahala tōkpakpoi ni he ye̱ sãmãisãmãi ke̱ damdam, kē̱ abotiai le̱ ateṅ nii ni he yo̱ truntrãi ke̱ damdam le̱ fẽ, nofẽno ní eye̱ṅ ta ehe ke̱ nii fẽ ni he dĩo̱ ye̱ gwanteṅi le̱ ateṅ, ni etuame̱ ewo ebihĩĩ le̱ adeṅ.

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: Ni eṅõ gbĩĩ etẽ gbe̱ eto ame̱ ke̱ le̱ Iakob le̱ teṅ. Ni Iakob yalè̱ Laban tōi ní ſe̱ le̱.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Ni Iakob kō̱le̱ libne-tšei ṅmõdši ke̱ luz-tšei ke̱ harmon-tšei, ni eye ame̱he sāmãi ye̱dši, ni ebã tšei le̱ amli eye̱dši le̱ dše ši.

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ni eṅõ tšei ni eye ame̱he sãmãi le̱ eſie nunumo̱ ahĩmai le̱ ni tōi le̱ banuo̱ nu ye̱ṅ le̱ amli, ye̱ tōi le̱ ahĩe, ní ké̱ amē̱ba nunumo̱ lē̱, ame̱náa mũsũ.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Ni Iakob tšere̱ tōgwanteṅbii le̱ eto afã, ni etšṍmo̱ tōi le̱ ahle ehã Laban tōi le̱ ateṅ nii ní he yo̱ sãmãisãmãi le̱ ke̱ nii ní dĩo̱ le̱ fë; ni eṅõ le̱dientše̱ etōku le̱ eto afã, ni eke̱ame̱ futuu Laban tōi le̱.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ dāne̱ ké̱ tōi ni be wa le̱ ná mũsũ le̱, Iakob ṅõo̱ tšei le̱ eſieo̱ tōi le̱ ahĩe ye̱ ahĩmai le̱ amli, koni ame̱ná mũsũ ye̱ tšei le̱ ahe.

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Ši ké̱ tōi le̱ ye̱ wudo̱wudo̱ le̱, no le̱ eṅṍ-ſiee mli; nohewo̱ le̱ wudo̱wudo̱i le̱ tšṍmoo̱ Laban nii, ni nii ní be wa le̱ tšṍmoo̱ Iakob nii.

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Ni gbomo le̱ ſere naakpa dientše̱, ni ená tōi pī ke̱ weyei ke̱ wehĩĩ ke̱ yomai ke̱ tedšii.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Ni enu ni Laban *bihĩĩ le̱ mīwiewie ake̱: Iakob ešõ wo̱tše̱ nii fẽ, ni we̱tše̱ nii le̱ eke̱-ná ehe ne̱. *

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Ni Iakob tšũ ayatšē̱ Rahel ke̱ Lea ke̱-ba ṅã le̱ no̱ heni etōi le̱ yo̱ le̱,

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: ni ekē̱ame̱ ake̱: Mina nyetše̱ hĩe, ake̱ etšee ehĩe ehããmi tamo̱ sá le̱; ši *mitše̱ Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ ke̱mi ye̱.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: Ke̱dši ekē̱: Nii ni be yo̱ *truntrãi le̱ atšṍ onyõmo̱wo le̱, bĕle̱ tōi le̱ fẽ efo̱ truntrãi; ni ke̱dši ekē̱: Nii ni he yo̱ sãmãisãmãi le̱ atšṍ onyõmo̱wo le̱, bẽle̱ tōi le̱ fẽ efo̱ nii ni he yo̱ sãmãisãmãi. *

ref: That's how God has taken sheep and goats from your father and given them to me.
text: Ne̱ke̱ Nyoṅmo̱ fe ešõ ṅyetše̱ koloi le̱ ehãmi.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: Ni eba mii ake̱ tōi le̱ amũsũnámo̱ be mli le̱ mihole mihĩṅme̱ii ano̱, ni mina ye̱ lamõ mli, ake̱ tōkpakpoi ní kwo̱ō̱ tōi le̱ ahe ye̱ sãmãisãmãi ke̱ truntrãi ke̱ ṅwãtãṅwãtã.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Midši *Betel, heni ofo̱ okadite̱ le̱ mũ ni owomi ši ye̱ le̱, Nyoṅmo̱ le̱. Aghe̱ne̱ te ši, dše šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱, ni okũ ose̱ oya owekumei ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱. *

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: Ani dšee meikpõdši ebuo̱wo̱? edšake̱ *ehõwe̱, ni eye wo̱yinii le̱ hũ.

ref: Then Jacob, his wives, and his children got on camels and left
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iakob te ši ni eṅõ ebii ke̱ eṅãmei le̱ etrá yomai ano̱,

ref: northern Syria for the home of his father Isaac in Canaan. Jacob took along all his flocks, herds, and other property.
text: ni ekpla ekoloi le̱ fẽ, ke̱ enii fẽ ni ená le̱, koloi ni elē̱ ni ená ye̱ Mesopotamia le̱, koni eya etše̱ Isak ṅõ ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni eke̱ nii ni eyo̱ le̱ fẽ dšo foi; ni ete ši ni eyafo fã le̱, ni eṅõ ehĩe eka Gilead goṅ le̱.

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Laban yanina Iakob; ni Iakob eši ebū ye̱ goṅ le̱ no̱, ni Laban hũ ke̱ enyemimei le̱ baši ame̱būi le̱ ye̱ Gilead goṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Ni Laban kē̱ Iakob ake̱: Meni ofe ne̱, ake̱ odšu mitšui ni olō mibiyei le̱ ke̱-mĩya take̱ mei ní aṅõame̱ nom ye̱ klante nā ne̱?

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Ši mofẽmo dẽ ní oona onyoṅmo̱i le̱ ye̱ le̱, kāhã ehĩ dšeṅl Wo̱nyemimei ne̱ ahĩe ne̱, taomo̱ noni dši onõ ní yo̱ miṅõ, ni oko̱ onii ke̱-ya. Ši Iakob lee ake̱ Rahel edšu. K.

ref: Laban searched the tents of Jacob, Leah, and the two servant women, but did not find the idols. Then he went to Rachel's tent.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Laban te Iakob bū le̱ mli ke̱ Lea bū le̱ mli ke̱ weyei enyo̱ le̱ abūi le̱ hũ mli, ši enaa; ni edše Lea bū le̱ mli ete Rahel bū le̱ mli.

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Ši Rahel ekō̱lo̱ amãgãi le̱, ni eṅõwo yoma le̱ no̱nii le̱ mli, ni eho etá no̱; ni Laban nyẽmo̱nyẽmo̱ ši ye̱ bū le̱ṅ fẽ, ši enaa.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Ni ekē̱ etše̱ ake̱: Akafe minuṅtšo̱ mlifũ ake̱ minyẽẽ ši mate ye̱ ohĩe le̱; ši mife yeianii. Ni ekpa ši etao amāgãi le̱ āhũ, ši enaa.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Ni Iakob mli wo la, ni eke̱ Laban bé. Ni Iakob here no̱ ni ekē̱ Laban ake̱: Mẽ tõ mitõ, ni mẽ eša hũ mife, ni ohĩe edo̱ ke̱-nyie mise̱ ne̱ke̱ ne̱?

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: Kole̱ ni otãratăra minibii fẽ he ne̱, meni ona ní dši ošĩa nibii le̱ eko? Nõ sane ne̱ oṅmẽ minyemimei ke̱ bo hũ onyemimei ne̱ ahĩe ye̱ bie̱, ni ame̱kodšo wo̱yi enyo̱ le̱ wo̱teṅ.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Noni koloi ye hū mike̱-bahãão, mi mitoo̱ nãdšiaṅ, ké̱ ſane adšu eko dšio, nyōṅ adšu eko dšio, *mideṅ oheo̱ ye̱. *

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.
text: Noni mina ne̱: latšā tšaämi ſane, ni fẽi yeo̱mi nyōṅ, ni miwo̱ hũ tšere̱o̱ ye̱ mihĩe.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Ni Laban here no̱ ni ekē̱ Iakob akē̱: Biyei nē̱ mibiyei ni, ni bii ne̱ hũ mibii ni, tōi ne̱ hũ mitōi ni, ni nibii ní onaä ne̱ fē le̱ minii ni; ši meni mawo mibiyei ne̱ ke̱ ame̱bii ni ame̱fo̱fo̱i le̱ afemo̱ ṅme̱ne̱?

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ ba, ni wo̱kpãṅ, mi ke̱ bo, ní atšŏ odase ye̱ mike̱o teṅ.

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Ni Iakob wó te̱ emā ši eṅõ-to *okadi.

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ enyemimei le̱ ake̱: Nyebuaä te̱i anā! Ni ame̱kö̱lo̱ te̱i ni ame̱tole̱ ku; ni ame̱ye nii ye̱ dše̱i ye̱ ku le̱ no̱.

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Ni Laban wole̱ Iegar-Sahaduta; ši Iakob le̱ ewole̱ Galed.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “This pile of rocks will remind us of our agreement.” That's why the place was named Galeed.
text: Ni Laban kē̱: Ne̱kē̱ ku ne̱ mĩye *odase ye̱ mike̱o teṅ ṅme̱ne̱; nohewo̱ atšé̱o̱ egbe̱i Galed, *Ios.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Ke̱dši oowa mibiyei le̱ ayi, alo oow̌e̱ yei krokomei ofata mibiyei le̱ ahe le̱, no le̱ gbomo ko fataa wo̱he moṅ, ši na, Nyoṅmo̱ dši odasefonyo ye̱ mike̱o teṅ!

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Ne̱ke̱ ku ne̱ dši odase, ni ne̱ke̱ okadite̱ ne̱ hũ dši odase, ake̱ mi le̱ mihoṅ ku ne̱ he mibaṅ oṅõ, ni bo hũ ohoṅ ku ne̱ ke̱ okadite̱ ne̱ he obaṅ miṅõ ye̱ efõṅ-femo̱ gbe̱.

ref: My father Nahor, your grandfather Abraham, and their ancestors all worshiped the same God, and he will make sure that we each keep the agreement. Then Jacob made a promise in the name of the fearsome God his father Isaac had worshiped.
text: Abraham Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ ke̱ Nahor Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ ke̱ ame̱tše̱ Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ afe *ko-dšolo̱ ye̱ wo̱teṅ. Ni Iakob kã etše̱ Isak †gbeyeišeme̱ le̱ kitã. *

ref: Jacob killed an animal and offered it as a sacrifice there on the mountain, and he invited his men to eat with him. After the meal they spent the night on the mountain.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iakob šã afo̱le ye̱ goṅ le̱ no̱, ni etšé̱ enyemimei le̱ niyeli; ni ame̱ye nii, ni ame̱hi ši nyõ le̱ṅ fẽ ye̱ goṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Ni lebi mā̃ṅkpa le̱ Laban te ši ni eſo̱ſō̱ ebihĩĩ ke̱ ebiyei le̱ anã ni edšō̱ame̱. Ni Laban ṅõ-tšere̱ mli ke̱-kũ ese̱ ke̱-te emaṅ.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Ni Iakob tšũ bo̱foi ke̱-tšṍ ehĩe ke̱-te enyemi Esau ṅõ ye̱ *Seir šikpoṅ ni dši Edom ṅã le̱ no̱. *

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Ni ewoame̱ kitã ake̱: Nyeyakē̱a minuṅtšo̱ Esau akē̱: Bo̱ni otšũlo̱ Iakob kē̱ ne̱: Miyato gbo̱ ye̱ Laban ṅõ, ni mitše̱ ye̱ dše̱i āhũ ke̱-baši bene̱,

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: ni miná tšinai ke̱ tedšii ke̱ tōi ke̱ tšūdšihĩĩ ke̱ tšũdšiyei; ni mitšũ ni abakē̱ minuṅtšo̱, koni mihĩe aba nyam ye̱ ohĩe.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: ni ekē̱: Ke̱dši Esau ba asafoku kome ne̱ no̱ ni ebatſiaame̱ nii le̱, koni ku kome ní eſe̱ le̱ aná gbe̱ adšo foi.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: omo̱bo̱nale̱i ke̱ onokwayeli fẽ ni odšie otšõ otšũlo̱ le̱, ekomeflo ne̱ke̱ *esaami; ši tšo mihĩe ke̱-fo Iordan ne̱, ni agbe̱ne̱ mitšṍ asafokui enyo. *

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ni Iakob bile̱ ake̱: Mīkpao fai ni *otšõmi ogbe̱i! Ni ekē̱: Meni hewo̱ ni obio̱ migbe̱i ne̱? Ni edšō̱le̱ ye̱ dše̱i. *Kodš.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: no le̱ okē̱ ake̱: Otšũlo̱ Iakob nikẽnii ní etšũ ake̱ ake̱-bake minuṅtšo̱ Esau ni; ni na, le̱ hũ enyie wo̱se̱.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: ni nyesā nyekē̱ale̱ ake̱: Na, otšũlo̱ Iakob nyie wo̱se̱. Edšake̱ ekē̱: Mike̱ nikēnii ni nyie mihĩe ne̱ yakpãtãle̱, ni no se̱ dãni mayana ehĩe, ekole̱ eekwe̱ mihĩe.

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Ni ete ši nakai nyō le̱ṅ, ni eṅõ eṅãmei enyo̱ le̱ ke̱ eweyei enyo̱ le̱ ke̱ ebihĩĩ nyoṅma ke̱ ekome le̱ ni eyafo Iabok fōhe le̱;

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Ni ebaſe̱ Iakob kome. Ni nū ko ke̱le̱ banõ ke̱-yaši dšetše̱remo̱. Hos.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ni beni ena ake̱ enyẽẽle̱ le̱, eta egboṅ talo̱ le̱ he, ni Iakob gboṅ talo̱ le̱ hwãṅ beni eke̱le̱ nõo̱ le̱.

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Ni ekē̱: Hã maya, ši dše mītše̱re! Ši ekē̱: *Mihãṅ oya, dša odšō̱mi dã! *Mat.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ni ekē̱ ake̱: *Atšē̱ṅ ogbe̱i Iakob doṅṅ, ši Israel aatšē̱o; edšake̱ oke̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱ gbomei eši mple ni oye kũnim. *

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Ni Iakob wo dše̱i Peniel; edšake̱ ekē̱: *Mina Nyoṅmo̱ hĩe kē̱ hĩe, ši migboo. *

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Ni beni efoṅ ye̱ Peniel le̱, hũlũ dše ye̱ eno̱, ni etšutšo̱ ye̱ egboṅ le̱ mli.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Ni lebi mā̃ṅkpa le̱ Laban te ši ni eſo̱ſō̱ ebihĩĩ ke̱ ebiyei le̱ anã ni edšō̱ame̱. Ni Laban ṅõ-tšere̱ mli ke̱-kũ ese̱ ke̱-te emaṅ.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: ni miná tšinai ke̱ tedšii ke̱ tōi ke̱ tšūdšihĩĩ ke̱ tšũdšiyei; ni mitšũ ni abakē̱ minuṅtšo̱, koni mihĩe aba nyam ye̱ ohĩe.

text: Ni beni efoṅ ye̱ Peniel le̱, hũlũ dše ye̱ eno̱, ni etšutšo̱ ye̱ egboṅ le̱ mli.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Ni lebi mā̃ṅkpa le̱ Laban te ši ni eſo̱ſō̱ ebihĩĩ ke̱ ebiyei le̱ anã ni edšō̱ame̱. Ni Laban ṅõ-tšere̱ mli ke̱-kũ ese̱ ke̱-te emaṅ.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Ni Iakob tšũ bo̱foi ke̱-tšṍ ehĩe ke̱-te enyemi Esau ṅõ ye̱ *Seir šikpoṅ ni dši Edom ṅã le̱ no̱. *

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Ni ewoame̱ kitã ake̱: Nyeyakē̱a minuṅtšo̱ Esau akē̱: Bo̱ni otšũlo̱ Iakob kē̱ ne̱: Miyato gbo̱ ye̱ Laban ṅõ, ni mitše̱ ye̱ dše̱i āhũ ke̱-baši bene̱,

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: ni miná tšinai ke̱ tedšii ke̱ tōi ke̱ tšūdšihĩĩ ke̱ tšũdšiyei; ni mitšũ ni abakē̱ minuṅtšo̱, koni mihĩe aba nyam ye̱ ohĩe.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: ni ekē̱: Ke̱dši Esau ba asafoku kome ne̱ no̱ ni ebatſiaame̱ nii le̱, koni ku kome ní eſe̱ le̱ aná gbe̱ adšo foi.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: omo̱bo̱nale̱i ke̱ onokwayeli fẽ ni odšie otšõ otšũlo̱ le̱, ekomeflo ne̱ke̱ *esaami; ši tšo mihĩe ke̱-fo Iordan ne̱, ni agbe̱ne̱ mitšṍ asafokui enyo. *

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ni Iakob bile̱ ake̱: Mīkpao fai ni *otšõmi ogbe̱i! Ni ekē̱: Meni hewo̱ ni obio̱ migbe̱i ne̱? Ni edšō̱le̱ ye̱ dše̱i. *Kodš.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: no le̱ okē̱ ake̱: Otšũlo̱ Iakob nikẽnii ní etšũ ake̱ ake̱-bake minuṅtšo̱ Esau ni; ni na, le̱ hũ enyie wo̱se̱.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: ni nyesā nyekē̱ale̱ ake̱: Na, otšũlo̱ Iakob nyie wo̱se̱. Edšake̱ ekē̱: Mike̱ nikēnii ni nyie mihĩe ne̱ yakpãtãle̱, ni no se̱ dãni mayana ehĩe, ekole̱ eekwe̱ mihĩe.

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Ni ete ši nakai nyō le̱ṅ, ni eṅõ eṅãmei enyo̱ le̱ ke̱ eweyei enyo̱ le̱ ke̱ ebihĩĩ nyoṅma ke̱ ekome le̱ ni eyafo Iabok fōhe le̱;

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Ni ebaſe̱ Iakob kome. Ni nū ko ke̱le̱ banõ ke̱-yaši dšetše̱remo̱. Hos.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ni beni ena ake̱ enyẽẽle̱ le̱, eta egboṅ talo̱ le̱ he, ni Iakob gboṅ talo̱ le̱ hwãṅ beni eke̱le̱ nõo̱ le̱.

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Ni ekē̱: Hã maya, ši dše mītše̱re! Ši ekē̱: *Mihãṅ oya, dša odšō̱mi dã! *Mat.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ni ekē̱ ake̱: *Atšē̱ṅ ogbe̱i Iakob doṅṅ, ši Israel aatšē̱o; edšake̱ oke̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱ gbomei eši mple ni oye kũnim. *

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Ni Iakob wo dše̱i Peniel; edšake̱ ekē̱: *Mina Nyoṅmo̱ hĩe kē̱ hĩe, ši migboo. *

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Ni beni efoṅ ye̱ Peniel le̱, hũlũ dše ye̱ eno̱, ni etšutšo̱ ye̱ egboṅ le̱ mli.

ref: But Esau ran toward Jacob and hugged and kissed him. Then the two brothers started crying.
text: Ni Esau ša foi eke̱le̱ bakpe, ni elōle̱ atū, ni eke̱ eyitšo fõ ekue̱ no̱, ni eſo̱ enā, ni ame̱fó.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Ni ehole ehīṅme̱ii ano̱, ni ena yei le̱ ke̱ gbekẽbii le̱, ni ekē̱: Namei dši mei ní fata ohe ne̱? Ni ekē̱: Bii *ní Nyoṅmo̱ edro otšũlo̱ le̱ ni. *Lal.

ref: Then the two servant women and their children came and bowed down to Esau.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni weyei le̱ tši ba, ame̱ke̱ ame̱bii le̱, ni ame̱bakoto ši.

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Ni Esau kē̱: Bo̱ni miyo̱ le̱ fá, minyemi, hã onõ le̱ ahĩ ši ahão!

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Ni Iakob kē̱: Dabida, mĩkpao fai, ni ke̱dši mihĩe eba nyam ye̱ ohĩe le̱, no le̱ hemo̱ mideṅ nikēnii le̱; ši ni mina ohĩe ne̱, tamo̱ noni mina *Nyoṅmo̱ hĩe, ni oná mihe tšui hũ. *

ref: Please accept these as gifts I brought to you. God has been good to me, and I have everything I need.” Jacob kept insisting until Esau agreed.
text: Mīkpao fai ni ohe *minikẽnii ni ake̱-bahão ne̱; edšake̱ Nyoṅmo̱ edromi, ni misã miye̱ nofēno hũ. Ni ehĩele̱ āhũ, ni ehe. *

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Minuṅtšo̱ le ake̱ gbekẽbii le̱ ahĩe esako, ni tōi ke̱ tšinai ni bãa fufo̱ fata mihe; ni ke̱dši atšire̱ ame̱yise̱ gbĩ kome dšenamo̱ le̱, koloi le̱ fẽ aabagboi.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ hã ni minuṅtšo̱ atšṍ etšũlo̱ hĩe, ni mi le̱ manyie ble̱ōble̱ō, bo̱ni koloi le̱ ni nyie mihĩe le̱ ke̱ gbekẽbii le̱ aanyẽ, ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ní maba minuṅtšo̱ ṅõ ye̱ Seir.

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Ni Esau kē̱: Hã mašĩ mei ni ke̱mi nyie lē̱ eko mato ohe. Ni ekē̱: Mēba ehia? Hã ni mihĩe aba nyam ye̱ minuṅtšo̱ hĩe ke̱ke̱!

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Ni Iakob ṅõ-ſie no̱ ke̱-te Sukot, ni eyamã etšũ ye̱ dše̱i, ni efe asesei ehã ekoloi le̱; nohewo̱ le̱ awo dše̱i Sukot.

ref: After leaving northern Syria, Jacob arrived safely at Shechem in Canaan and set up camp outside the city.
text: Ni Iakob še̱ šekem maṅ le̱ ni yo̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱ ſeſēſe, beni edše̱ Mesopotamia ebaä le̱, ni ebašišĩ ebūi ye̱ maṅ le̱ hĩe.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Ni ebā dſie̱te̱i kukudši oba *eke̱he šikpoṅ le̱ ni eši ebū le̱ ye̱ no̱ le̱, ye̱ Šekem tše̱ Hamor bii le̱ adeṅ. *Ios.

ref: then he set up his tents and built an altar there to honor the God of Israel.
text: Ni emã afo̱lešãlate̱ ye̱ dše̱i, ni ewole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Israel Nyoṅmo̱ Mawu le nõ.

ref: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, went to visit some of the women who lived nearby.
text: Ni Dina, Lea hiyõ le̱ *ní eke̱ Iakob fo̱ le̱, dše kpo ete šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ biyei le̱ akwe̱mo̱. *

ref: But Shechem was attracted to Dinah, so he told her how much he loved her.
text: Ni etšui mõ Iakob biyō Dina le̱, ni ešumo̱ oblayō le̱ sane, ni ehã oblayō le̱ tšui.

ref: Shechem even asked his father to arrange for him to marry her.
text: Ni Šekem kē̱ etše̱ Hamor ake̱: Yaw̌e̱ ne̱ke̱ oblayō ne̱ ohãmi.

ref: Hamor said to Jacob and his sons: My son Shechem really loves Dinah. Please let him marry her.
text: Ni Hamor ke̱ame̱ wie ekē̱: Mibi Šekem tšui emõ nyebiyō le̱; mĩkpanye fai ni nyeṅõle̱ nyehãle̱ gblã.

ref: Why don't you start letting your families marry into our families and ours marry into yours?
text: Nyeke̱wo̱ ayea yōw̌ē̱; nyeṅōa nyebiyei nyehãawo̱ gblã, ni nye hũ nyew̌e̱ wo̱biyei,

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: ni nyeke̱wo̱ ahĩa ši. Šikpoṅ le̱ nā kã hãnye; nyehĩa no̱, ni nyetšu no̱ nii, ni nyenáa nii ye̱ no̱.

ref: Shechem added, “Do this favor for me, and I'll give whatever you want.
text: Ni Šekem kē̱ etše̱ ke̱ enyemimei le̱ ake̱: Nyehãa mihĩe abaä nyam ye̱ nyebleaṅ, ši nofẽno ni nyeabimi le̱, mahã.

ref: Ask anything, no matter how expensive. I'll do anything, just let me marry Dinah.”
text: Nyebiami gblãnii ke̱ kẽnii bo̱fẽbo̱, ni bo̱ni nyeabimi le̱ mahãnye, koni nyeṅõ oblayō le̱ nye̱bãmi.

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Ni Iakob bii le̱ wie ni ame̱ke̱ hĩelakamo̱ hã Šekem ke̱ etše̱ Hamor hetō, edšake̱ ebule ame̱nyemiyō Dina le̱,

ref: So they tricked them by saying: You're not circumcised! It would be a disgrace for us to let you marry Dinah now.
text: ni ame̱kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Wo̱nyẽṅ ne̱ke̱ sane wo̱ye, ni wo̱ṅõ wo̱nyemiyõ le̱ wo̱hã moko ní dši fōlo̱, edšake̱ no le̱ wo̱to kpa.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Ši gbe̱ no̱ ni wo̱o̱kplẽnye ke̱ke̱ ne̱: ke̱dši nyeatšṍmo̱ tamo̱wo̱, ni aaflo̱ nyeteṅ hĩĩ fẽ ketia le̱.

ref: Then your families can marry into ours, and ours can marry into yours, and we can live together like one nation.
text: No le̱ wo̱o̱ṅõ wo̱biyei wo̱hãnye gblã, ni wo̱ hũ wo̱o̱ṅõ nyebiyei, ni wo̱ke̱nye aabī ši, ni woo̱tšṍ maṅ kome.

ref: But if you don't agree to be circumcised, we'll take Dinah and leave this place.
text: Ši ke̱dši nyeboowo̱ toi ní nyehã aflo̱nye ketia le̱, no le̱ wo̱o̱ṅõ wo̱biyō le̱ ní wo̱ya

ref: Hamor and Shechem liked what was said.
text: Ni ame̱wiemo̱i le̱ sa Hamor ke̱ Hamor bi Šekem hĩe.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Ni oblanyo le̱ eṅõõ nakai nii le̱ femo̱ le̱ ešašaoo ši, edšake̱ eye̱ Iakob biyō le̱ be tšui. Ni abuo̱le̱ fè etše̱ we le̱ bii fẽ.

ref: The two men met with the other leaders of their city and told them:
text: Ni Hamor ke̱ebi Šekem le̱ ba ame̱mā le̱ṅ agbó le̱ nā, ni ame̱ke̱ ame̱mã le̱ṅ hĩĩ le̱ wie ní ame̱kē̱:

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Ši kē̱ aflo̱ wo̱teṅ hĩĩ fẽ ketia, tamo̱ bo̱ni aflo̱ame̱ le̱, dša no, dãni gbomei le̱ aakplẽ wo̱ke̱ šihĩle̱, ni ame̱ke̱wo̱ aafe maṅ kome.

ref: Just think! We'll get their property, as well as their flocks and herds. All we have to do is to agree, and they will live here with us.
text: Ame̱kolokui ke̱ ame̱nii ní ame̱yo̱ ke̱ ame̱šĩa koloi fẽ le̱, dšee wo̱nii ebatšṍmo̱ṅ? Nyehãa wo̱kplẽa wo̱hãa-ame̱ ke̱ke̱, ni ame̱ke̱wo̱ ahī ši.

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ gbĩ ní dši gbĩ etẽ le̱, beni ame̱fadši le̱ ewo wu le̱, Iakob bihĩĩ enyo̱ *ni dši Simeon ke̱ Levi, Dina nyemimei le̱, kŏ̱lo̱ ame̱klantei ni amebatua maṅ le trukã, ni amegbe hĩĩ le̱ fē; *

ref: including Hamor and Shechem. Then they took Dinah and left.
text: ni ame̱gbe Hamor ke̱ ebi Šekem hũ ye̱ klante nā, ni ame̱yaṅõ Dina ye̱ Šekem we le̱, ni ame̱yiṅ ame̱te.

ref: Jacob's other sons came and took everything they wanted. All this was done because of the horrible thing that had happened to their sister.
text: Ni Iakob bihĩĩ le̱ banina mei ni enyõnyõi Ie̱, ni ame̱ha maṅ le̱, edšake̱ ame̱bule ame̱nyemiyō le̱.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Ame̱tōi ke̱ ame̱tšinai ke̱ ame̱tedšii ke̱ nii ní yo̱ mã le̱ṅ ke̱ nii ni yo̱ ṅãno̱ le̱, ome̱lō ke̱-te.

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.
text: Ni ame̱ṅõ ame̱henii fẽ ke̱ ame̱bii bibii fẽ ke̱ ame̱ṅãmei nom, ni ame̱ha ame̱we nibii fẽ.

ref: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “Look what you've done! Now I'm in real trouble with the Canaanites and Perizzites who live around here. There aren't many of us, and if they attack, they'll kill everyone in my household.”
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ Simeon kē̱ Levi ake̱: Nyewomi amane mli, ni nyebã mihe *edše fũ ewo šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ bii ní dši Kanaanbii ke̱ Perizbii le̱; ni mi hũ ni miyikule̱ dši fiõ ne̱, ame̱abua ame̱he nä amebatuami ni ame̱agbemi; ni aakpãtã mihĩe, mike̱ miwe le̱. *

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Ni ame̱ke̱: Aso edša ake̱ afe wo̱nyemiyō le̱ tamo̱ afeo̱ adſamaṅ ko?

ref: Jacob said to his family and to everyone else who was traveling with him: Get rid of your foreign gods! Then make yourselves acceptable to worship God and put on clean clothes.
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ ewe lē̱ ke̱ mei fẽ ni ke̱le̱ yo̱ le̱ ake̱: *Nyedšiea maṅse̱ nyoṅmo̱i ni yo̱ nyeteṅ le̱ nyeſiea, ni nyetšūa nyehe, ni nyetšakea nyetadei. *

ref: Jacob built an altar there and called it “God of Bethel,” because that was the place where God had appeared to him when he was running from Esau.
text: Ni emã *afo̱lešãlate̱ ye̱ dše̱i, ni etšē̱ dše̱i El-Betel, edšake̱ dše̱i Nyoṅmo̱ dšie ebe ši etšõle̱ ye̱, beni edšo foi ye̱ enyemi le̱ hĩe le̱. *

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: ni šikpoṅ le̱ ni miṅõ-hã Abraham ke̱ Isak le̱, bo maṅõ-hã, ni oseši ni aaba ye̱ ose̱ le̱ maṅõ šikpoṅ ne̱ mahã.

ref: Jacob set up a large rock, so that he would remember what had happened there. Then he poured wine and olive oil on the rock to show that it was dedicated to God,
text: Ni Iakob ṅõ te̱ eto okadi ye̱ heni eke̱le̱ wie ye̱ le̱, ni efo̱se wein ke̱ mũ eſie no. *

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: Ni Iakob wo heni Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱le̱ wie ye̱ le̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Betel.

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Ni ame̱fã ke̱-dše̱ Betel, ni eſe̱ gbe̱ kukuō ko ni ame̱še̱o̱ Efrat, ke̱ke̱ ni Rahel bõi kõmo̱, ni efo̱mo̱ le̱ he yawa hãle̱.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni efo̱mo̱ le̱ mli wa le̱, yōfo̱yō le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Kāše gbeyei, ši obafo̱ nū ekoṅ.

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni esusnma beo̱ emli adše ni ebu enā le̱, ewole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Benoni; ši etše̱ wole̱ Benyamin.

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Ni Rahel gbo, ni afùle̱ ye̱ Efrat ni dši Betlehem le̱ gbe̱ le̱ mase̱i. Mika

ref: Jacob set up a tombstone over her grave, and it is still there.
text: Ni Iakob ke̱ te̱ to okadi ye̱ esãṅ le̱ no̱; no dši Rahel sãṅ no̱ te̱ ke̱-baši ṅme̱ne̱.

ref: Jacob, also known as Israel, traveled to the south of Eder Tower, where he set up camp.
text: Ni Israel ṅõ-ſie no̱ ni eyaši ebū le̱ ye̱ *Eder mõ le̱ se̱ghe̱. *Mika

ref: During their time there, Jacob's oldest son Reuben slept with Bilhah, who was one of Jacob's other wives. And Jacob found out about it. Jacob's Twelve Sons
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, heni Israel yo̱ nakai šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱, *Ruben yiṅ ete ni eke̱ etše̱ ṅã Bilha le̱ yawo̱; ni Israel nu. Agbe̱ne̱ Iakob bihĩĩ le̱, ame̱yi nyoṅma ke̱ enyo̱: *

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Ni Iakob ba etše̱ Isak ṅõ ye̱ Mamre, ye̱ Kiriat-Arba ni dši Hebron le̱, heni Abraham ke̱ Isak hato gbo̱ ye̱ le̱.

ref: Esau, also known as Edom, had many descendants.
text: Agbe̱ne̱ Esau *moni atšé̱o̱le̱ Edom le̱ sešibii ne̱: *

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: ke̱ Išmael biyō Basemat ni dši Nebaiot nyemiyō le̱.

ref: He did this because the land was too crowded and could not support him and his brother with their flocks and herds.
text: Edšake̱ ame̱nii ni ame̱ná le̱ fá tšõ, bo̱ni ame̱anyẽ ši ame̱hĩ ye̱ hekome, ni ame̱lē̱koloi le̱ ahewo̱ le̱ ame̱boo šikpoṅ ni ame̱bato gbo̱ ye̱ no̱ le̱ no̱.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Ki Esau yahĩ Seir goṅ le̱ no̱. Esau noṅṅ dši Edom le̱.

ref: Esau and Adah's oldest son was Eliphaz, and the clans that descended from him were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz,
text: Ni Esau bii le̱ ateṅ lume̱i ne̱: Esau kromo̱bi Elifaz bii dši: Lumo Teman, lumo̱ Omar, lumo̱ Zefo, lumo̱ Kenaz,

ref: Korah, Gatam, and Amalek. These and Esau's other descendants lived in the land of Edom.
text: lumo̱ Kora, lumo̱ Gatam, lumo̱ Amalek. Mene̱mei dši Elifaz lume̱i ye̱ Edom šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni ame̱dši Ada bii.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Basemath's son Reuel were Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
text: Ni Esau bi Reuel le̱ bii ne̱: Lnmo Nahat, lumo̱ Zera, lumo̱ Šama, lumo̱ Miza. Mene̱mei dši Reuel lume̱i ye̱ Edom šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni ame̱dši Esau ṅã Basemat bii.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Ni Esau ṅã Obolibama hii ne̱: Lumo Ieuš, lumo̱ Ialam, lumo̱ Kora. Mene̱mei dši Ana biyō Oholibama ni dši Esau ṅã le̱ lume̱i le̱.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Mene̱mei dši Esau bii ni mene̱mei dši ame̱lumei. Le̱ noṅṅ dši Edom le̱.

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: *Hornyo Seir bii ní yo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ne̱: Lotan ke̱ Šobal ke̱ Zibeon ke̱ Ana, *

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Ni Lotan bii dši Hori ke̱ Hemam; ni Lotan nyemiyō dši Timna.

ref: Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
text: Ni Šobal bii ne̱: Alvan ke̱ Manahat ke̱ Ebal, Šefo ke̱ Onam.

ref: Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah—the same Anah who found an oasis in the desert while taking the donkeys of his father out to pasture.
text: Ni Zibeon bii ne̱: Aia ke̱ Ana. Mone̱ dši Ana le̱ ni yana nula-nubui ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱, beni elè̱o̱ etše̱ Zibeon tedšii le̱.

ref: Anah's children were Dishon and Oholibamah.
text: Ni Ana bii ne̱: Dišon ke̱ Ana biyō Oholibama.

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Ni Dišon bii ne̱: Hemdan ke̱ Ešban ke̱ Itran ke̱ Keran.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Ezer bii ne̱: Bilhan ke̱ Zaavan ke̱ Akan.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Ni Dišan bii ne̱: Uz ke̱ Aran.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Horbii alumei ne̱: Lumo Lotan, lumo̱ Šobal, lumo̱ Zibeon, lumo̱ Ana,

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: lumo̱ Dišon, lumo̱ Ezer, lumo̱ Dišan. Mene̱mei dši Horbii le̱ alumei ni ye lume̱i ye̱ Seir šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau took their names from their families and the places where they lived. They are Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
text: Ni Esau lume̱i ye̱ ame̱wekui ke̱ ame̱dšé̱hei ke̱ ame̱gbe̱ii anā le̱ agbe̱ii ne̱: Lumo Timna, lumo̱ Alva, lumo̱ Ietet,

ref: Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
text: lumo̱ Oholibama, lumo̱ Ela, lumo̱ Pinon,

ref: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
text: lumo̱ Kenaz, lumo̱ Teman, lumo̱ Mibzar,

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: lumo̱ Magdiel, lumo̱ Iram. Mene̱mei dši Edom lume̱i le̱ ye̱ ame̱šihĩle̱hei le̱ anà ye̱ šikpoṅ ni ame̱ná le̱ no̱. Le̱ dši Esau ni dši Edombii atše̱ le̱.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: Ni nū le̱ kē̱: Ame̱dše biē̱; ši minu ake̱ ame̱kē̱: Nyehãa wo̱yaä Dotan. Ni Iosef tiu enyemimei le̱, ni eyanaame̱ ye̱ Dotan.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: Ni beni enyemimei le̱ na ake̱ le̱ ame̱tše̱ sumoo̱ fẽ enyemimei le̱ fẽ le̱, ame̱nye̱le̱, ni ame̱nyẽẽ ame̱ke̱le̱ awie ye̱ hedšo̱le̱ mli.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Ni Iosef la lamõ ko, ni edšadše etšõ enyemimei le̱, ni ame̱sā ame̱nye̱le̱ fè tšutšu le̱.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: Ši na, wo̱mĩfĩmo̱ ṅmā̃kei ye̱ ṅmõši, ni na, mike le̱ te ši damo̱ ši, ni na, nyekei le̱ babo̱le mike le̱ ni ame̱kotole̱.

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Ni enyemimei le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Aso maṅtše̱ po okē̱ ooye ye̱ wo̱no̱ ne̱? alo ye obaye wo̱no̱ ne̱? Ni ame̱nye̱le̱ fè tšutšu le̱ ye̱ elamõi le̱ ke̱ ewiemo̱i le̱ ahewo̱.

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Ni esā ela lamõ kroko hũ, ni egba enyemimei le̱, ni ekē̱: Na, mila lamõ ko ekoṅ, ni na, hũlũ ke̱ nyoṅtšẽre ke̱ ṅulamii myoṅma ke̱ ekome hakotomi.

ref: When he told his father about this dream, his father became angry and said, “What's that supposed to mean? Are your mother and I and your brothers all going to come and bow down to you?”
text: Ni beni egba etše̱ ke̱ enyemimei le̱, etše̱ kã ehĩe, ni eké̱le̱ ake̱: Mẽ lamõ ola ne̱? Bĕle̱ kotomo̱ mike̱ onye ke̱ enyemimei le̱ aabakotoö ye̱ šikpoṅ?

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Ni enyemimei le̱ yiṅ ame̱te ame̱tše̱ tōi le̱ lè̱ *ye̱ Šekem. *

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Ya ni oyakwe̱ ake̱ onyemimei le̱ ke̱ tōi le̱ ye̱ dšogbāṅ lo, ni obakē̱mi. Ke̱ke̱ ni etšũle̱ ke̱-dše̱ *Hebron dšō̱ le̱ mli, ni eba Šekem. *

ref: and wandering through the fields, when a man asked, “What are you looking for?”
text: Ni nū ko nale̱, ni na, ētšomlo ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱; ni nū le̱ bile̱ ake̱: Meni otaoo̱?

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Ni ekē̱: Mītao minyemimei lē̱; mīkpao fai, tšõmo̱mi heni ame̱lè̱o̱ ame̱tōi le̱ ye̱.

ref: But before he got there, they saw him coming and made plans to kill him.
text: Ni beni ame̱nale̱ ye̱ šoṅṅ ní enáko ebe̱ṅke̱ame̱ le̱, ame̱kpãṅ eyi no̱ ake̱ ame̱agbele̱.

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Ni ame̱kē̱kē̱ ame̱be ake̱: Na, lamõi atše̱ le̱ baä.

ref: pieces of silver they sold him to the Ishmaelites who took him to Egypt.
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ nyebaä ni wo̱gbeale̱, ni wo̱še̱ale̱ we̱fõa bui le̱ eko mli, ni wo̱kē̱: Kolo fõṅ ko eyele̱; ni wo̱kwe̱ bo̱ni elamõi le̱ aate ahã.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Ni Ruben nu, *ni ẽtao ni edšiele̱ ye̱ amedeṅ, ni ekẽ: Nyekahãa wogbeale! *

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Ni Ruben sā kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekaſiea lá ši, ši nyeṅõale̱ nyeyawoa bu ni yo̱ ṅã le̱ no̱ ne̱ mli, ni nyekataä ehe! beni afe ni edšiele̱ ye̱ ame̱deṅ, koni eke̱le̱ ayahã etše̱ ekoṅ.

ref: When Joseph came to his brothers, they pulled off his fancy coat
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni Iosef še̱ enyemimei le̱ aṅõ le̱, ame̱kplo̱ etade ni dši atade wuyã fe̱fe̱o ni ewo le̱ ye̱ ehe, K.

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Ni ame̱ho ame̱trá ši ni ame̱baye nii. Ni ame̱hole ame̱hĩṅme̱ii ano̱ ame̱kwe̱ ni na, Išmaelbii gbe̱nyielo̱i asafo ko dše̱ Gilead miba ke̱ ame̱yomai ni tĕre tšofãi-ke̱-ṅmai ke̱ gbonyowō tšofã ke̱ ohe̱ ni dšeo̱ ṅma ke̱-mĩya Mizraim.

ref: Let's sell him to the Ishmaelites and not harm him. After all, he is our brother.” And the others agreed.
text: Nyebaä ni nyehãa wo̱hõale̱ wo̱hãa Išmaelbii le̱, ši nyekahāa wo̱ke̱ wo̱niné mïsa ehe; edšake̱ wo̱nyemi ke̱ wo̱belo dšilo̱. Ni enyemimei le̱ kplẽ.

ref: When the Midianite merchants came by, Joseph's brothers took him out of the well, and for
text: Ni *Midianbii ni dši guo̱yelo̱i le̱ baho; ni ame̱yagbla Iosef ni ame̱dšiele̱ ye̱ bu le̱ mli, ni ame̱yahõ Iosef ame̱hã Išmaelbii le̱ ame̱he dſie̱te̱i kukudši nyoṅmai-enyo. Ni ameke Iosef te Mizraim. *

ref: Joseph's brothers killed a goat and dipped Joseph's fancy coat in its blood.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni ame̱ko̱ Iosef atade le̱, ni ame̱gbe tõkpakpo ko, ni ame̱ṅõ atade le̱ ame̱tšo̱ lá le̱ mli,

ref: After this, they took the coat to their father and said, “We found this! Look at it carefully and see if it belongs to your son.”
text: ni ame̱tšũ ake̱ atade wuyā fe̱fe̱o le̱ yahã ame̱tše̱ akē̱: Noni wo̱na ne̱; kwe̱mo̱ ake̱ obi le̱ atade le̱ ni lo dšee noṅ,

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Ni eyo, ni ekē̱: Mibi le̱ atade le̱ ni! *kolo fõṅ ko eyele̱, tšere̱mo̱ etšere̱ Iosef mli ne̱! *K.

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Ni Iakob *gbla ehe atade le̱, hi eke̱ kpekpe emĩa ehe̱, ni eye ebi le̱ he ṅko̱mo̱ gbĩĩ pī. *K.

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: Ni ebihĩĩ le̱ ke̱ ebiyei le̱ fẽ te ši, koni ame̱baše̱dše emī, ši ekplẽẽ mīše̱dšemo̱, ni ekē̱: Nko̱mo̱yeli mike-akpleke ši ke̱yanina mibi le ye gbohīĩadše. Ni etše̱ fóle̱.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ni Midianbii le̱ yahõle̱ ye̱ Mizraim ame̱hã Potifar ni dši Farao piafonyo ke̱ eblafoi atše̱ le̱.

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Ni Iuda na Kanaannyo ni atšé̱o̱le̱ Šua le̱ biyō ko ye̱ dše̱i; ni eṅōle̱, ni ebawo̱ eṅõ.

ref: she named the next one Onan.
text: Ni ená mũsũ ekoṅ, ni efo̱ binū, ni ewole̱ Onan.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Ni Iuda w̌e̱ yō ko ni atšé̱o̱le̱ Tamar ehã ekromo̱bi Er le̱.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Ni Iuda kromo̱bi Er fe ehe gbomo fõṅ ye̱ Iehowa hĩe, ni Iehowa gbele̱.

ref: So Judah told Onan, “It's your duty to marry Tamar and have a child for your brother.”
text: Ni Iuda ké̱ Onan ake̱: Ya onyemi le̱ ṅã le̱ ṅõ, ni oṅõle̱ ake̱ gbošiṅã, koni oke̱le̱ afo̱ ato onyemi le̱ se̱.

ref: Onan knew the child would not be his, and when he had sex with Tamar, he made sure that she would not get pregnant.
text: Ši Onan le ake̱ bii le̱ ebatšṍṅ enõ, ṅi eba mli ake̱, ké̱ ete enyemi le̱ ṅã le̱ ṅõ le̱, eṅõ-ſieo̱ ši ye̱ šikpoṅ efiteo̱, koni ekahã enyemi le̱ mīná sešibii.

ref: The Lord wasn't pleased with Onan and took his life too.
text: Ni noni efe le̱, efõṅ dši ye̱ Iehowa hĩe; nohewo̱ le̱ egbele̱ hũ.

ref: Judah did not want the same thing to happen to his son Shelah, and he told Tamar, “Go home to your father and live there as a widow until my son Shelah is grown.” So Tamar went to live with her father.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iuda kē̱ ešāyō Tamar ake̱: Yahĩ ši ake̱ okũlafo ye̱ otše̱ we le̱ ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni mibi Šela lē̱ aada. Edšake̱ ekē̱: Koni le̱ hũ ekabagbo tamo̱ enyemimei le̱. Ni Tamar te eyahĩ etše̱ we le̱.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Ni ye̱ mli āhũ le̱, Šua biyō le̱ ni dši Iuda ṅã le̱ bagbo. Ni beni Iuda tšé̱ etšui eṅmẽ emli le̱, eke̱ enanyo Hira, Adulamnyo le̱, yiṅ te etōiahetšo̱i kulo̱i le̱ aṅõ ye̱ Timna.

ref: Tamar found out that her father-in-law Judah was going to Timnah to shear his sheep.
text: Ni ayakē̱ Tamar akē̱: Na, ošānũ le̱ miya Timna ní eyaku etōi le̱ ahetšo̱i.

ref: She also realized that Shelah was now a grown man, but she had not been allowed to marry him. So she decided to dress in something other than her widow's clothes and to cover her face with a veil. After this, she sat outside the town of Enaim on the road to Timnah.
text: Ni edšie ekũlafemo̱ atade le̱ ye̱ ehe efõ ši, ni eṅõ hĩehānõ eha ehĩe, ni eṅõbla ehe, ni eyatá ši ye̱ Enaim klotia ye̱ Timna gbe̱ le̱ toi; edšake̱ ena ake̱ Šela eda ni aṅõõle̱ ahããle̱ gblã.

ref: When Judah came along, he did not recognize her because of the veil. He thought she was a prostitute
text: Ni beni Iuda nale̱ le̱, ebule̱ ake̱ yō adſamaṅ ko; edšake̱ eha ehĩe.

ref: and asked her to sleep with him. She asked, “What will you give me if I do?”
text: Ni egba ke̱-te eṅõ ye̱ gbe̱ le̱ toi, ni ekē̱: Kwe̱, hã ni maba oṅõ; edšake̱ *elee ake̱ ešāyõ le̱ ni. Ni ekē̱: Meni oohãmi, koni oba miṅõ? *

ref: “One of my young goats,” he answered. “What will you give me to keep until you send the goat?” she asked.
text: Ni ekē̱: Mahã amõ tõkpakpobi ko ye̱ tōi le̱ ateṅ abahão. Ni ekē̱: Ké̱ ootomi awaba ko ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni oohã ake̱-ba le̱.

ref: “What do you want?” he asked in return. “The ring on that cord around your neck,” was her reply. “I also want the special walking stick you have with you.” He gave them to her, they slept together, and she became pregnant.
text: Ni ekē̱: Mẽ awaba matoõ? Ni ekē̱: Owolonāso̱omo̱nõ ke̱ ehe kpã ke̱ odẽhĩemo̱tšo le̱. Ni eṅõ-hãle̱, ní eba eṅõ, ni eke̱le̱ ná mũsũ.

ref: After returning home, Tamar took off the veil and dressed in her widow's clothes again.
text: Ni ete ši ete, ni eyadšie ehĩehānõ le̱ efõ ši, ni eko̱ ekūlafemo̱ atade le̱ ewo.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Ni Iuda ṅõ tōkpakpobi le̱ ehã enanyo Adulamnyo le̱ ke̱-te, koni eyahe ewabatōnii le ye̱ yō le̱ deṅ; ši enaa yō le̱.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni eli dše̱ibii ke̱ ake̱: Nẽgbe̱ yō adſamaṅ ní tá Enaim gbe̱ le̱ toi le̱ yo̱? Ni ame̱ke: Yō adſamaṅ ko be̱ bie̱ ne̱.

ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Ni eku ese̱ eba Iuda nõ ekoṅ, ni ekē̱: Minaale̱; ni asaṅ dše̱ibii le kē̱: Yō adſamaṅ ko be̱ bie̱ ne̱.

ref: “If you couldn't find her, we'll just let her keep the things I gave her,” Judah answered. “And we'd better forget about the goat, or else we'll look like fools.”
text: Ni Iuda kē̱: Hã ni eke̱-ya, koni wo̱hĩe akabagboi; na, mihã oke̱ tōkpakpobi ne̱ te, ni onaale̱.

ref: About three months later someone told Judah, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar has behaved like a prostitute, and now she's pregnant!” “Drag her out of town and burn her to death!” Judah shouted.
text: Ni no se̱ aafe nyõdši etẽ le̱ eba mli ake̱ adekē̱ Iuda eke̱: Ošāyō Tamar eyabo̱ adſamaṅ, ni asaṅ na, ená mũsũ ye̱ adſamaṅbō̱ le mli. Ni Iuda kē̱: Ake̱le̱ adše kpo ke-ba, koni abašãle̱.

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Ni beni ake̱le̱ dše kpo ba le̱, etšũ ate ešānū le ṅõ ayakē̱le̱ ake̱: Nū mo nõ ní dši nii ne̱ le, le̱ mike̱-nà mũsũ le̱. Ni esā ekē̱: Kwe̱mo̱ ake̱e ooyo mo nõ ni dši wolonāso̱omo̱nõ ne̱ ke̱ ehe kpã ne̱ ke̱ tšo ne̱ lo?

ref: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
text: Ni Iuda yo ni ekē̱: Dšalo̱ dšile̱ fèmi, ake̱ noni miṅõõle̱ midãã mibi Šela le̱. Ni ehi esaa ebe doṅṅ.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni efo̱ö̱ le̱, mokome dšie enine. Ni yōfo̱yō lo mō mli, ni eke̱ kpã tšuru fĩ enine le̱, ni ekē̱: Mone̱ tšutšu ba.

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni esu enine le̱ ekoṅ le̱, na, enyemi le̱ ba; ni ekē̱: Mẽba otšere̱ mll ke̱-ba ne̱? Ni awole̱ Perez. Mat.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Ni no se̱ le̱ enyemi le ni kpã tšuru le yo̱ enineši le hũ ba; ni awole̱ Zera.

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ni ake̱ Iosef *te Mizraim, ni Farao piafonyo ke̱ eblafoi atše̱ Potifar ní dši Mizraimnyo le̱ hele̱ ye̱ Išmaelbii le̱ ní ke̱le̱ ba dše̱i le̱ adeṅ. *

ref: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
text: Ni Iosef hĩe de nyam ye̱ ehĩe, ni esúmoo̱le̱, ni ewole̱ onukpa ye̱ ewe le̱ no̱, ni nofẽ̱no ní eyo̱ le̱ etu ewo edeṅ.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ etu enibii fẽ ní eyo̱ le̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni ekwe̱e̱ enokonoko ake̱ dša nii ní eyeo̱ ke̱ke̱. Ni Iosef he ye̱ fe̱o ni ehĩe hũ ye̱ fe̱o.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: Ni nii ne̱ ase̱ le̱ eba mli ake̱ enuṅtšo̱ ṅã le̱ dšie ehĩe eka Iosef ekē̱: Oke̱mi abawo̱! Abe̱i

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Ši ekplẽẽ, ni ekē̱ enuṅtšo̱ ṅã le̱ ake̱: Na, minuṅtšo̱ kwe̱e̱ noni yo̱ mideṅ ye̱ šĩa ne̱, ni etu nii ni eyo̱ fẽ ewo mideṅ;

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: mokomoko be̱ ni fèmi ye̱ šĩa ne̱, ni ekãmããmi nokonoko, ake̱ dša bo ke̱ke̱, edšake̱ bo le̱ eṅã dšio. Té aafe tẽṅ ni mafe efõṅ kpeteṅkple ni tamo̱ ne̱ke̱ ne̱, ni mafe eša maši Nyoṅmo̱ṅ?

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ ehĩe Iosef ke̱ wiemo̱ dāne̱, ši eboole̱ toi nó eke̱le̱ awo̱ lo ebe̱ṅke̱le̱.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Ni eba mli gbĩ le̱ eko ake̱ Iosef te šĩa le̱ eyatšu enii, ni šĩa le̱ bii le̱ eko-eko be̱ dše̱i.

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: Ni emõ etade le̱ mli, ni ekē̱: Oke̱mi abawo̱! Ši ešĩ etade le̱ ye̱ edeṅ, ni edšo foi ke̱-dše kpo.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: etšé̱ ešiabii le̱, ni ekē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyebakwe̱a noko, eyaṅõ Hebrinyo ne̱ ke̱-ba wo̱ṅõ, koni ebaye wo̱he fe̱o. Ebe miṅõ, ni eke̱mi abawo̱, ni mibo ke̱ gbē wā;

ref: And when he heard me scream, he ran out of the house, leaving his coat with me.”
text: ni eba mli ake̱, beni enu ake̱ miwó migbē no̱ ni mibo le̱, eši etade le ye̱ miṅõ, ni edšo foi ke̱-dše kpo.

ref: Potiphar's wife kept Joseph's coat until her husband came home.
text: Ni eṅõ etade le̱ efò emase̱i ke-yeši beyino̱ ni enuṅtšo̱ le̱ ba šĩa.

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: Ni edšadše ne̱ke̱ wiemo̱i ne̱ noṅṅ etšõle̱ ake̱: Nyṍṅnū Hebrinyo le̱ ni ba wo̱ṅõ le̱ ba ní ebaye mihe fe̱o.

ref: But when I screamed for help, he left his coat and ran out of the house.”
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni miwó migbē no̱ ni mibo le̱, ešĩ etade le̱ ye̱ miṅõ, ni edšo foi.

ref: and threw Joseph in the same prison where the king's prisoners were kept. While Joseph was in prison,
text: Ni Iosef nuṅtšo̱ le̱ mõle̱ ni eyawole̱ tšũṅ ye̱ beni awoo̱ maṅtše̱ le̱ gbokle̱foi atšũṅ ye̱ le̱, ni gbokle̱foi atšũ le̱ṅ eyo̱.

ref: the Lord helped him and was good to him. He even made the jailer like Joseph so much that
text: Ši Iehowa ke̱ Iosef ye̱, ni enale̱ mo̱bo̱, ni ewo ehĩe nyam ye̱ gbokle̱foi akwe̱lo̱ le̱ hĩe.

ref: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
text: Ni gbokle̱foi akwe̱lo̱ le̱ tu gbokle̱foi le̱ ni yo̱ tšũ le̱ṅ le̱ fẽ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni nofẽno ni afeo̱ ye̱ dše̱i le̱, le̱ efeo̱.

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Gbokle̱foi akwe̱lo̱ le̱ kwe̱e̱ nokonoko ni yo̱ edeṅ le̱; edšake̱ Iehowa ke̱le̱ ye̱, ni nofẽno ni efeo̱ le̱, Iehowa hãa eſereo̱.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: ni eyawoame̱ tšũṅ ye̱ oblafoi atše̱ le̱ šĩa le̱, ye̱ gbokle̱foi atšũ le̱ṅ, heni aṅõ *Iosef awo le̱. *

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Ni oblafoi atše̱ le̱ tuame̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni esṅmoo̱ame̱, ni ame̱tše̱ kplaṅ ye̱ tšũ le̱ṅ.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Ni Iosef ba ame̱sramo̱ lebi, ni ekwe̱amē̱, ni na, ame̱fe mo̱bo̱mo̱bo̱.

ref: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
text: Ni ame̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Wo̱la lamõ ko, ni moko be̱ ni aatšõ šiši. Ni Iosef kē̱-ame̱ ake̱: Ani *šišitšõmo̱ le̱ dšee Nyoṅmo̱ nõṅ? mīkpanye fai, nyegbaämi! *

ref: The king's personal servant told Joseph, “In my dream I saw a vine
text: Ni dā̃wolo̱i anukpa le̱ gba Iosef elamõ le̱, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Milamõ le̱ mli le̱, na, weintšo ko mã mihĩe,

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: ni egbla nidši etẽ, ni efĩmo̱ afofrokpo̱i, ni egba ni ewo yibii šabii ni etšu;

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: ni mihĩe Farao kpulu le̱; nimitšere̱ weinyibii le̱, ni miṅã miwo Farao kpulu le̱ mli, ni miṅõ kpulu le̱ miwo Farao deṅ.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Ni Iosef kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ešišitšõmo̱ ne̱; nidši etẽ le̱ gbĩĩ etẽ ni;

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: biē̱ gbĩĩ etẽ le̱ Farao aawó oyitšo no̱ ni eke̱o aayato ogbe̱he le̱ ekoṅ, ni ooṅõ Farao kpala le̱ owo edeṅ, take̱ bo̱ni ofeo̱ sá, beni odši edā̃wolo̱ le̱.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Ši *dšu adšumi ye̱ Hebrimei ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni bie̱ ne̱ hũ mifeko noko ni sa ake ameaṅõmi amewo bu neṅ. *

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Ni beni abološãlo̱i anukpa le̱ na ake̱ šišitšõmo̱ le̱ hĩ dšogbāṅ le̱, ekē̱ Iosef akē̱: Mi hũ milamõ le̱ mli le̱, na, mitēre akpānõ kpãnyãi etẽ ye̱ miyiteṅ;

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: ni kpãnyã ni mã yiteṅ le̱, niyenii sro̱toi fẽ ní dši abološãlo̱ nitšumo̱ ni etšuo̱ ehãa Farao le̱ ye̱ mli; ni lofõdši ye ye̱ kpãnyã le̱ mli ye̱ miyiteṅ.
