ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Ni Iosef here no̱ ni ekē̱: Ešišitšõmo̱ nē̱: kpãnyãi etẽ le̱ gbĩĩ etẽ ni;

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: bie̱ gbĩĩ etẽ le̱ Farao aawó oyitšo no̱, ni eese̱ṅo ye̱ tšo no̱; ni lofõdši aaye ohelo ye̱ ohe.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ gbĩ ni dši gbĩ etẽ le̱ ní dši Farao fo̱mo̱gbĩ le̱, eṅmẽ etšũdši le̱ fē okplõ; ni ehole dā̃wolo̱i anukpa le̱ ke̱ abološãlo̱i anukpa le̱ ayitšei ano̱ ye̱ etšũdši le̱ ateṅ;

ref: He put the personal servant back in his old job
text: ni ehã dā̃wolo̱i anukpa le̱ yaye enukpa le̱ ekoṅ, ni eṅõ kpulu le̱ ewo Farao deṅ;

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: ši abološãlo̱i anukpa le̱, ese̱ṅle̱, take̱ be̱ni Iosef tšõame̱ šiši le̱.

ref: but the king's personal servant completely forgot about Joseph.
text: Ši dā̃wolo̱i anukpa le̱ *eyakaii Iosef, ši ehĩe kpa eno̱. *K.

ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ afii nãkplo̱i enyo̱ se̱ le̱ Farao la lamõ ko, ni na, edamo̱ fā le̱ nā.

ref: Later, seven other heads of grain appeared, but they were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: Ni na, abletšei kpawo ni noko be̱ yi ni bokã kō̱yo̱ egbĩame̱ keklẽ bakwẽ ye̱ ame̱se̱.

ref: The king's personal servant said: Now I remember what I was supposed to do.
text: Ke̱kē̱ ni dā̃woio̱i anukpa le̱ ke̱ Farao wie ake̱: Mikai mitõmo̱ ṅme̱ne̱.

ref: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
text: ni wo̱la lamõi komei nyōṅ kome mli, mi ke̱ le̱, wo̱la, mo ke̱ elamõ ke̱ ešiši.

ref: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, bo̱ni edšadše šiši etšõwo̱ le̱, nakai eba mli; mi le̱ aṅõmi ato migbe̱he ekoṅ, ni le̱ le̱ ase̱ṅle̱.

ref: The king said to him, “I had a dream, yet no one can explain what it means. I am told that you can interpret dreams.”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Mila lamõ ko, ni mokomoko be̱ ni nyẽ šiši tšõ; ni minu ni akē̱ ye̱ ohe ake̱ ke̱dši onu lamõ ko le̱, onyẽo̱ šiši otšõo̱.

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
text: Ni Iosef here Farao no̱ ake̱: *No he̱ mimli; Nyoṅmo̱ aahã Farao hedšo̱le̱ hetō. *

ref: The king told Joseph: I dreamed I was standing on the bank of the Nile River.
text: Ni Farao dšadše etšõ Iosef ake̱ Milamõ le̱ mli le̱, na, midamo̱ fā le̱ nā,

ref: I saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river, and they began feeding on the grass.
text: ni na, tšinai kpawo ni eſiſī ni he yo̱ fe̱o fã kpo ke̱-dše̱ fā le̱ mli, ni ame̱yaye nii ye̱ dſe̱iaṅ.

ref: Next, seven skinny, bony cows came up out of the river. I have never seen such terrible looking cows anywhere in Egypt.
text: Ni na, tšinai krokomei kpawo hũ nyie ame̱se̱ ba, ame̱nii ye̱ mo̱bo̱ ni ame̱he ye̱ nyãṅemo̱ ni ame̱trãtrā̃ lō hũ, ni minako ame̱no̱ pẽṅ ke̱ tãṅ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no fẽ.

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Ni beni ame̱bote ame̱mũsũṅ le̱, anaa ake̱ ame̱yeame̱; ši moṅ ame̱be ye̱ nyãṅemo̱ lolo tamo̱ tšutšu le̱ noṅṅ. Ke̱ke̱ ni mihĩe tšẽ.

ref: I also dreamed that I saw seven heads of grain growing on one stalk. The heads were full and ripe.
text: Ni misā mikwe̱ ye̱ milamõ le̱ mli, ni na, abletšei kpawo ba tšo kome yi, eba kpokplō.

ref: Then seven other heads of grain came up. They were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: Ni na, abletšei kpawo ni nii be̱ yi, ní yo̱ legelege, ní bokã kō̱yo̱ egbĩame̱ keklē bakwẽ ye̱ ame̱se̱.

ref: These heads of grain swallowed the full ones. I told my dreams to the magicians, but none of them could tell me the meaning of the dreams.
text: Ni abletšei legelegei le̱ mĩ abletšei kpakpai kpawo le̱. Ni midšadše ene̱ mitšõ woloṅlelo̱i le̱, ši mokomoko enaa he eko ekē̱ē̱mi.

ref: Joseph replied: Your Majesty, both of your dreams mean the same thing, and in them God has shown what he is going to do.
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ Farao akē̱: Farao lamõ lē̱ ekomeṅ. Nyoṅmo̱ etšõ Farao noni ebafe.

ref: The seven good cows stand for seven years, and so do the seven good heads of grain.
text: Tšinai kpakpai kpawo le̱ afii kpawoṅ, ni abletšei kpakpai kpawo le̱ hũ afii kpawo ni; lamõ lē̱ ekomeṅ.

ref: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
text: Ni tšinai kpawo ní etrã lõ, ni he yo̱ nyãṅemo̱, ni nyie ame̱se̱ ba le̱, afii kpawoṅ, ni abletšei kpawo ni noko be̱ yi ni bokã kō̱yo egbĩame̱ keklē le̱, hõmo̱yeli afii kpawo ni.

ref: It is just as I said—God has shown what he intends to do.
text: Ene̱ dši noni mikē̱ Farao akē̱ noni Nyoṅmo̱ bafe le̱, no edšie etšò Farao ne̱ le̱.

ref: The famine will be so bad that no one will remember that once there had been plenty.
text: Ni hõmo̱ ni banyie se̱ aba le̱ hãṅ ale nifále̱ le̱ doṅṅ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no; edšake̱ enā bawa naakpa.

ref: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
text: Ni noni akũ lamõ le̱ abo̱ he toi enyo̱ ahã Farao le̱, ešiši dši ake̱ Nyoṅmo̱ bafe nakai le̱le̱ṅ, ni Nyoṅmo̱ bafe le̱ oyá.

ref: Your Majesty, you should find someone who is wise and will know what to do, so that you can put him in charge of all Egypt.
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ hã Farao atao moko ni dši hĩešikãlo̱ ke̱ nilelo̱, ni eṅõle̱ eto Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: Then appoint some other officials to collect one fifth of every crop harvested in Egypt during the seven years when there is plenty.
text: Hã Farao afe ene̱, ni eto no̱kwe̱lo̱i ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni eṅõ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ niyenii le̱ amlidšā enumo̱ mli ekome ye̱ nifále̱ afii kpawo le̱ mli.

ref: It can be stored until it is needed during the seven years when there won't be enough grain in Egypt. This will keep the country from being destroyed because of the lack of food. Joseph Is Made Governor over Egypt
text: ni aṅõ nakai niyenii le̱ ato se̱ ahã šikpoṅ le̱ ye̱ hõmo̱ afii kpawo le̱ ni baba Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱ hewo̱, koni šikpoṅ le̱ akafite ye̱ hõmo̱ le̱ mli.

ref: So the king said to them, “Who could possibly handle this better than Joseph? After all, the Spirit of God is with him.”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ etšũdši le̱ ake̱: *Ani wo̱o̱nyẽ wo̱ná moko ni Nyoṅmo̱ Mumo̱ yo̱ emli tamo̱ mone̱? *Abe̱i

ref: The king told Joseph, “God is the one who has shown you these things. No one else is as wise as you are or knows as much as you do.
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef akē̱: Gbĩĩabo̱ ni Nyoṅmo̱ eṅõ ene̱ fẽ etšõo ne̱, mokomoko be̱ ni dši hĩešikãlo̱ ke̱ nilelo̱ tamo̱ bo.

ref: I'm putting you in charge of my palace, and everybody will have to obey you. No one will be over you except me.
text: Bo ooye miwe le̱ no̱, ni odāṅ gbẽ toi mimaṅbii le̱ fē aabo; sẽi le̱ gbe̱fãṅ pe̱ mafèo ye̱. Dsads.

ref: You are now governor of all Egypt!”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Na, miṅõo mito Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱. Bo̱f.

ref: Then the king took off his royal ring and put it on Joseph's finger. He gave him fine clothes to wear and placed a gold chain around his neck.
text: Ni Farao *dšie ewolonāso̱omo̱ga le̱ ye̱ enine ni eṅõ-wo Iosef nine, ni ewole̱ klala fe̱fe̱o atadei, ni eṅõ †šika kõsõṅ-kõsõ ewo ekue̱; *Est.

ref: He also let him ride in the chariot next to his own, and people shouted, “Make way for Joseph!” So Joseph was governor of Egypt.
text: ni ehã etá eſīli ni dši enyo̱ le̱ mli, ni ame̱ble̱ ke̱-nyie ehĩe ake̱: Nyekũlaä ši! Ni ewole̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱ onukpa.

ref: The king told Joseph, “Although I'm king, no one in Egypt is to do anything without your permission.”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef akē̱: Mi, midši Farao le̱; ni moko edšeṅ ose̱ ewóṅ enine lo enane no̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱.

ref: He gave Joseph the Egyptian name Zaphenath Paneah. And he let him marry Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera, a priest in the city of Heliopolis. Joseph traveled all over Egypt.
text: Ni Farao wo Iosef gbe̱i ake̱ Zafenat-Panea; ni eṅõ On oso̱fo Potifera biyō Asenat le̱ ehãle̱ gblã. Ni Iosef dše kpo eyakpa Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: For seven years there were big harvests of grain.
text: Ni nifále̱ afii kpawo le̱ mli le̱ šikpoṅ le̱ ba nii babaō ke̱-teke no̱.

ref: Joseph collected and stored up the extra grain in the cities of Egypt near the fields where it was harvested.
text: Ni ebua afii kpawo le̱ mli niyenii le̱ fẽ ni ba ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱ nā, ni eto niyenii le̱ ye̱ mãdši le̱ amli; ṅmõši niyenii ni yo̱ maṅ-fẽ-maṅ be le̱ eṅõ-to no noṅṅ mli.

ref: In fact, there was so much grain that they stopped keeping record, because it was like counting the grains of sand along the beach.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef bua able nā eto babaō dientše̱ take̱ ṅšo̱ nā šia, ke̱-yaši beyino̱ ni efõ kanemo̱; edšake̱ ebe̱ yikule̱.

ref: Joseph and his wife had two sons before the famine began.
text: Ni Iosef fo̱ bibĩĩ enyo̱, dãni hõmo̱ afii le̱ ba, mei ní eke̱ Asenat, On oso̱fo Potifera biyō le̱, fo̱ le̱.

ref: Their first son was named Manasseh, which means, “God has let me forget all my troubles and my family back home.”
text: Ni Iosef wo ekromo̱bi le̱ Manase, ni ekē̱: Edšake̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ehã mihĩe ekpa mihaomo̱ le̱ fẽ ke̱ mitše̱ we le̱ fẽ no̱.

ref: His second son was named Ephraim, which means “God has made me a success in the land where I suffered.”
text: Ni ewo moni dši enyo̱ le̱ Efraim, ni ekē̱: Edšake̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ehã miwo ſĩei ye̱ mimanehũlũ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: Egypt's seven years of plenty came to an end,
text: Ni afii kpawo ní niyenii ba babaō ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱ se̱ fo;

ref: and the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. There was not enough food in other countries, but all over Egypt there was plenty.
text: ni hõmo̱yeli afii kpawo le̱ bõi bā, take̱ bo̱ni Iosef kē̱ lē̱. Ni hõmo̱ ba šikpõdši fẽ no̱; ši Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ le̱ niyenii ye̱.

ref: When the famine finally struck Egypt, the people asked the king for food, but he said, “Go to Joseph and do what he tells you to do.”
text: Ni beni Mizraim šikpoṅ mũ le̱ fē bõi hõmo̱yeli le̱, maṅ le̱ fó tšē̱ Farao ake̱ ehãame̱ niyenii; ni Farao kē̱ Mizraimbii le̱ fẽ ake̱: Nyeyaä Iosef ṅõ, ni noni eekē̱nye le̱, nyefea.

ref: The famine became bad everywhere in Egypt, so Joseph opened the storehouses and sold the grain to the Egyptians.
text: Ni beni hõmo̱ le̱ eba šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ le̱, Iosef gble fiasei le̱ fẽ nā, ni ehõ able ehã Mizraimbii le̱; edšake̱ hõmo̱ le̱ nā wa naakpa ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: People from all over the world came to Egypt to buy grain, because the famine was so severe in their countries.
text: Ni mãdšimãdši fẽ bahe able ye̱ Iosef ṅõ ye̱ Mizraim; edšake̱ hõmo̱ le̱ nā ewa naakpa ye̱ šikpõdši le̱ fë no̱.

ref: When Jacob found out there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, “Why are you just sitting here, staring at one another?
text: Ni beni Iakob na ake̱ able ye̱ Mizraim le̱, ekē̱ ebibĩĩ le̱ ake̱: Mẽba nye-kwe̱kwe̱o̱ nyehe ne̱?

ref: I have heard there is grain in Egypt. Go down and buy some, so we won't starve to death.”
text: Ni ekē̱: Na, minn akē̱ able ye̱ Mizraim; nyeyaä dše̱i ní nyeyahea eko ke̱-baä, koni wo̱yi anã wala, ni wo̱kagboi.

ref: Ten of Joseph's brothers went to Egypt to buy grain.
text: Ni Iosef nyemimei nyoṅma le̱ yiṅ ke̱-te able hemo̱ ye̱ Mizraim.

ref: But Jacob did not send Joseph's younger brother Benjamin with them; he was afraid that something might happen to him.
text: Ši Iosef nyemi Benyamin le̱, Iakob hãã eke̱ enyemimei le̱ aya; edšake̱ ekē̱: Koni aleenõ ošrã ko akayašrãle̱.

ref: Since Joseph was governor of Egypt and in charge of selling grain, his brothers came to him and bowed with their faces to the ground.
text: Ni Iosef dši lumo̱ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni le̱ ehõo̱ able ehãa šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ bii fẽ. Ni Iosef nyemimei le̱ ba, ni ame̱ba-kotole̱, ni ame̱ke̱ ame̱hĩe bumo̱ ši.

ref: “No sir,” they replied. “We're your servants, and we have only come to buy grain.
text: Ni ame̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Dabi, minuṅtšo̱, ši niyenii hemo̱ otšũdši ba.

ref: We're honest men, and we come from the same family—we're not spies.”
text: Wo̱fẽ nū kome bii dšiwo̱; ano̱kwafoi dšiwo̱, dšee sralo̱i dši otšũdši.

ref: “That isn't so!” Joseph insisted. “You've come here to find out where our country is weak.”
text: Ni ekē̱ame̱ ake̱: Dabi, ši šikpoṅ ne̱ yayaiaṅ kwe̱mo̱ nyeba.

ref: But they explained, “Sir, we come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and one of our brothers is dead.”
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Otšũdši le̱ nyemimei nyoṅma ke̱ enyo̱ ni, nū kome bii dšiwo̱ ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱; ni na, gbekẽ le̱ ye̱ wo̱tše̱ ṅõ amrõ ne̱, ni mokome be̱.

ref: Joseph replied: It's like I said. You're spies,
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Ene̱ mina mi mikē̱nye akē̱: Sralo̱i dšinye lē̱!

ref: and I'm going to find out the truth. I swear by the life of the king that you won't leave this place until your youngest brother comes here.
text: Noni akē̱-akpanye ši nē̱: Mina Farao ake̱ nyedšeṅ bie̱ nyeyaṅ, dša nyenyemi gbekẽ le̱ eba bie̱ dã.

ref: Choose one of you to go after your brother, while the rest of you stay here in jail. That will show whether you are telling the truth. But if you are lying, I swear by the life of the king that you are spies!
text: Nyetšũa nyeteṅ mokome, ni eyaṅõ nyenyemi le̱ ke̱-ba; ni nye le̱ aawonye tšũṅ, koni aṅõ-kpa nyewiemo̱i le̱ aši akwe̱ akē̱ ano̱kwale ye̱ nyemli lo; ke̱-dšee nakai le̱, mina Farao ake̱, sralo̱i dšinye!

ref: Joseph kept them all under guard for three days,
text: Ni ewoame̱ tšũṅ gbĩĩ etẽ.

ref: before saying to them: Since I respect God, I'll give you a chance to save your lives.
text: Ni gbĩ ní dši gbĩ etẽ le̱ Iosef kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyefea ene̱, ní nyeyi aná wala; ši mi le̱ mīše Nyoṅmo̱ gbeyei.

ref: If you are honest men, one of you must stay here in jail, and the rest of you can take the grain back to your starving families.
text: Ké̱ ano̱kwafoi dšinye le̱, nyehãa afĩa nyenyemimei le̱ ateṅ mokome awoa heni awonye tšũṅ ye̱ le̱; ši nye le̱, hõmo̱ ní yo̱ nyešĩai le̱ hewo̱ le̱, nyeyaṅõa able le̱ ke̱-yaā;

ref: But you must bring your youngest brother to me. Then I'll know that you are telling the truth, and you won't be put to death. Joseph's brothers agreed
text: ni nyeke̱ nyenyemi gbekẽ le̱ abaä miṅõ, no ake̱-ana nyewiemo̱i le̱ ano̱kwale, koni nyekagboi. Ni ame̱fe nakai.

ref: They did not know that Joseph could understand them, since he was speaking through an interpreter.
text: Ši ame̱ le̱ ame̱lee ake̱ Iosef enu noni ame̱ke̱ le̱; edšake̱ nādšielo̱ dšieo̱ ame̱nā.

ref: Joseph turned away from them and cried, but soon he turned back and spoke to them again. Then he had Simeon tied up and taken away while they watched. Joseph's Brothers Return to Canaan
text: Ni etšṍ ehe ke̱-dše ame̱ṅõ ni eyafó; ni ekũ ese̱ eba ame̱ṅõ ekoṅ ni ekeame̱ bawie; ni edšie Simeon ye̱ ame̱teṅ, ni efĩle ye̱ ame̱hĩe.

ref: Joseph gave orders for his brothers' grain sacks to be filled with grain and for their money to be put in their sacks. He also gave orders for them to be given food for their journey home. After this was done,
text: Ni Iosef fá ake̱ awo ame̱nibii le̱ amli ahle obõbõ, ni ake̱ mofẽmo šika akũ se̱ awo ekotoku mli ekoṅ, ní ahãame̱ ghe̱no̱ niyenii. Ni afe nakai ahãame̱.

ref: they each loaded the grain on their donkeys and left.
text: Ni ame̱ṅõ ame̱ble le̱ ame̱tẽre ame̱tedšii le̱, ni ame̱dše dše̱i ame̱te.

ref: When they stopped for the night, one of them opened his sack to get some grain for his donkey, and at once he saw his moneybag.
text: Ni heni ame̱teṅ moko fẽne ekotoku nā ní ebahã etedši niyenii ye̱ tōhe le̱, ena ešika le̱, ni na, ekā no̱ ye̱ ekotoku le̱ mli.

ref: “Here's my money!” he told his brothers. “Right here in my sack.” They were trembling with fear as they stared at one another and asked themselves, “What has God done to us?”
text: Ni ekē̱ enyemimei le̱ ake̱: Ake̱ mišika le̱ ekũ se̱ ehãmi, ni na, eye̱ mikotoku le̱ mli po. Ni ame̱tšui fã ni ame̱kwe̱kwē̱ amē̱he ke̱ hekpokpomo̱, ni ame̱kē̱: Meni Nyoṅmo̱ fewo̱ ne̱?

ref: When they returned to the land of Canaan, they told their father Jacob everything that had happened to them:
text: Ni ame̱ba ame̱tše̱ Iakob ṅõ ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱, ni ame̱bagbale̱ nii ni afeame̱ le̱ fē ake̱:

ref: The governor of Egypt was rude and treated us like spies.
text: Nū le̱ ni dši šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ nuṅtšo̱ lē̱ tutu ehĩe ewowo̱, ni ebuwo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ sralo̱i.

ref: But we told him, “We're honest men, not spies.
text: Ni wo̱kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ano̱kwafoi dšiwo̱, wo̱dšee sralo̱i;

ref: We come from a family of twelve brothers. The youngest is still with our father in Canaan, and the other is dead.”
text: nyemimei nyoṅma ke̱ enyo̱ dši wo̱tše̱ bii le̱; mokome be̱, ni gbekẽ le̱ ye̱ wo̱tše̱ ṅõ amrõ ne̱ ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: Then the governor of Egypt told us, “I'll find out if you really are honest. Leave one of your brothers here with me, while you take the grain to your starving families.
text: Ni nū le̱ ni dši šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ nuṅtšo̱ le̱ kē̱wo̱ akē̱: Ene̱ maṅõ-le ake̱ anokwafoi dšinye: nyešĩa nyenyemimei le̱ ateṅ mokome nyehãami, ni hõmo̱ ní yo̱ nyešĩai le̱ hewo̱ le̱ nyeṅõa niyenii ke̱-yaã,

ref: But bring your youngest brother to me, so I can be certain that you are honest men and not spies. After that, I'll let your other brother go free, and you can stay here and trade.”
text: ni nyeyaṅõa nyenyemi gbekẽ le̱ ke̱-ba, no dāni male ake̱ dšee sralo̱i dšinye, ši ano̱kwafoi dšinye, koni madšie nyenyemi le̱ mahãnye, ni nyeye dšra ye̱ šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱.

ref: When the brothers started emptying their sacks of grain, they found their moneybags in them. They were frightened, and so was their father Jacob,
text: Ni eha mli ake̱, beni ame̱dšie ame̱kotokui le̱ amli nii le̱, na, mofẽmo šika ni afĩ le̱ ye̱ ekotoku le̱ mli. Ni beni ame̱na ame̱sika ni afĩmo̱fĩmo̱i le̱, ame̱ke̱ ame̱tše̱ fẽ še gbeyei.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!

ref: Reuben spoke up, “Father, if I don't bring Benjamin back, you can kill both of my sons. Trust me with him, and I'll bring him back.”
text: Ni Ruben kē̱ etšē̱ ake̱: Ke̱dši mike̱le̱ ekũũ se̱ ebabãão le̱, no le̱ gbe mibihĩĩ enyo̱ le̱; tule̱ owo mideṅ, ni mike̱le̱ aakũ se̱ ababão.

ref: But Jacob said, “I won't let my son Benjamin go down to Egypt with the rest of you. His brother is already dead, and he is the only son I have left. I am an old man, and if anything happens to him on the way, I'll die from sorrow, and all of you will be to blame.”
text: Ni ekē̱: Mibi le̱ ke̱nye eyaa; edšake̱ enyemi le̱ egbo, ni le̱ ekome eſe̱; ke̱dši ošrã ko yašrãle̱ ye̱ gbe̱ ni nyeyanyie le̱ no̱ le̱, no le̱ nyebaṅõ miwadši le̱ ke̱-ya gbohïĩadše ke̱ aw̌ere̱hō.

ref: Judah replied, “The governor strictly warned us that we would not be allowed to see him unless we brought our youngest brother with us. If you let us take Benjamin along, we will go and buy grain. But we won't go without him!”
text: ši ke̱dši ohãṅ eya le̱, no le̱ wo̱woṅ yā; edšake̱ nū le̱ kē̱wo̱ ake̱: Nyenaṅ mihĩe, akē̱ dša nyenyemi le̱ fata nyehe.

ref: If we had not wasted all this time, we could already have been there and back twice.”
text: Ši dšee šõ wo̱fe kũle̱, bene̱ wo̱te wo̱ba šii enyo̱.

ref: Their father said: If Benjamin must go with you, take the governor a gift of some of the best things from our own country, such as perfume, honey, spices, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Israel kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Ke̱dši nakai le̱, no le̱ bo̱ni nyefe ne̱: nyeṅõa šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱ yibii kpakpai le̱ eko nyewoa nyenibii amli, ni *nyeke̱-yakea nū le̱, gbonyowō tšofã fiō ke̱ wò fiō ke̱ tšofã-ke̱-ṅma ke̱ ohe̱ ni dšeo̱ ṅma, ke̱ amitšro̱bii ke̱ ṅme̱i; *Abe̱i

ref: Also take along twice the amount of money for the grain, because there must have been some mistake when the money was put back in your sacks.
text: ni nyeṅõa šika kroko nyehĩea nyedeṅ, ni šikai le̱ ni akũ se̱ ake̱-ſie no̱ ye̱ nyekotokui amli le̱ nyeṅõa no hũ nyehĩea nyedeṅ ke̱-yaä ekoṅ, ekole̱ hene̱ hĩe dše no̱.

ref: When you go in to see the governor, I pray that God All-Powerful will be good to you and that the governor will let your other brother and Benjamin come back home with you. But if I must lose my children, I suppose I must.
text: Ši Mawu Ofe le̱ ahã nū le̱ mũsũṅ atšõle̱ ye̱ nyehe, koni eṅmë nyenyemi mokome le̱ ke̱ Benyamin he ehãnye. *Ši mi le̱, ké̱ akpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe dšiṅṅ, akpakpā. *

ref: The brothers took the gifts, twice the amount of money, and Benjamin. Then they hurried off to Egypt. When they stood in front of Joseph,
text: Ni hĩĩ le̱ ṅõ nakai nikēnii le̱, ni ame̱kũ šika le̱ ame̱bo̱ he ame̱hĩe ame̱deṅ, ke̱ Benyamin, ni ame̱te ši ni ame̱yiṅ ame̱te Mizraim, ni ame̱yadamo̱ Iosef hĩe.

ref: he saw Benjamin and told the servant in charge of his house, “Take these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and cook it, so they can eat with me at noon.”
text: Ni beni Iosef na ake̱ Benyamin fata ame̱he le̱, ekē̱ ešĩa le̱ no̱ onukpa le̱ ake̱: Oke̱ ne̱kē̱ hĩĩ ne aya šĩa ni ogbe koloi oke̱-fe niyenii oto; ši hĩĩ ne̱ ke̱mi baye nii ſane ne̱.

ref: The servant did as he was told and took the brothers to Joseph's house.
text: Ni nū le̱ fe take̱ bo̱ni Iosef kē̱le̱ le̱, ni eke̱ hĩĩ le̱ te Iosef we le̱.

ref: So when they arrived at Joseph's house, they said to the servant in charge,
text: Ni ame̱tši ame̱be̱ṅke̱ Iosef šĩa le̱ no̱ onukpa le̱, ni ame̱ke̱le̱ wie ye̱ šĩa le̱ šinā le̱ ši,

ref: “Sir, we came to Egypt once before to buy grain.
text: ni ame̱kē̱: Ofai ne̱, wo̱nuṅtšo̱! Kleṅkleṅ ní wo̱ba niyenii hemo̱ le̱,

ref: But when we stopped for the night, we each found in our grain sacks the exact amount we had paid. We have brought that money back,
text: eba mli ake, beni wo̱še̱ tōhe le̱, ní wo̱fẽne wo̱kotokui le̱ anã le̱, na, mofẽmo šika kã no̱ ye̱ ekotoku lē̱ mli, wo̱šika le̱ ye̱ ho̱ni aṅmē le̱ pe̱pē̱pe̱; ni wo̱ke̱-hĩe wo̱dẽ ke̱-ha ekoṅ;

ref: together with enough money to buy more grain. We don't know who put the money in our sacks.”
text: ni šika kroko hũ wo̱ke̱-hĩe wo̱dẽ ke̱-ba niyenii heme̱ ne̱; wo̱lee moni ṅõ wo̱šika le̱ ewo wo̱kotokui le̱ amli.

ref: “It's all right,” the servant replied. “Don't worry. The God you and your father worship must have put the money there, because I received your payment in full.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.
text: Ni ekē̱: Hedšo̱le̱ abãnye; nyekašea gbeyei! Nye-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ ke̱ nyetše̱ Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ ehã nyeto ahṹ ye̱ nyekotokui le̱ amli; nyešika le̱ eše̱ midẽ. Ni edšie *Simeon ebahãame̱. *

ref: The servant took them into Joseph's house and gave them water to wash their feet. He also tended their donkeys.
text: Ni nū le̱ ke̱ hĩĩ le̱ ba Iosef we le̱, ni ebahãame̱ nu, ni *ame̱fo̱ ame̱nãdši ahe, ni ehã ame̱tedšii le̱ anii. *

ref: The brothers got their gifts ready to give to Joseph at noon, since they had heard they were going to eat there.
text: Ni ame̱sā nikēnii le̱ ame̱to Iosef se̱, dãni eba ſane le̱, edšake̱ ame̱nu ake̱ ame̱baye nii ye̱ dše̱i.

ref: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, “What about your elderly father? Is he still alive?”
text: Ni ebiame̱ bo̱ni ame̱yo̱, ni ekē̱: Ani nyetše̱ nūmo le̱ *ni nyetšĩ etā le̱ ye̱ dšogbāṅ lo? Ani eye̱ dšeṅ lolo? *

ref: They answered, “Your servant our father is still alive and well.” And again they bowed down to Joseph.
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Otšũlo̱, wo̱tšē̱ le̱, eye̱ dšogbāṅ, eye̱ dšeṅ lolo. Ni *ame̱mãmo̱ ame̱yitšo ši, ni ame̱kotole̱. *

ref: When Joseph looked around and saw his brother Benjamin, he said, “This must be your youngest brother, the one you told me about. God bless you, my son.”
text: Ni ehole ehĩṅme̱ii ano̱ ni ena enyemi Benyamin, enye mũsũṅ bi le̱, ni ekē̱: Nyenyemi gbekẽ le̱ ni nyetšĩ etã nyetšõmi le̱ ne̱? Ni ekē̱: Nyoṅmo̱ adšō̱o, mibi!

ref: Then, because of his love for Benjamin, he rushed off to his room and cried.
text: Ni Iosef he fe oyá, edšakē̱ emũsũṅ tšōle̱ ye̱ enyemi le̱ hewo̱, ni ẽtao heko eyafó ye̱, ni eyabote ese̱tšũṅ, ni eyafó ye̱ dše̱i.

ref: After washing his face and returning, he was able to control himself and said, “Serve the meal!”
text: Ni efo̱ ehĩe ni edše kpo, ni emĩa ehĩe, ni ekē̱: Nyeke̱ niyenii abamãmo̱a ši!

ref: Joseph was served at a table by himself, and his brothers were served at another. The Egyptians sat at yet another table, because Egyptians felt it was disgusting to eat with Hebrews.
text: Ni ame̱ke̱ enõ bamã ši eyimū ke̱ ame̱ bũ ame̱nõ yimūyimū, ke̱ Mizraimbii ni ke̱le̱ yeo̱ nii le̱ hũ anõ yimūyimū; edšake̱ Mizraimbii ke̱ Hebribii kaa nine yee nii, edšake̱ Mizraimbii *mīhĩ nõ. *

ref: To the surprise of Joseph's brothers, they were seated in front of him according to their ages, from the oldest to the youngest.
text: Ni ake̱ame̱ batrá ehĩe, kromo̱bi le̱ ye̱ enukpa gbe̱nã, ni gbekẽ hũ ye̱ egbekẽ gbe̱nã; ni hĩĩ le̱ anā kpe̱ ame̱he ni ame̱kwe̱kwē̱ ame̱hĩeaṅ ke̱-kpe.

ref: They were served food from Joseph's table, and Benjamin was given five times as much as each of the others. So Joseph's brothers ate and drank with him and had a good time.
text: Ni edšieo̱ nii ke̱-dše̱o̱ ehĩe ehãa-ame̱; ši Benyamin nõ le̱ ſè ame̱teṅ mofẽmo nõ šii enumo̱. Ni ame̱nu noko ni ame̱ke̱le̱ *ná mīše̱. *Hag.

ref: Later, Joseph told the servant in charge of his house, “Fill the men's grain sacks with as much as they can hold and put their money in the sacks. Also put my silver cup in the sack of the youngest brother.” The servant did as he was told.
text: ni mikpuln ni dši midſie̱te̱i kpulu le̱ oke̱-fata able šika le̱ he ofõ no̱ ye̱ gbekẽ le̱ kotoku le̱ mli. Ni efe bo̱ni Iosef kē̱ le̱.

ref: But they had not gone far from the city when Joseph told the servant, “Go after those men! When you catch them, say, ‘My master has been good to you. So why have you stolen his silver cup?
text: Beni ame̱dše kpo ye̱ mã le̱ṅ ni ame̱še̱ko šoṅṅ heko le̱, ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef kē̱ ešĩa le̱ no̱ onukpa le̱ ake̱: Te ši, ni otiu gbomei le̱, ni ké̱ oninaame̱ le̱, kē̱mo̱ame̱ ake̱: Menihewo̱ nyeke̱ efõṅ to ekpakpa nãdšiaṅ ne̱?

ref: Not only does he drink from his cup, but he also uses it to learn about the future. You have done a terrible thing.’ ”
text: Té ene̱ mli minuṅtšo̱ nuo̱ dā̃ ye̱, ni esā ekē̱-klaä? Nyefe efõṅ ake̱ nyefe nakai le̱!

ref: We even returned the money we found in our grain sacks when we got back to Canaan. So why would we want to steal any silver or gold from your master's house?
text: Na, šika le̱ ni wo̱na dſere no̱ ye̱ wo̱kotokui le̱ amli le̱ wo̱ke̱-dše̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ tõ *wo̱bahão; té aafe tẽṅ mo̱ ni wo̱o̱dšu dſie̱te̱i lo šika ye̱ onuṅtšo̱ we̱ṅ? *

ref: “Good!” the man replied, “I'll do what you have said. But only the one who has the cup will become my slave. The rest of you can go free.”
text: Ni ekē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ nyehãa afea bo̱ni nyekē̱ lē̱. Mofẽmo deṅ ni aana ye̱ le̱, eetšṍ minyṍṅ, ni nye le̱ sõ ko wulaṅnye.

ref: Each of the brothers quickly put his sack on the ground and opened it.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni ame̱he fe oyá ni ame̱totō ame̱kotokui le̱ ame̱māmo̱ ši, ni ame̱fẽnefẽnē ame̱kotokui le̱ anā.

ref: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
text: Ni ebṍi taomo̱ ke̱-dše̱ onukpa le̱ no̱ ni eyagbe nā ye̱ gbekẽ le̱ no̱; ni ayana kpulu le̱ ye̱ Benyamin kotoku le̱ mli.

ref: “Sir, what can we say?” Judah replied. “How can we say we are innocent, when God has shown we are guilty? And now all of us are your slaves, especially the one who had the cup.”
text: Ni Iuda kē̱: Meni wo̱bakē̱ minuṅtšo̱? Mẽ wiemo̱ wo̱bawie? Alo té wo̱badšie wo̱nā tẽṅ? *Nyoṅmo̱ ena otšũdši anõša le̱. Na, wo̱tšṍmo̱ minnntšo̱ nyṍdši, wo̱ ke̱ mo dẽ ni ana kpulu le̱ ye̱ le̱ fẽ. *

ref: Joseph told them, “I would never punish all of you. Only the one who was caught with the cup will become my slave. The rest of you are free to go home to your father.” Judah Pleads for Benjamin
text: Ši ekē̱: Kpaõ, minyẽṅ nakai mafe; mo deṅ ni ana kpulu le̱ ye̱ le̱, le̱ eetšṍ minyṍṅ, ni nye le̱ nyeyaä nyetše̱ ṅõ ye̱ hedšo̱le̱ mli.

ref: Judah went over to Joseph and said: Sir, you have as much power as the king himself, and I am only your slave. Please don't get angry if I speak.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iuda tši be̱ṅke̱le̱ ni ekē̱: Ofai ne̱, minuṅtšo̱! Hã ní otšũlo̱ awie wiemo̱ ko ye̱ minuṅtšo̱ toiiaṅ; ni kãhã omli mīwo otšũlo̱ la, ši otamo̱ Farao noṅṅ!

ref: You asked us if our father was still alive and if we had any more brothers.
text: Minuṅtšo̱ bi etšũdši ake̱: *Ani nyeye̱ tše̱ lo nyeminū ko? *

ref: So we told you, “Our father is a very old man. In fact, he was already old when Benjamin was born. Benjamin's brother is dead. Now Benjamin is the only one of the two brothers who is still alive, and our father loves him very much.”
text: Ni wo̱kē̱ minuṅtšo̱ ake̱: Wo̱ye̱ tše̱ nūmo ke̱ enakutšoṅbi fiō ko; ni enyemi le̱ egbo, ni enye bi kome ní eſe̱ dšile̱, ni etše̱ sumoo̱le̱.

ref: You ordered us to bring him here, so you could see him for yourself.
text: Ni okē̱ otšũdši ake̱: Nyeyaṅōale̱ ke̱-baä, koni manale̱ ke̱ mihiṅme̱ii.

ref: We told you that our father would die if Benjamin left him.
text: Ni wo̱kē̱ minuṅtšo̱ akē̱: Gbekẽ le̱ nyēṅ ašĩ etše̱, edšake̱ ke̱dši ešĩ etše̱ le̱, etše̱ aagbo.

ref: But you warned us that we could never see you again, unless our youngest brother came with us.
text: Ni *okē̱ otšũdši ake̱: Ke̱dši nyenyemi gbekẽ le̱ ke̱nye baa le̱, nyenaṅ mihĩe doṅṅ. *

ref: So we returned to our father and reported what you had said.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱, beni wo̱ba otšũlo̱, mitše̱, ṅõ le̱, wo̱kē̱le̱ minuṅtšo̱ wiemo̱i le̱.

ref: Later our father sent us back here to buy more grain.
text: Ni beni wo̱tše̱ kē̱ ake̱: Nyeyaä ekoṅ ni nyeyabea niyenii fiō ko nyebahãawo̱ le̱,

ref: But we told him, “We can't go back to Egypt without our youngest brother. We will never be let in to see the governor, unless he is with us.”
text: wo̱kē̱: Wo̱nyẽṅ wo̱ya; ke̱dši wo̱nyemi gbekẽ le̱ fata wo̱be le̱, no dãni wo̱o̱te; ši wo̱nyẽṅ nū le̱ hĩe wo̱na, dša wo̱nyemi gbekẽ le̱ fata wo̱he.

ref: Sir, our father then reminded us that his favorite wife had given birth to two sons.
text: Ni otšũlo̱, mitše̱, kē̱wo̱ akē̱: Nyedientše̱ nyele ake̱ miṅã le̱, bii enyo̱ mike̱le̱ fo̱;

ref: One of them was already missing and had not been seen for a long time. My father thinks the boy was torn to pieces by some wild animal,
text: ni mokome dše mihe, ni *mikē̱: Tšere̱mo̱ kẽ atšere̱ emli ne̱! ni minakole̱ ke̱-baši ṅme̱ne̱; *

ref: and he said, “I am an old man. If you take Benjamin from me, and something happens to him, I will die of a broken heart.”
text: ni ke̱dši *nyeṅõ mone̱ hũ ke̱-dše̱ mibe ke̱-te ni ošrã ko yašrãle̱ le̱, no le̱ nyebaṅṍ miwadši le̱ ke̱-ya gbohĩĩadše ke̱ aw̌rere̱hō. *

ref: That's why Benjamin must be with us when I go back to my father. He loves him so much
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ ke̱dši mite otšũlo̱, mitše̱, ṅõ ni gbekẽ le̱ fataa wo̱he le̱, ake̱ noni gbekẽ ne̱ susuma he esusuma kpe̱te̱ hewo̱ le̱,

ref: I promised my father that I would bring him safely home. If I don't, I told my father he could blame me the rest of my life.
text: Edšake̱ otšũlo̱ *damo̱ mli ye̱ gbekẽ ne̱ hewo̱ ye̱ mitše̱ ṅõ, ni mikē̱: Ke̱dši mike̱le̱ ekũũ se̱ ebahãão le̱, no le̱ maye mitše̱ yi sõ ke̱-ate nãno̱. *

ref: Sir, I am your slave. Please let me stay here in place of Benjamin and let him return home with his brothers.
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱no̱ mĩkpao fai ni ohã otšũlo̱ ahĩ bie̱ ake̱ minuṅtšo̱ ayṍṅ ye̱ gbekẽ le̱ nãdšiaṅ, ni ohã gbekẽ le̱ ke̱ enyemimei le̱ aya.

ref: How can I face my father if Benjamin isn't with me? I couldn't bear to see my father in such sorrow.
text: Ši té aafe tẽṅ ni mate mitše̱ ṅõ ni gbekẽ le̱ fataa miheṅ? No le̱ mayana efõṅ ní aba mitše̱ no̱!

ref: Since Joseph could no longer control his feelings in front of his servants, he sent them out of the room. When he was alone with his brothers, he told them, “I am Joseph.”
text: Agbe̱ne̱ Iosef nyẽẽ ehĩe emĩa doṅṅ ye̱ mei ní damo̱ emase̱i le̱ fẽ hĩe, ni ebo ake̱: Nyehãa mofẽmo adšea kpo ke̱-dšea mimase̱i! Ni mokomoko ke̱ Iosef damo̱o̱ ši, beni edšie ehe ši etšõ enyemimei le̱.

ref: Then he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him and told about it in the king's palace.
text: Ni ewó egbē ni efó, ni Mizraimbii ke̱ Farao webii le̱ tete nu.

ref: Joseph asked his brothers if his father was still alive, but they were too frightened to answer.
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ enyemimei le̱ ake̱: Mi Iosef ni. Ani mitše̱ ye̱ dšeṅ lolo? Ni enyemimei le̱ nyẽẽle̱ hetõ ahã, edšake̱ ame̱dšidšā ye̱ ehĩe.

ref: Joseph told them to come closer to him, and when they did, he said: Yes, I am your brother Joseph, the one you sold into Egypt.
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ enyemimei le ake̱: Nyetšia nyebe̱ṅke̱ami kol Ni ame̱tši ame̱ba. Ni ekē̱: Midši nyenyemi Iosef, moni nyehõ *ke̱-ba Mizraim le̱! *

ref: Don't worry or blame yourselves for what you did. God is the one who sent me ahead of you to save lives.
text: Ni agbe̱ne̱ nyekahãa mĩdo̱anye ni nyemli akawowoa nyehe la, ye̱ hõmo̱ ní nyehõmi ke̱-ba bie̱ le hewo̱; edšake̱ *mei ayiwalaheremo̱ hewo̱ Nyoṅmo̱ tšũmi ke̱-tšṍ nyehĩe. *

ref: There has already been a famine for two years, and for five more years no one will plow fields or harvest grain.
text: Ši afii enyo̱ ne̱ ní hõmo̱ ba šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni eſe̱ afii enumo̱ hũ lolo ni hũmo̱ lo kpamo̱ be̱ mli.

ref: But God sent me on ahead of you to keep your families alive and to save you in this wonderful way.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ tšũmi ke̱-tšṍ nyehĩe, koni mahā nyeteṅ meikomei kẽ aſe̱ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni mahere nyeyiwala ke̱ dšiemo̱ kpeteṅkple.

ref: After all, you weren't really the ones who sent me here—it was God. He made me the highest official in the king's court and placed me over all Egypt.
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ dšee nye nyehã miba bie̱, ši Nyoṅmo̱ moṅ ni; ni le̱ noṅṅ ebaṅõmi efe take̱ Farao tše̱ ke̱ ewe le̱ fẽ no̱ nuṅtšo̱ ke̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱ lumo̱.

ref: Now hurry back and tell my father that his son Joseph says, “God has made me ruler of Egypt. Come here as quickly as you can.
text: Nyebe afea oyá ni nyeyaä mitše̱ ṅõ, ni nyekē̱ale̱ ake̱: Bo̱ni obi Iosef kē̱ ne̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ewomi Mizraim fẽ no̱ nuṅtšo̱; ba miṅõ, kātše̱!

ref: You will live near me in the region of Goshen with your children and grandchildren, as well as with your sheep, goats, cattle, and everything else you own.
text: Ni obahĩ Gošen šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni obe̱ṅke̱mi, ho ke̱ obii ke̱ onãbii ke̱ otōi ke̱ otšinai ke̱ nibii fẽ ni oye̱;

ref: I will take care of you there during the next five years of famine. But if you don't come, you and your family and your animals will starve to death.”
text: ni malè̱o ye̱ dše̱i; edšake̱ eſe̱ hõmo̱ afii le̱ enumo̱ lolo, koni okaye ohĩa, bo ke̱ owe ke̱ mei fẽ ni oyo̱.

ref: All of you, including my brother Benjamin, can tell by what I have said that I really am Joseph.
text: Ni na, nyehĩe tua, ni minyemi Benyamin hũ hĩe tua ake̱ midientše̱ mike̱nye wieo̱ ke̱ minābu ne̱.

ref: Tell my father about my great power here in Egypt and about everything you have seen. Hurry and bring him here.
text: Ni nyeyagbaä mitše̱ minumnyam fẽ ni miyo̱ ye̱ Mizraim ke̱ nibii fẽ ni nyena le̱, ni nyehe afea oyá ni nyeke̱ mitše̱ abaã bie̱.

ref: Joseph and Benjamin hugged each other and started crying.
text: Ni eho egbē enyemi Benyamin kue̱ no̱ ni efó, ni Benyamin hũ fó ſie ekue̱ no̱.

ref: Joseph was still crying as he kissed each of his other brothers. After this, they started talking with Joseph.
text: Ni esā eſo̱ſō̱ enyemimei le̱ fẽ nā, ni efó eſie ame̱no̱; ni no se̱ le̱ enyemimei le̱ ke̱le̱ wie.

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Ni anu sane ne̱ ye̱ Farao we le̱ ake̱: Iosef nyemimei eba! Ni Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱ ná be mīše̱.

ref: So the king said to Joseph: Tell your brothers to load their donkeys and return to Canaan.
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Kē̱mo̱ onyemimei le̱ ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyetẽrea nyekoloi le̱ adšatšui, ni nyeyiaṅ ke̱-yaä Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱,

ref: Tell them to bring their father and their families here. I will give them the best land in Egypt, and they can eat and enjoy everything that grows there.
text: ni nyeyaṅõa nyetše̱ ke̱ nyebii ke̱ ho̱i, ni nyebaä miṅõ, ni mahãnye Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ nikpakpai, ni nyeaye šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fo̱ le̱.

ref: Also tell your brothers to take some wagons from Egypt for their wives and children to ride in. And be sure they bring their father.
text: Ni bo le̱ afā̃o ake̱: Noni nyefe ne̱: nyeṅõa ſīlii ke̱-dše̱a Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱ nyebāa nyebii ke̱ nyeṅãmei, ni nyeyaṅõa nyetše̱ ke̱-baä.

ref: They can leave their possessions behind, because they will be given the best of everything in Egypt.
text: Ni asaṅ nyekadia nyenibii ase̱; ši Mizraim šikpoṅ mū le̱, fẽ no̱ nikpakpai le̱ nyenõṅ.

ref: Jacob's sons agreed to do what the king had said. And Joseph gave them wagons and food for their trip home, just as the king had ordered.
text: Ni Israel bihĩĩ le̱ fe nakai. Ni Iosef hāame̱ ſīlii; take̱ boni Farao fále̱ le̱, ni ehãame̱ gbe̱no̱ niyenii.

ref: To his father he sent ten donkeys loaded with the best things in Egypt, and ten other donkeys loaded with grain and bread and other food for the return trip.
text: Ni etše̱ le̱, nii ni emādšele̱ ne̱: Tedšii nyoṅma ni tĕre Mizraim nibii kpakpai, ke̱ tedšiyei nyoṅma ni tẽre able ke̱ abolo ke̱ niyenii, ni ake̱-yahã etše̱, ni eke̱-nyie gbe̱ le̱.

ref: Then he sent his brothers off and told them, “Don't argue on the way home!”
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni ewo enyemimei le̱ agbe̱ ni ame̱ṅõ-ſie no̱; ni ekē̱ame̱ ake̱: *Nyekayabéa ye̱ gbe̱ le̱ ne̱! *

ref: Joseph's brothers left Egypt, and when they arrived in Canaan,
text: Ni ame̱dše Mizraim, ni ome̱ba ame̱tše̱ Iakob ṅõ ye̱ Kanaan sikpoṅ le̱ no̱,

ref: they told their father that Joseph was still alive and was the ruler of Egypt. But their father was so surprised that he could not believe them.
text: ni ame̱bakē̱le̱ ake̱: Iosef ye̱ dšeṅ lolo, ni le̱ eyee̱ lumo̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ no̱. Ni eyiṅ fele̱ ko̱šiko̱ši, edšake̱ eheeame̱ eyee.

ref: Then they told him everything Joseph had said. When he saw the wagons Joseph had sent, he felt much better
text: Ni ame̱kē̱le̱ Iosef wiemo̱i fẽ ni eke̱ame̱ wie le̱. Ni beni ena ſīlii le̱ ni Iosef hã ake̱-bale̱ ṅõ le̱, ame̱tše̱ Iakob he tšele̱.

ref: and said, “Now I can believe you! My son Joseph must really be alive, and I will get to see him before I die.”
text: Ni Israel kē̱: Efá; mibi Iosef ye̱ dšeṅ lolo! Mate mayakwe̱ ehĩe däni magbo.

ref: When Jacob went to Egypt, his children who were born in northern Syria also went along with their families. Jacob and his wife Leah had a total of
text: Mene̱mei dši Lea bihĩĩ ni eke̱ Iakob fo̱ ye̱ Mesopotamia le̱, ke̱ ebiyō Dina le̱. Ebihĩĩ ke̱ ebiyei le̱ fẽ dši susumai nyoṅmai-etẽ ke̱ etẽ,

ref: The king will call you in and ask what you do for a living.
text: Ni kē̱ aaba mii akē̱ Farao aabatšé̱nye, ni eebinye ake̱: Meni dši nye-nitšumo̱? le̱,

ref: Jacob and Zilpah, the servant woman Laban had given his daughter Leah, had a total of
text: Mene̱mei dši Zilpa, moni Laban ṅõ-hā ebiyō Lea le̱, bii; ni mene̱mei eke̱ Iakob fo̱, susumai nyoṅma ke̱ ekpa.

ref: Jacob and Bilhah, the servant woman Laban had given his daughter Rachel, had seven children and grandchildren. Their son Dan took his son Hushim. Their son Naphtali took his sons Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
text: Mene̱mei dši Bilha, moni Laban ṅõ-bã ebiyō Rahel le̱, bii ní eke̱ Iakob fo̱ le̱, snsumai le̱ fẽ dši kpawo.

ref: Sixty-six members of Jacob's family went to Egypt with him, not counting his daughters-in-law.
text: Susumai fē ni ke̱ Iakob ba Mizraim ni dše̱ ehe̱ mli ne̱, sõro ebii le̱ aṅãmei le̱, *susumai le̱ fẽ dši nyoṅmai-ekpa ke̱ ekpa.

ref: Jacob's two grandsons who were born there made it a total of
text: Ni Iosef bii ni efo̱ ye̱ Mizraim le̱ dši susumai enyo̱. Iakob we le̱ fẽ ni ba Mizraim le̱ nā šio̱ *susumai nyoṅmai-kpawo. *

ref: So Joseph got in his chariot and went to meet his father. When they met, Joseph hugged his father around the neck and cried for a long time.
text: Ni Iosef sã eſīli ni eyiṅ ete etše̱ Israel kpēmo̱ ye̱ Gošen. Ni beni enale̱ le̱ ebo egbẽ ekue̱ no̱, ni efó eſie ekue̱ no̱ āhũ.

ref: Jacob said to Joseph, “Now that I have seen you and know you are still alive, I am ready to die.”
text: Ni Israel kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ni mina ohĩe ake̱ oye̱ dšeṅ lolo ne̱, hã magbo.

ref: Then Joseph said to his brothers and to everyone who had come with them: I must go and tell the king that you have arrived from Canaan.
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ enyemimei le̱ kē̱ etše̱ we le̱ ake̱: Miya ni miyagba Farao ake̱: Minyemimei ke̱ mitše̱ we le̱ ni yo̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱ eba miṅõ;

ref: When he does, be sure to say, “We are shepherds. Our families have always raised sheep.” If you tell him this, he will let you settle in the region of Goshen. Joseph wanted them to say this to the king, because the Egyptians did not like to be around anyone who raised sheep.
text: nyekē̱ale̱ ake̱: Otšũdši lè̱o̱ koloi kē̱-dše̱ wo̱blahĩaṅ ke̱-baši ṅme̱ne̱, wo̱ke̱ wo̱tše̱mei le̱ fẽ; koni ehã nyehĩ Gošen šikpoṅ le̱ no̱. Edšake̱ Mizraimbii *nyãṅeo̱ mofẽmo ní dši kolokwe̱lo̱ *

ref: But in our country all the pastures are dried up, and our sheep have no grass to eat. So we, your servants, have come here. Please let us live in the region of Goshen.”
text: Ni ame̱sā ame̱kē̱ Farao akē̱: Gbo̱tō wo̱ba ye̱ šikpoṅ ne̱ no̱, edšake̱ otšũdši le̱ akoloi le̱ náá niyelihe; edšake̱ hõmo̱ le̱ nā ewa ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱; nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ wo̱mīkpao fai ni ohā otšũdši ahĩ Gošen šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: The king said to Joseph, “It's good that your father and brothers have arrived.
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef akē̱: Otše̱ ke̱ onyemimei le̱ eba oṅõ ne̱;

ref: I will let them live anywhere they choose in the land of Egypt, but I suggest that they settle in Goshen, the best part of our land. I would also like for your finest shepherds to watch after my own sheep and goats.”
text: Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ kã ohĩe; šikpoṅ le̱, beni hĩ fè fẽ ni dši Gošen šikpoṅ le̱, hã otše̱ ke̱ onyemimei le̱ ahĩ dše̱i; ni ke̱dši ole ame̱teṅ meikomei ṅí dši gbomei ni sa le̱, no le̱ ṅõame̱ ofe oṅukpai ye̱ mikoloi le̱ ano̱.

ref: and the king asked him, “How old are you?”
text: Ni Farao bi Iakob ake̱: Afii enyie oye?

ref: The famine was bad everywhere in Egypt and Canaan, and the people were suffering terribly.
text: Ši niyenii be̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ hekoheko; edšake̱ hõmo̱ le̱ nā ewa naakpa, ni Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ke̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ mīgboi ke̱ hõmo̱.

ref: So Joseph sold them the grain that had been stored up, and he put the money in the king's treasury.
text: Ni Iosef bua šika fẽ ni ana ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ke̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ni ame̱ke̱-bahe ṅmā̃ le̱ nā, ni Iosef ke̱ šika le̱ ba Farao we le̱.

ref: But when everyone had run out of money, the Egyptians came to Joseph and demanded, “Give us more grain! If you don't, we'll soon be dead, because our money's all gone.”
text: Ni heni šika tã ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ke̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱; Mizraimbii fẽ ba Iosef ṅõ ni ame̱hakē̱: Hãwo̱ nii wo̱ye! ši mẽhewo̱ ni ookwé̱ ni wo̱o̱gboi, ake̱-ní we̱šika etã hewo̱ le̱?

ref: “If you don't have any money,” Joseph answered, “give me your animals, and I'll let you have some grain.”
text: Ni Iosef ké̱: Ni nyešika etã ne̱, nyeṅõa nyekoloi nyehãa, ni mahãnye ye̱ nyekoloi le̱ anā.

ref: Then the people came to him and said: Sir, there's no way we can hide the truth from you. We are broke, and we don't have any more animals. We have nothing left except ourselves and our land.
text: Beni nakai afi le̱ ho le̱, ame̱ba eṅṍ ye̱ afi ni dši enyo̱ le̱ no̱, ni ame̱bakē̱le̱ ake̱: Wo̱ṅõ-tēṅ wo̱nuṅtšo̱ ake̱ wo̱šika ke̱ koloi kui le̱ fẽ etã, ni ake̱-ba wo̱nuṅtšo̱ ṅõ; eſe̱ko nokonoko ye̱ wo̱nuṅtšo̱ hĩe doṅṅ, ake̱ dša wo̱gbomotšei ke̱ wo̱šikpõdši.

ref: Don't let us starve and our land be ruined. If you'll give us grain to eat and seed to plant, we'll sell ourselves and our land to the king. We'll become his slaves.
text: Mẽhewo̱ ookwe̱ ni wo̱o̱gboi, wo̱ke̱ wo̱šikpoṅ? Nõ niyenii ohewo̱ ke̱ wo̱šikpoṅ le̱, koni wo̱ke̱ wo̱šikpoṅ le̱ atšṍmo̱ Farao nyṍdši, ni ohãwo̱ dũmo̱nii, koni wo̱yi aná wala, ni wo̱kagboi, ni wo̱šikpoṅ le̱ mīhatšṍ amaṅfõ.

ref: The famine became so severe that Joseph finally bought every piece of land in Egypt for the king
text: Ni Iosef he Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ fẽ ehã Farao; edšake̱ Mizraimnyo-fẽ-Mizraimnyo hõ ešikpoṅ, edšake̱ hõmo̱ le̱ efèame̱; bo̱ni fe ni šikpoṅ le̱ tšṍ Farao nõ ne̱.

ref: except the priests. The king gave the priests a regular food allowance, so they did not have to sell their land.
text: Dša oso̱foi le̱ ašikpoṅ le̱ ke̱ke̱ ni ehee; edšake̱ oso̱foi le̱ ye̱ gbe̱nä ko ni Farao hãa-ame̱, ni ame̱gbe̱nã ni Farao hãa-ame̱ le̱ ame̱yeo̱; nohewo̱ le̱ ame̱hõõ ame̱šikpoṅ le̱.

ref: Then Joseph said to the people, “You and your land now belong to the king. I'm giving you seed to plant,
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Iosef kē̱ maṅ le̱ ake̱: Na, mihenye ke̱ nyešikpoṅ le̱ ṅme̱ne̱ gbĩ ne̱ mihã Farao; na, nyedũmo̱nii ne̱, ní nyedũ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.

ref: Then Joseph made a law that one fifth of the harvest would always belong to the king. Only the priests did not lose their land. Jacob Becomes an Old Man
text: Ni Iosef ṅõ ene̱ efe akpo̱ efõ ši ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ke̱-baši ṅme̱ne̱, ake̱ Farao aṅõ mlidšãi enumo̱ mli ekome, dša oso̱foi le̱ ašikpoṅ le̱ ke̱ke̱ tšṍṍ Farao nõ.

ref: The people of Israel made their home in the land of Goshen, where they became prosperous and had large families.
text: Ni Israel hĩ Mizraim, ye̱ Gošen šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni ame̱náná šikpṍdši ye̱ no̱, ni ame̱fo̱fo̱i, ni *ame̱yi fá babaō. *

ref: Instead, bury me in the place where my ancestors are buried.” “I will do what you have asked,” Joseph answered.
text: ši ké̱ mike̱ mitše̱mei le̱ yawo̱ le̱, owómi ke̱-dše Mizraim, ni oyafũmi ye̱ *amefũhe le̱. Ni eké̱: Mafe bo̱ni okē̱ le̱.

ref: “Will you give me your word?” Jacob asked. “Yes, I will,” Joseph promised. After this, Jacob bowed down and prayed at the head of his bed.
text: Ni eké̱: Kãmi kitã! Ni ekále̱ kitã. Ni Israel mã eyi ši ye̱ sātšo le̱ yiteṅ.

ref: When Joseph arrived, someone told Jacob, “Your son Joseph has come to see you.” Jacob sat up in bed, but it took almost all his strength.
text: Ni ayakē̱ Iakob akē̱: Na, obi Iosef mība oṅõ. Ni Israel wadše ehe ete ši etá sātšo le̱ no̱.

ref: Jacob told Joseph: God All-Powerful appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, where he gave me his blessing
text: Ni Iakob kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Mawu Ofe le̱ dšie ehe kpo etšṍmi ye̱ *Luz ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni edšō̱mi, *

ref: Unfortunately, your mother Rachel died in Canaan after we had left northern Syria and before we reached Bethlehem. And I had to bury her along the way.
text: Ši mi le̱, beni midše̱ Mesopotamia mihaä le̱, *Rahel gbo ye̱ mihe ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ye̱ gbe̱ teṅ, beni eſe̱ gbe̱ kukuō ko ni aše̱o̱ Efrat le̱, ni mifũle̱ ye̱ Efrat ni dši Betlehem le̱ gbe̱ le̱ mase̱i. *

ref: Then Joseph made his sons move away from Jacob's knees, and Joseph bowed down in front of him with his face to the ground.
text: Ni Iosef dšieame̱ ke̱-dše̱ enakutšei ateṅ ke̱-ba, ni eho ekoto ši, eṅõ ehĩe ebu ši.

ref: But before Jacob gave them his blessing, he crossed his arms, putting his right hand on the head of Ephraim and his left hand on the head of Manasseh.
text: Ši Israel kpã eninedšūrõ mli ni eṅõṅmẽ Efraim ni dši gbekẽ le̱ yiteṅ, ni eṅõ ebe̱ku eṅmẽ Manase yiteṅ, ni ehĩe kã he ni eto enidši le̱ nakai; ši Manase dši kromo̱bi.

ref: Then he gave Joseph his blessing and said: My grandfather Abraham and my father Isaac worshiped the Lord God. He has been with me all my life,
text: Ni edšō̱ Iosef ni eke̱: Nyoṅmo̱, mo hĩe ni *mitše̱mei, Abraham ke̱ Isak, nyie le̱, Nyoṅmo̱ ní dši †mikwe̱lo̱ ke̱-dše̱ be-yino ni miyo̱ ke-baši ṅmene̱ ne̱, *

ref: and his angel has kept me safe. Now I pray that he will bless these boys and that my name and the names of Abraham and Isaac will live on because of them. I ask God to give them many children and many descendants as well.
text: *bo̱fo le̱ ni kpõmi ke̱-dše efõṅ fẽ mli le̱, adšō̱ ghekẽbii ne̱, ni ehã aṅõ migbe̱i ke̱ mitše̱mei, Abraham ke̱ Isak, gbe̱ii atšé̱ame̱, ni ame̱yi afá ni ame̱tšṍme̱ mei babaō ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱. *

ref: Joseph said, “Father, you have made a mistake. This is the older boy. Put your right hand on him.”
text: Ni Iosef kē̱ etšē̱ ake̱: Dšee nakai ni, atā; ši mone̱ dši kromo̱bi; ṅõ oninedšũrõ oṅmẽ eyiteṅ.

ref: But his father said, “Son, I know what I am doing. It's true that Manasseh's family will someday become a great nation. But Ephraim will be even greater than Manasseh, because his descendants will become many great nations.”
text: Ni etše ekplẽẽ ni ekē̱: Mile, mibi, mile; le̱ hũ ehatšṍ maṅ, ebatšṍ kpeteṅkple moṅ; ši kẽle̱ enyemi gbekẽ le̱ batšṍ kpeteṅkple fèle̱, ni eseši aatšṍ mãdši babaō.

ref: Jacob told him that in the future the people of Israel would ask God's blessings on one another by saying, “I pray for God to bless you as much as he blessed Ephraim and Manasseh.” Jacob put Ephraim's name first to show that he would be greater than Manasseh.
text: Ni edšō̱ame̱ nakai gbĩ le̱, ni ekē̱: Bo, Israel aaṅõ-dšō̱ akē̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ahã otšṍ tamo̱ Efraim ke̱ Manasel Ni eṅõ Efraim eto Manase hĩe. Heb.

ref: After that, Jacob said, “Joseph, you can see that I won't live much longer. But God will be with you and will lead you back to the land he promised our family long ago.
text: Ni Israel kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Na, mi le̱ mīgbo, ši Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱nye ahì ši, ní eke̱nye akũ se̱ aya nyetše̱mei ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ekoṅ.

ref: You will tie your donkey to a choice grapevine and wash your clothes in wine from those grapes.
text: Eefĩ etedšibi eṅmõ weintšo ke̱ etedšiyō bi hũ eṅmõ weintšonine; eke̱ wein aafo̱ etade he, ni ehebo le̱ eke̱ weinyibii amli lá aafo̱ he. Ioel

ref: Your eyes are darker than wine, your teeth whiter than milk.
text: Ehĩṅme̱ii tšuo̱ fō̱ ke̱ wein, ni enyã-nyõdši ye̱o̱ futā ke̱ fufo̱.

ref: Issachar, you are a strong donkey resting in the meadows.
text: Isakar le̱, tedši ni wuiaṅ esa dšile̱, ni ebuo̱ ši ye̱ koloi atšũi ateṅ;

ref: You found them so pleasant that you worked too hard and became a slave.
text: ni ena ake̱ hedšō̱mo̱ ṅõ, ni Šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ bũ ye̱ fe̱o̱; ni eba ekõṅ ši etẽre, ni etšṍ tšũlo̱ ni tšuo̱ onia.

ref: You are a snake that bites the heel of a horse, making its rider fall.
text: Ni Dan aatšṍ onũfũ ni ble gbe̱ no̱, onãṅkã ni kã tempõṅ no̱, ni kõo̱ okpo̱ṅo̱ nanetšitši, ni eno̱tálo̱ le̱ kpaä gbēo̱ ese̱.

ref: Our Lord, I am waiting for you to save us.
text: Iehowa, owalaheremo̱ le̱ gbe̱ mikwe̱o̱ ne̱! Lal.

ref: Naphtali, you are a wild deer with lovely fawns.
text: Naftali le̱, ofrote ni he yo̱ oyá ni, ni ewieo̱ wiemo̱i ke̱ ṅõmo̱i. Kods.

ref: Joseph, you are a fruitful vine growing near a stream and climbing a wall.
text: Iosef le̱, tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, nu ni baä he tšo ni woo̱ yibii ni, ni enidši le̱ kwō̱lo̱ö̱ gbogbo. Hos.

ref: But you stood your ground, swiftly shooting back with the help of Jacob's God, the All-Powerful One— his name is the Shepherd, Israel's mighty rock.
text: Ši egãi le̱ emli wa kpẽṅṅ, ni eni-dši ke̱ ekõdši ahe ye̱ oyá, edše̱ Iakob kãkãlo̱ le̱ deṅ, edše̱ tōkwe̱lo̱ ní dši Israel te̱sā le̱ gbe̱i amli,

ref: My son, the blessings I give are better than the promise of ancient mountains or eternal hills. Joseph, I pray these blessings will come to you, because you are the leader of your brothers.
text: Otše̱ dšō̱mo̱i le̱ teke miblemabii adšō̱mo̱i le̱, ke̱-yaši nāno̱ goṅkpo̱i le̱ ahũsũi ano̱ tõ; ame̱aba Iosef yiteṅ, ke̱ le̱ ni dši enyemimei le̱ ateṅ le̱ *Nazirnyo le̱ yiteṅ pampamso! *

ref: Benjamin, you are a fierce wolf, destroying your enemies morning and evening.
text: Benyamin le̱, klaṅ ni yo̱ hahāha ni; eeye emĩle̱lo lebi, ni gbe̱ke̱ le̱ eedša nomṅõnii le̱ amli. Kods.

ref: These are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is how Jacob gave each of them their proper blessings. Jacob's Death
text: Mene̱mei fẽ dši Israel akutšei nyoṅma ke̱ enyo̱ le̱, ni noni ame̱tše̱ ke̱ame̱ wie ni eṅõ-dšō̱ame̱ ne̱; dšō̱mo̱ ni sa mofẽmo le̱, no eke̱-dšō̱le̱.

ref: Both the cave and the land that goes with it were bought from the Hittites.
text: Abō̱ le̱ ke̱ te̱kplo ni yo̱ mli le̱ ahe ye̱ Hetbii le̱ adeṅ.

ref: When Jacob had finished giving these instructions to his sons, he lay down on his bed and died.
text: Ni beni Iakob ewo ebii le̱ akitã egbe nā le̱, ebua enãdši anā eſïe sā le̱ no̱, ni eṅõ emumo̱ ehã, ni abua enā aši emei le̱ ano̱.

ref: Joseph started crying, then leaned over to hug and kiss his father.
text: Ni Iosef ho bu etše̱ hĩe, ni efó *eſĩe eno̱, ni eſo̱ enā. *

ref: When the time of mourning was over, Joseph said to the Egyptian leaders, “If you consider me your friend, please speak to the king for me.
text: Ni beni efómo̱ gbĩĩ le̱ ebo le̱, Iosef ke̱ Farao we le̱ bii le̱ wie ni ekē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ke̱dši mihĩe eba nyam ye̱ nyehĩe le̱, mīkpanye fai, nyewoa Farao toi no̱ ake.

ref: The king answered, “Go to Canaan and keep your promise to your father.”
text: Ni Farao kē̱: Ya ni oyafũ otše̱, take̱ bo̱ni ehā okã kitã le̱.

ref: After crossing the Jordan River, Joseph stopped at Atad's threshing place, where they all mourned and wept seven days for Jacob.
text: Ni beni ame̱še̱ Atad ablešāhe le̱ ni yo̱ Iordan se̱ le̱, ame̱fe yăra kpeteṅkple ni nā wa hũ ye̱ dše̱i; ni eye etše̱ be ṅko̱mo̱ gbĩĩ kpawo.

ref: So Jacob's sons did just as their father had instructed.
text: Ni ebii le̱ fe bo̱ni *ewoame kitã le̱ amghãle̱; *

ref: After the funeral, Joseph, his brothers, and everyone else returned to Egypt. Joseph's Promise to His Brothers
text: Ni beni Iosef efũ etše̱ se̱ le̱, eke̱ enyemimei le̱ ke̱ mei fẽ ni ke̱le̱ te etše̱ fũ le̱ kũ ame̱se̱ ke̱-te Mizraim ekoṅ.

ref: After Jacob died, Joseph's brothers said to each other, “What if Joseph still hates us and wants to get even with us for all the cruel things we did to him?”
text: Ni beni Iosef nyemimei le̱ na ake̱ ame̱tše̱ egbo le̱, ame̱kē̱: Ekole̱ Iosef ebè̱wo̱ ewo emli, ni eebawowo̱ efõṅ fẽ ni wo̱fele̱ le̱ he nyõmo̱.

ref: So they sent this message to Joseph: Before our father died,
text: Ni ame̱hã ayakē̱ Iosef ake̱: Otše̱ wo kitã dãni egbo ake̱:

ref: he told us, “You did some cruel and terrible things to Joseph, but you must ask him to forgive you.” Now we ask you to please forgive the terrible things we did. After all, we serve the same God that your father worshiped. When Joseph heard this, he started crying.
text: Bo̱ni nyekē̱ Iosef ne̱: Mīkpao fai ni oke̱ onyemimei le̱ ano̱tõmo̱ ke̱ ame̱be eša le̱ afaame̱; ši ame̱feo efõṅ. Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ wo̱ṅkpao fai ní oṅõ otše̱ Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ tšũdši le̱ ano̱tõmo̱ le̱ ofaame̱! Ni beni ame̱ke̱ Iosef wie nakai le̱, efó.

ref: Right then, Joseph's brothers came and bowed down to the ground in front of him and said, “We are your slaves.”
text: Ni enyemimei le̱ hũ te ame̱yabumo̱ ši ye̱ ehĩe ni ame̱kē̱: Na, otšũdši dšiwo̱!

ref: You tried to harm me, but God made it turn out for the best, so that he could save all these people, as he is now doing.
text: Nye le̱, nyesusumi efõṅ; ši *Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, esusule̱ ekpakpa, beni afe ni ahere mei babaõ yiwala take̱ bo̱ni eba mli ùmene ne.

ref: Don't be afraid! I will take care of you and your children.” After Joseph said this, his brothers felt much better. Joseph's Death
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ nyekašea gbeyei! malè̱nye ke̱ nyebii le̱. Ni eše̱dše ame̱mĩ, ni eke̱ame̱ wie tšuidšũrõ nā.

ref: Joseph lived long enough to see Ephraim's children and grandchildren. He also lived to see the children of Manasseh's son Machir, and he welcomed them into his family.
text: Ni Iosef na Efraim nãnãkãnsowai; ni Manase bi Makir bii le̱ hũ afo̱ame̱ *aſie Iosef ſuo̱ no̱. *

ref: Now promise me that you will take my body with you when God leads you to that land.”
text: Ni Iosef hã Israelbii le̱ kãle̱ kitã ni ekē̱: Sramo̱ Nyoṅmo̱ aabasranye kẽ, ni nyeṅõ miwui le̱ ke-dše bie̱.

ref: After Joseph, his brothers, and everyone else in that generation had died,
text: Ni *Iosef ke̱ enyemimei le̱ fẽ ke̱ nakai yino̱bii le̱ fẽ gboi. *

ref: Many years later a new king came to power. He did not know what Joseph had done for Egypt,
text: Agbene maṅtše̱ hẽ ko bate ši ye̱ Mizraim ní lee Iosef.

ref: and he told the Egyptians: There are too many of those Israelites in our country, and they are becoming more powerful than we are.
text: Ni eke emaṅbii le̱ ake: Nyekwe̱a, Israelbii amaṅ le̱ yi efá ni ame̱ná hewale̱ hũ fèwo̱.

ref: If we don't outsmart them, their families will keep growing larger. And if our country goes to war, they could easily fight on the side of our enemies and escape from Egypt.
text: Nyebaä, nyehãa woke̱ame̱ ayea ṅā mli, koni ame̱yi akafá ní ebaba mli ake, ké̱ ta ko bate ši le̱, ni ame̱ hũ ame̱yafata wo̱henye̱lo̱i le̱ ahe ní ame̱ke̱wo̱ ṅwu ní ame̱mīfã ke̱-ṅdše šikpoṅ le̱ no̱!

ref: But even though the Israelites were mistreated, their families grew larger, and they took over more land. Because of this, the Egyptians feared them worse than before
text: Ši babaō ní ame̱waäame̱ yi le̱, babaō ní ame̱yi fáa ní ame̱ſereo̱ dši no, ni ame̱tšui suo̱ Israelbii le̱ ano̱.

ref: and made them work so hard
text: Ni Mizraimbii le̱ hĩe Israelbii le̱ nyṍdšiahĩemo̱ še̱ī,

ref: that their lives were miserable. The Egyptians were cruel to the people of Israel and forced them to make bricks and to mix mortar and to work in the fields.
text: ni nyṍdšiahĩemo̱ še̱ī ní ame̱ke̱-hĩe-ame̱ ye̱ sũ ke̱ ntaya femo̱ ke̱ ṅmõši ni-tšumo̱ sro̱toi fẽ mli le̱, ha ame̱dšeṅhĩle̱ dše ame̱tšine.

ref: Finally, the king called in Shiphrah and Puah, the two women who helped the Hebrew mothers when they gave birth.
text: Ni Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ kē̱ Hebriyei-fo̱yei, ní ame̱teṅ mokome gbe̱i dši Šifra ní mokome hũ gbei dši Pua le̱,

ref: He told them, “If a Hebrew woman gives birth to a girl, let the child live. If the baby is a boy, kill him!”
text: ake: Ké̱ nyete Hebriyei le̱ afo̱me̱ ní nyena ye̱ fomo-sẽi le̱ no̱ ake binūṅ le̱, nyegbeale̱, ši ke̱dši biyōṅ le̱, ehĩ ši.

ref: But the two women were faithful to God and did not kill the boys, even though the king had told them to.
text: Ši yeifo̱yei le̱ yaše Nyoṅmo̱ gbeyei, ni ame̱fee boni Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ kē̱ame̱ le̱, ši ame̱here gbekẽbiihĩĩ le̱ ayiwala.

ref: The king called them in again and asked, “Why are you letting those baby boys live?”
text: Ni Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ tšé̱ yeife̱yei le̱ ni ekē̱ame̱ ake: Mẽhewo̱ nyefe nakai ni nyehere gbekẽbiihĩĩ le̱ ayiwala le̱?

ref: They answered, “Hebrew women have their babies much quicker than Egyptian women. By the time we arrive, their babies are already born.”
text: Ni yeife̱yei le̱ kē̱ Farao ake̱: Edšake̱ Hebriyei le̱ tamo̱o̱ Mizraimyei, ši ame̱ le̱, ame̱ye̱ kamkam, ni beni yōfo̱yō le̱ kē̱ eše̱o̱ ame̱ṅõ le̱, ame̱fo̱ momo.

ref: God was good to the two women because they truly respected him, and he blessed them with children of their own. The Hebrews kept increasing
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ ní yeife̱yei le̱ še Nyoṅme̱ gbeyei hewo le̱, ehã ame̱ſere.

ref: until finally, the king gave a command to everyone in the nation, “As soon as a Hebrew boy is born, throw him into the Nile River! But you can let the girls live.”
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Farao wo emaṅbii le̱ fẽ akpo ake̱: Binū-fẽ-binū ní aafo̱ le̱, nyeya-še̱ale̱ nyefoa fā le̱ mli, ši biyō-fe-biyō le̱, nyehãa eyi anaa wala!

ref: and she later had a baby boy. He was a beautiful child, and she kept him inside for three months.
text: Ni yō le̱ ná mũsũ, ni efo̱ binū; ni beni ena ake̱ *ehe ye̱ fe̱o le̱, eṅole̱ etē nyõdši etẽ. *Bo̱f.

ref: The baby's older sister stood off at a distance to see what would happen to him.
text: Ni *enyemiyō damo̱ se̱ fle̱ṅṅ ēkwe̱ noni aafele̱. *

ref: About that time one of the king's daughters came down to take a bath in the river, while her servant women walked along the river bank. She saw the basket in the tall grass and sent one of them to pull it out of the water.
text: Ni Farao biyō le̱ ba ebedšū ye̱ fā le̱ nā, ni eweyei le̱ mīšĕra ši ye̱ fā le̱ toi; ni ena adeka le̱ ye̱ gălãi le̱ amli, ni etšũ eweyō yawó ke̱-ba.

ref: When the king's daughter opened the basket, she saw the baby crying and felt sorry for him. She said, “This must be one of the Hebrew babies.”
text: Ni beni egble nā le̱, ena gbekẽ le̱, ni na, gbekẽnū le̱ mīfo. Ni emũsũṅ tšõle ye̱ ehewo̱ ni ekē̱: Hebribii le̱ abihĩĩ le̱ eko ne̱.

ref: “Yes,” the king's daughter answered. So the girl brought the baby's mother,
text: Ni Farao biyō le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ya! Ni oblayō le̱ te eyatšé̱ gbekẽ le̱ nye ke̱-ba.

ref: and the king's daughter told her, “Take care of this child, and I will pay you.” The baby's mother carried him home and took care of him.
text: Ni Farao biyō le̱ kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ṅõ gbekẽ ne̱ ke̱-ya, ni oyahãle̱ fufo̱ ohãmi, ni mawoö nyõmo. Ni yō le̱ ṅõ gbekẽ le̱ ni eyahãle̱ fufo̱.

ref: Moses looked around to see if anyone was watching, then he killed the Egyptian and hid his body in the sand.
text: Ni etšṍ ehe ekwe̱ bie̱ ke̱ bie̱, ni beni ena ake̱ moko be̱ dše̱i le, etſa Mizraimnyo le̱ nõ, ni eṅõle̱ etē šia mli. Bo̱f.

ref: the seven daughters of Jethro, the priest of Midian, came up to water their father's sheep and goats.
text: Agbene *Midian oso̱fo le̱ ye̱ biyei kpawo; ni ameba amebagbla nu ame̱wo nunumo̱-ahĩmai le̱ obõbõ, ní ame̱tše̱ tōi le̱ anu. *

ref: Some shepherds tried to chase them away, but Moses came to their rescue and watered their animals.
text: Ši tōkwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba baſieame̱; ni Mose te ši, ni efã amehe, ni *ehã ametōi le̱ anu. *

ref: They answered, “An Egyptian rescued us from the shepherds, and he even watered our sheep and goats.”
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Mizraimnyo ko dšiewo̱ ye̱ tōkwe̱lo̱i le̱ adeṅ, ni esā egbla nu le̱ tete ehãwo̱, ni ehã tōi le̱ anu.

ref: “Where is he?” Jethro asked. “Why did you leave him out there? Invite him to eat with us.”
text: Ni ekē̱ ebiyei le̱ ake̱: Ni nẽgbe̱ eyo? Mẽhewo̱ ni nyešĩ nū le̱? Nyeya-tšé̱ale̱ ke̱-baä, koni ebaye nii!

ref: Moses agreed to stay on with Jethro, who later let his daughter Zipporah marry Moses.
text: Ni Mose kplẽ nū le̱ ke̱ šihĩle̱; ni eno ebiyō Zipora ehã Mose.

ref: And when she had a son, Moses said, “I will name him Gershom, since I am a foreigner in this country.”
text: Ni efo̱ binū, ni Mose wole̱ Geršom; edšake̱ ekē̱: Mibato gbo̱ ye̱ maṅse̱ šikpoṅ no̱.

ref: After the death of the king of Egypt, the Israelites still complained because they were forced to be slaves. They cried out for help,
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ nakai gbĩĩ pī le̱ amli le̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ bagbo, ni Israelbii le̱ dõmo̱ ntšo̱i ye̱ nyṍṅyeli le̱ hewo, ni ame̱fó ame̱tšé̱ Nyoṅmo̱; ni ame-nyṍṅyeli le̱ mli fómo̱-ke̱-tšémo̱ le̱ *yase̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ṅõ. *

ref: and God heard their loud cries. He did not forget the promise he had made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob,
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ nu ame̱ṅke̱mo̱yeli blo̱mo̱ le̱, ni Nyoṅmo̱ kai *ekpãṅmo̱ ní eke̱ Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob kpãṅ le̱; *

ref: and because he knew what was happening to his people, he felt sorry for them.
text: ni Nyoṅmo̱ kwe̱ Israelbii le̱, ni Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱ ame̱yi te̱.

ref: The Lord said: I have seen how my people are suffering as slaves in Egypt, and I have heard them beg for my help because of the way they are being mistreated. I feel sorry for them,
text: *Ni Iehowa kē̱: Na mina amanehũlũ ní mimaṅ ní yo̱ Mizraim le̱ naä le̱, ni *ame̱blo̱mo̱ ní ame̱blo̱ö̱ ye̱ ame̱kplalo̱i le̱ ahĩe le̱ minu, edšake̱ mile ame̱nidō̱nii le̱. *

ref: My people have begged for my help, and I have seen how cruel the Egyptians are to them.
text: Ni agbene, na, Israelbii le̱ ablo̱mo̱ le̱ eše̱ miṅõ, ni misā mina haomo̱ ní Mizraimbii haoo̱ame̱ le̱;

ref: Moses answered, “I will tell the people of Israel that the God their ancestors worshiped has sent me to them. But what should I say, if they ask me your name?”
text: Ni Mose kē̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ake̱: Na, mi le̱, kē̱ mite Israelbii le̱ aṅõ, ni mikē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyetše̱mei a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ tšũmi nyeṅõ ne̱, ni ame̱abimi ake̱: Té egbe̱iṅ? le̱, té mikē̱amē̱ tẽṅ?

ref: Call together the leaders of Israel and tell them that the God who was worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Tell them I have seen how terribly they are being treated in Egypt,
text: Ya ní oyabua Israel onukpai le̱ anā, ni okē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa, nyetše̱mei a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, ní dši Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, edšie ehe kpo etšõmi, ni ekē̱: Sramo̱ mibasranye, ni mina noni afeo̱nye ye̱ Mizraim le̱;

ref: and I promise to lead them out of their troubles. I will give them a land rich with milk and honey, where the Canaanites, Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites now live.
text: ni mikē̱ ake̱: Madšienye ke̱-dše Mizraim amanehũlũ le̱ mli ke-ya Kanaanbii ke̱ Hitbii ke̱ Amorbii ke̱ Perizbii ke̱ Hivbii ke̱ Iebusbii le̱ ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱, šikpoṅ ní fufo̱ ke̱ wò hoo ye̱ no̱ le̱.

ref: The leaders of Israel will listen to you. Then you must take them to the king of Egypt and say, “The Lord God of the Hebrews has appeared to us. Let us walk three days into the desert, where we can offer a sacrifice to him.”
text: Ni ame̱abo ogbē toi, ni oke̱ Israel onukpai le̱ aya Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ ṅõ, ni nyeyakē̱le̱ ake̱: *Iehowa, Hebribii a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ ke̱wo̱ ebakpe; nohewo̱ le̱ wo̱mīkpao fai, hã wo̱nyie gbĩĩ etẽ gbe̱ ke-ya ṅã le̱ no̱, koni wo̱yašã afo̱le we̱hã Iehowa, wo̱-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱. *

ref: But I know that the king of Egypt won't let you go unless something forces him to.
text: Ši mi le̱ mile ake̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ eṅmẽṅnye gbe̱ ní nyeya, dša ninešiwale̱ nā.

ref: So I will use my mighty power to perform all kinds of miracles and strike down the Egyptians. Then the king will send you away.
text: Ni makpã minine mli, ni mike̱ minākpe̱nii fẽ ní mafe ye̱ dše̱i le̱ atſia Mizraim nii, ni no se̱ dãni eehã nyeya.

ref: On the way the Lord said to Moses: When you get to Egypt, go to the king and work the miracles I have shown you. But I will make him so stubborn that he will refuse to let my people go.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Ke̱ okũ ose̱ ote Mizraim le̱, kwe̱ ní ofe nākpe̱-nii le̱ fẽ ní miṅõ-wo odeṅ le̱ ye̱ Farao hĩe; ši *mi le̱ mawadše etsui, ni ehãṅ maṅ le aya. *

ref: Then tell him that I have said, “Israel is my first-born son,
text: Ni okē̱ Farao ake̱: Bo̱ni Iehowa kē̱ ne̱; *Mibi, mikromo̱bi dši Israel; *Ier.

ref: and I commanded you to release him, so he could worship me. But you refused, and now I will kill your first-born son.” Zipporah's Son Is Circumcised
text: ni mīkē̱o ake̱: Hã mibi le̱ aya, ni eyasúmo̱mi; ši ke̱dši okplẽẽ ní eya le̱, no le, na, *magbe obi, okromo̱bi le̱! *

ref: One night while Moses was in camp, the Lord was about to kill him.
text: Ni eba mli ye̱ gbe̱ le̱ no̱ ye̱ šitōhe le̱ ake̱ Iehowa bahẽṅle̱, ni ētaole̱ egbe.

ref: But Zipporah circumcised her son with a flint knife. She touched his legs with the skin she had cut off and said, “My dear son, this blood will protect you.”
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Zipora ko̱ *te̱ ní nā ba, ni eṅõ-fo ebi le̱ ketia, ni eše̱ efõ enãdši anā ni ekē̱: Le̱le̱ṅ ake̱ lá ayemforowu dšio hãmi. *Ios.

ref: So the Lord did not harm Moses. Then Zipporah said, “Yes, my dear, you are safe because of this circumcision.” Aaron Is Sent To Meet Moses
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni eṅmē̃ ehe. Ketiafo le̱ hewo le̱ ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Lá ayemforowu dšio le̱.

ref: The Lord sent Aaron to meet Moses in the desert. So Aaron met Moses at Mount Sinai and greeted him with a kiss.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Aaron ake̱: Ya ní oke̱ Mose ayakpe ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱. Ni ete ní eke̱le̱ yakpe ye̱ Nyoṅmo̱ goṅ le̱ he, ni eſo̱ enā.

ref: Moses told Aaron what God had sent him to say; he also told him about the miracles God had given him the power to perform.
text: Ni Mose dšadse Iehowa wiemo̱i fẽ ní eke̱-tšũle̱ le̱ ke̱ okadii fẽ ní efã́le̱ ake̱ efe le̱ etšõ Aaron.

ref: Later they brought together the leaders of Israel,
text: Ni Mose ke̱ Aaron te, ni ame̱yabua Israelbii anukpai le̱ fẽ nā.

ref: and Aaron told them what the Lord had sent Moses to say. Then Moses worked the miracles for the people,
text: Ni Aaron dšadše wiemo̱i fẽ ní Iehowa ke̱ Mose ewie le̱, ni efe okadii le̱ yẽ maṅ le̱ hĩe.

ref: and everyone believed. They bowed down and worshiped the Lord because they knew that he had seen their suffering and was going to help them.
text: Ni maṅ le̱ he ye. Ni heni ame̱nu ake̱ Iehowa *ebasra Israelbii le̱ ni ena ame̱manehũlũ le̱, ke̱ke̱ ni ame̱mãme̱ ame̱yitšei aši ni ame̱so̱le. *

ref: “Who is this Lord and why should I obey him?” the king replied. “I refuse to let you and your people go!”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ ake̱: Namo *dši Iehowa le̱ ni mabo egbē toi ní mahã Israel aya? Milee Iehowa, ni Israel hũ mihãã ame̱ya. *Dan.

ref: They answered, “The Lord God of the Hebrews has appeared to us. Please let us walk three days into the desert where we can offer sacrifices to him. If you don't, he may strike us down with terrible troubles or with war.”
text: Ni ame̱kē̱: Hebribii a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱ ke̱wo̱ ebakpe; nohewo̱ le̱ wo̱mīkpao fai ní ohã wo̱ya ni wo̱yanyie gbĩĩ etẽ gbe̱ ke̱-ya ṅã le̱ no̱, ni wo̱yašã afole wo̱hã Iehowa, wo̱-Nyoṅme̱ le̱, koni ekabatuawo̱ ke̱ gbĕle-hela la klante.

ref: The king said, “Moses and Aaron, why are you keeping these people from working? Look how many you are keeping from doing their work. Now everyone get back to work!”
text: Ni Farao sā kē̱: Na, agbe̱ne̱ maṅ le̱ *yi efa momo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni nyehã ame̱dšo̱ö̱ amehe noṅṅ ne̱! *

ref: That same day the king gave orders to his Egyptian slave bosses and to the Israelite men directly in charge of the Israelite slaves. He told them:
text: Ni nakai gbĩ le̱ no̱ noṅṅ le̱ Farao wo maṅ le̱ kplalo̱i ke̱ ame̱no̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ akitã ake̱:

ref: Don't give the slaves any more straw to put in their bricks. Force them to find their own straw wherever they can,
text: Nyekahãa maṅ le̱ dſe̱i ame̱ṅõ-fe ntayāi le̱ doṅṅ tamo̱ tšutšu le̱; ame̱dientše̱ ameya ní ame̱yabuabua dſe̱i anā ame̱hã amehe;

ref: but they must make the same number of bricks as before. They are lazy, or else they would not beg me to let them go and sacrifice to their God.
text: ši ntayāi le̱ ayikule̱ ní amefe tšutšu le̱, nyehãa ame̱fea nakai noṅṅ, nyekatšea no̱; ši ame̱mīfe anihao, nohewo̱ ni ame̱blo̱ö̱ ake̱: Hã wo̱ya ní wo̱yašã afole wo̱hã wo̱-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱!

ref: Make them work so hard that they won't have time to listen to these lies.
text: Ahã nitšumo̱ le̱ anyẽ gbomei le̱, koni ato̱ame̱ ní ame̱kabo yakawiemo̱i atoi!

ref: The slave bosses and the men in charge of the slaves went out and told them, “The king says he will not give you any more straw.
text: Ni maṅ le̱ kplalo̱i le̱ ke̱ ame̱ne̱kwe̱loi le̱ dše kpo ni ame̱yakē̱ maṅ le̱ ake̱: Bo̱ni Farao kē̱ ne̱: Mihããnye dſe̱i doṅṅ;

ref: Go and find your own straw wherever you can, but you must still make as many bricks as before.”
text: nyedientše̱ nyeyaä nyeyataoa dſe̱i ye̱ hefẽhe ní nyeana eko ye̱ nyehãa nyehe; ši kẽle̱ atšeṅ nyenitšumo̱ le̱ ne̱ nokonoko.

ref: The slaves went all over Egypt, looking for straw.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni maṅ le̱ gbe̱ ſã Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ, ni ame̱buabua gbetšelii anā ame̱ṅõ-fe dſe̱i.

ref: But the slave bosses were hard on them and kept saying, “Each day you have to make as many bricks as you did when you were given straw.”
text: Ši kplalo̱i le̱ tšire̱ ame̱yise̱ ame̱kē̱: Nyegbea nyedāgbĩ nitšumo̱i le̱ anā tamo̱ be mli ní dſe̱i yo̱ le̱!

ref: The bosses beat the men in charge of the slaves and said, “Why didn't you force the slaves to make as many bricks yesterday and today as they did before?”
text: Ni ayi Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i ní Farao kplalo̱i le̱ ṅõ-to ame̱no̱ le̱ ake̱: Mẽhewo̱ ni nyè ke̱ ṅme̱ne̱ fẽ nyegbee nyentayāi ní atonye le̱ anā tamo̱ sá le̱?

ref: Finally, the men in charge of the slaves went to the king and said, “Why are you treating us like this?
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba ni ame̱bablo̱ ame̱wo Farao ame̱kē̱: Mẽhewo̱ oke̱ otšũdši yeo̱ ne̱ke̱?

ref: No one brings us any straw, but we are still ordered to make the same number of bricks. We are beaten with whips, and your own people are to blame.”
text: Ahãã otšũdši adſe̱i, ši ntayāi le̱, ame̱kē̱ wo̱fe dō kẽ; ni na, āyi otšũdši le̱, tše̱ tõmo̱ le̱ dše̱ omaṅ le̱.

ref: The king replied, “You are lazy—nothing but lazy! That's why you keep asking me to let you go and sacrifice to your Lord.
text: Ši ekē̱: Hedšõlo̱i dšinye, hedšõlo̱i! nohewo̱ nyekē̱ ake̱: Hã wo̱ya ní wo̱yašã afole wohã Iehowa le̱.

ref: Get back to work! You won't be given straw, but you must still make the same number of bricks.”
text: Agbe̱ne̱ nyeyaä ní nyeyatšua nii; ni dſe̱i le̱ ahããnye, ši kẽle̱ ntayāi le̱ ayikule̱ nyefea nakai noṅṅ!

ref: The men knew they were in deep trouble when they were ordered to make the same number of bricks each day.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ na ake̱ sane fõṅ eyeame̱, edšake̱ akē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekatšea nyentayāi le̱ ní dši nyedāgbĩ nitšumo̱ le̱ no̱ gbĩkogbĩko.

ref: After they left the king, they went to see Moses and Aaron, who had been waiting for them.
text: Ni beni ame̱dše̱ Farao ṅõ ame̱baä le̱, ame̱banina Mose ke̱ Aaron ní ame̱damo̱ gbe̱ le̱ no̱,

ref: Then the men said, “We hope the Lord will punish both of you for making the king and his officials hate us. Now they even have an excuse to kill us.” The Lord's Promise to Moses
text: ni ame̱kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa aakwe̱ ní ekodšo, edšake̱ nyehã wohe *edše fũ ewo Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱, ni nyeṅõ klante nyewo ame̱dẽ ní ame̱ke̱-gbewo̱. *

ref: Moses left them and prayed, “Our Lord, why have you brought so much trouble on your people? Is that why you sent me here?
text: Ši Mose kũ ese̱ ke̱-te Iehowa ṅõ ni ekē̱: Nuṅtšo̱, mẽba ofe maṅ le̱ efõṅ ne̱ke̱? Mẽba otšũmi ke̱-ba bie̱ mo̱?

ref: Ever since you told me to speak to the king, he has caused nothing but trouble for these people. And you haven't done a thing to help.”
text: Ši ke̱-dše̱ beyino ní miba Farao ṅõ ní mike̱le̱ bawie ye̱ ogbe̱i amli ne̱, efõṅ moṅ efeo̱ maṅ ne̱, ni dšiemo̱ hũ odšieko omaṅ le̱!

ref: I will bring you into the land that I solemnly promised to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and it will be yours. I am the Lord!”
text: ni mike̱nye aate šikpoṅ ni *miwó minine no̱ ake̱ mike̱-ahã Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob le̱ no̱, ni mike̱-ahãnye ní nyeye no, midši Iehowa le̱! *

ref: But the Lord sent Aaron and Moses with a message for the Israelites and for the king. He also ordered Aaron and Moses to free the people from Egypt. Family Record of Aaron and Moses
text: Ni Iehowa ke̱ Mose ke̱ Aaron wie, ni efã́ame̱ ake̱ ameya Israelbii le̱ ke̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ Farao le̱ ṅõ, ní ame̱ya-dšie Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱.

ref: The following men were the heads of their ancestral clans: The sons of Reuben, Jacob's oldest son, were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
text: Mene̱mei dši onukpai ye̱ ame̱tše̱mei awei le̱: Israel kromo̱bi Ruben le̱ bii ne̱: Hanok ke̱ Palu, Hezron ke̱ Karmi; mene̱mei dši Ruben wekui le̱.

ref: The sons of Simeon were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman.
text: Ni Simeon bii ne̱: Iemuel ke̱ Iamin ke̱ Ohad ke̱ Iakin ke̱ Zohar ke̱ Saul, Kanaanyō le̱ bi le̱; mene̱mei dši Simeon wekui le̱.

ref: Gershon's sons were Libni and Shimei.
text: Geršon bii ne̱: Libni ke̱ Šimei ye̱ ame̱wekui le̱ anā.

ref: Merari's sons were Mahli and Mushi. All of the above were from the Levi tribe.
text: Ni Merari bii ne̱: Maheli ke̱ Muši. Mene̱mei dši Levi wekui le̱ ye̱ ame̱fo̱mo̱i le̱ anā.

ref: Izhar's sons were Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
text: Ni Izehar bii ne̱: *Kora ke̱ Nefeg ke̱ Zikri. *

ref: Uzziel's sons were Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
text: Ni Uziel bii ne̱: Mišael ke̱ Elzafan ke̱ Sitri.

ref: Aaron married Elisheba. She was the daughter of Amminadab and the sister of Nahshon; they had four sons, Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text: Ni Aaron we̱ Aminadab biyō Eli-šeba ní dši Nahašon nyemiyō le̱, ni eke̱le̱ fo̱ *Nadab ke̱ Abihu, Eleazar ke̱ Itamar. *

ref: Korah's sons were Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph.
text: Ni Kora bii ne̱: Asir ke̱ Elkana ke̱ Abiasaf. Mene̱mei dši Korabii wekui le̱.

ref: The Lord had commanded Aaron and Moses to lead every family and tribe of Israel out of Egypt,
text: Mene̱mei dši Aaron ke̱ Mose ni Iehowa kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyeyadšiea Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ye̱ ame̱tai le̱ anā le̱.

ref: and so they told the king of Egypt to set the people of Israel free. The Lord Commands Moses and Aaron To Speak to the King
text: Ame̱dši mei ní ke̱ Farao, Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱, wie, koni adšie Israelbii le̱ ke̱-dše̱ Mizraim le̱; nakai Mose ke̱ Aaron le̱ ne̱.

ref: When the Lord spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,
text: Ni eba mli ye̱ gbĩ no̱ ní Iehowa ke̱ Mose wie ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ le̱,

ref: he said, “I am the Lord. Tell the king of Egypt everything I say to you.”
text: ake̱ Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Midši Iehowa le̱; kē̱mo̱ Farao, Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱, nii fẽ ní mike̱o wieo̱ le̱.

ref: But Moses answered, “You know I am a very poor speaker, and the king will never listen to me.”
text: Ni Mose kē̱ ye̱ Iehowa hĩe ake̱: Na, minā tšee; té aafe tēṅ ní Farao aabomi toiṅ? K.

ref: Moses and Aaron went to the king and his officials and did exactly as the Lord had commanded—Aaron threw the stick down, and it turned into a snake.
text: Ni Mose ke̱ Aaron te Farao ṅõ, ni amefe bo̱ni Iehowa efã́ame̱ le̱; ni Aaron ṅõ etšo le̱ efõ ši ye̱ Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱ ahĩe, ni etšṍ šikpã.

ref: they threw down sticks that turned into snakes. But Aaron's snake swallowed theirs.
text: Ni ame̱teṅ mofẽmo ṅõ etšo efõ ši, ní ame̱tšã́mo̱ šikpãi; ši Aaron tšo le̱ mī̃ ame̱tšei le̱.

ref: The king behaved just as the Lord had said and stubbornly refused to listen. The Nile River Turns into Blood
text: Ni awadše Farao tšui, ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ bo̱ni Iehowa *ewie le̱. *

ref: The Lord said to Moses: The Egyptian king stubbornly refuses to change his mind and let the people go.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Farao tšui wa, ekplẽẽ ní maṅ le̱ aya.

ref: Tomorrow morning take the stick that turned into a snake, then wait beside the Nile River for the king.
text: Ya Farao ṅõ lebi; na, ēdše kpo eya fā le̱ nā, ni oyadamo̱ fā le̱ nā omẽle̱, ni tšo le̱ ní tšṍ onũfũ le̱ ko̱ ohĩe odẽ,

ref: Tell him, “The Lord God of the Hebrews sent me to order you to release his people, so they can worship him in the desert. But until now, you have paid no attention.
text: ni okē̱le̱ ake̱: Iehowa, Hebribii le̱ a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, tšũmi oṅõ ake̱ mibakē̱o ake̱: *Hã ní mimaṅ le̱ aya ní ame̱ya-súmo̱mi ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱; ni na, okplẽko ke̱-baši bene̱. *

ref: “The Lord is going to do something to show you that he really is the Lord. I will strike the Nile with this stick, and the water will turn into blood.
text: Boni Iehowa kē̱ ne̱: Ene̱ oke̱-ale ake̱ midši Iehowa le̱: na, mike̱ tšo ní mihĩe ye̱ midẽ ne̱ batſa fā le̱ mli nu le̱, ni *eetšṍ lá. *

ref: The fish will die, the river will stink, and none of you Egyptians will be able to drink the water.”
text: Ni lōi ni yo̱ fā le̱ mli le̱ aagboi, ni fá le̱ aaša, ni Mizraimbii le̱ aanyãṅe fā le̱ mli nu le̱ numo̱.

ref: Moses, then command Aaron to hold his stick over the water. And when he does, every drop of water in Egypt will turn into blood, including rivers, canals, ponds, and even the water in buckets and jars.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Kē̱mo̱ Aaron ake̱: Ko̱ otšo le̱, ni okpã onine mli ye̱ Mizraim *nui le̱ ke̱ ame̱nudšō̱i le̱ ke̱ ame̱fāi le̱ ke̱ ame̱kpākpoi le̱ ke̱ ame̱nui ní bua ši le̱ fẽ no̱ ní ame̱tšṍmo̱ lá; ní nu ní yo̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ, ye̱ tšonibii ke̱ te̱nibii amli le̱, aatšo lá. *Kpods.

ref: Moses and Aaron obeyed the Lord. Aaron held out his stick, then struck the Nile, as the king and his officials watched. The river turned into blood,
text: Ni Mose ke̱ Aaron fe bo̱ni Iehowa fã́ame̱ le̱, ni ewó etšo le̱ no̱, ni eke̱-tſa nu ní yo̱ fā le̱ mli le̱ ye̱ Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱ ahĩe, ni fā le̱ mli nu le̱ fè tšṍ lá.

ref: the fish died, and the water smelled so bad that none of the Egyptians could drink it. Blood was everywhere in Egypt.
text: Ni lōi ní yo̱ fā le̱ mli le̱ gboi, ni fā le̱ ša, ni Mizraimbii le̱ nyẽẽ fā le̱ mli nu le̱ anu; ni lá so̱ṅṅ yo̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ.

ref: But the Egyptian magicians used their secret powers to do the same thing. The king did just as the Lord had said—he stubbornly refused to listen.
text: Ni *Mizraim ṅkũnyāyelo̱i le̱ ke̱ ame̱ṅkũnyāyeli-ṅāi le̱ fe nakai noṅṅ. Ni awadše Farao tšui ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ boni Iehowa ewie le̱. *K.
